source.subject: Machine-readable export of subprocessor metadata and service mapping request
messages:
From: Elaine Marsh <elaine.marsh@deltalabs.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@deltalabs.com
Date: Wed, 2 Oct 2029 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Request: machine-readable subprocessor export + service mapping

Hi Ben,

Hope you’re well. Procurement and our security team are finalizing the data flow review for the pilot contract and we need a machine-readable dump of your current subprocessor inventory and a simple mapping to the services they support. Specifically our checklist asks for: 

- A CSV (or JSON) with one row per subprocessor including: name, country of processing, contract start date, purpose (telemetry, storage, auth, metrics, caching, analytics), whether they handle plaintext or encrypted tokens, KMS/HSM usage (yes/no), and whether there is any cross-border egress.
- A short free-text field describing where customer data flows through that subprocessor (e.g. "logs -> telemetry pipeline -> third-party analytics"), and whether you have any onshore/fallback routes configured.
- Links to attestation artifacts (SOC 2 report location, DPA subprocessor addendum, encryption attestations).

We attached our procurement template (delta_subprocessor_template_v1.csv) so your team can drop in the data. Can you provide the export and a copy of any relevant DPA addenda by EOD Oct 4? Our legal team will check the mapping and may come back with a few redlines.

Also a quick clarification: do you include ephemeral cache partners (CDN variants used only for <30 days) in your primary roster, or are those surfaced separately?

Thanks — we’re trying to keep the review window tight for the pilot.

Best,
Elaine
Elaine Marsh 
Senior Procurement Manager | Delta Labs
elaine.marsh@deltalabs.com

Attachment: delta_subprocessor_template_v1.csv
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Elaine Marsh <elaine.marsh@deltalabs.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 2 Oct 2029 12:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: machine-readable subprocessor export + service mapping

Elaine — thanks for the clear template and timeline. We can provide a CSV that matches your columns and include an explicit column for "ephemeral" partners (yes/no) so you can filter them.

Plan:
- I’ll coordinate with Legal and Privacy to produce a sanitized machine-readable export.
- Kimberly, Jordan — adding you to the thread for legal/privilege and privacy review.
- Target delivery: Oct 4 EOD PDT (we’ll push earlier if possible).

Question for Delta: do you need contract references (Doc IDs or start dates) at the vendor-line level or is an aggregated "DPA Addendum present: yes/no" sufficient for the first pass?

Ben
Ben Carter
Sr. Partnerships Manager | Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0132

On Wed, Oct 2, 2029 at 09:15 Elaine Marsh <elaine.marsh@deltalabs.com> wrote: 
> (original message)
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Elaine Marsh <elaine.marsh@deltalabs.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 3 Oct 2029 09:32:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: machine-readable subprocessor export + service mapping

Hi all — copying in a short legal/privacy note so Delta has the right fields.

Ben: we will produce two artifacts
1) redwood_subprocessor_inventory_v1.xlsx — a human-readable inventory that includes contract reference, clause references (DPA section), and notes about processor/subprocessor roles.
2) redwood_subprocessor_export_20291003.csv — the machine-readable CSV aligned to Delta's template (we will include a column "dpa_addendum_link" with the DocuSign URL if an addendum exists).

For procurement: please expect the following columns in the CSV (in addition to Delta's template):
- contract_reference_id
- dpa_addendum_present (yes/no)
- data_categories_processed (PII, logs, telemetry)
- encryption_in_transit_at_rest (yes/no)
- ephemeral_partner_flag (yes/no)

Note on redactions: we will not expose internal vendor pricing or full contractual terms in the CSV. If Delta requires copies of specific DPA addenda we can provide signed addenda PDFs via secure link.

We will attach both files to the next message. Jordan — please advise if we should attach SOC 2 summary or provide a link to the attestation portal only.

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Security | Redwood Inference
kimberly_park@redwood.com

On Wed, Oct 2, 2029 at 12:02 PM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com> wrote: 
> (Ben's ack)
From: Elaine Marsh <elaine.marsh@deltalabs.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@deltalabs.com, dpo@deltalabs.com
Date: Fri, 4 Oct 2029 16:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: machine-readable subprocessor export + service mapping

Thanks — that sounds good. Delta confirms we prefer the full contract_reference_id on the CSV (so long as it’s a reference ID and not the full contract PDF). We will accept links to signed addenda for any subcontractor that requires separate approval.

Two quick asks before you send: 
1) Please make sure the "ephemeral_partner_flag" is present and set to "yes" only if the vendor has been used for <30 days in production; we will treat flagged rows differently in our procurement scoring.
2) If possible, include a separate column for "data_residency_override" to call out any onshore fallback that would apply for EU/US centroid customers.

If you can drop the CSV into the secure folder linked below we’ll pull it into our compliance review flow and try to finish by Oct 11.

Secure upload: https://deltalabs.share/secure/redwood-dpa-upload

Appreciate the quick turnaround — will loop back if we need any clarifications.

Best,
Elaine

Forwarded message:
> From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
> Date: Thu, Oct 3, 2029 at 09:32 AM
> Subject: Re: Request: machine-readable subprocessor export + service mapping
> To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Elaine Marsh <elaine.marsh@deltalabs.com>
>
> (Inventory and export to be attached)

Attachments mentioned: redwood_subprocessor_inventory_v1.xlsx, redwood_subprocessor_export_20291003.csv

source.subject: RFI: telemetry and deployment boundary — artifact & control bundle request
messages:
From: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@sentinelsys.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alex Torres <alex.torres@finovate.co>
Date: Sat, 15 Jun 2029 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: RFI: telemetry & deployment boundary — artifact list request

Hi David / Jordan,

Thanks again for the walkthrough this morning. As part of Sentinel Systems' procurement packet we need a scoped RFI response focused on telemetry, deployment boundaries (hosted vs dedicated), and cryptographic key stewardship. The security team asked specifically for: 

- A completed SIG questionnaire (we attached the spreadsheet for context).
- Samples showing telemetry retention and a redacted log bundle for review (token/latency traces acceptable, PII redacted).
- KMS/key rotation proof (screenshots or audit log entries).
- A mapping table showing how SIG/SOC2/ISO controls map to Redwood components (serving runtime, gateway, control plane).

Can you confirm what artifacts you can share and whether you need an NDA or additional access? Our target is to have materials submitted to procurement by 2029-06-21.

Thanks,
Priya Sharma
Security Review Lead
Sentinel Systems

Attachments: SIG_questionnaire_20290615.xlsx (spreadsheet)
HubSpot: https://hubspot.redwood.ai/ticket/HS-78921
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Sat, 15 Jun 2029 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: RFI: telemetry & deployment boundary — artifact list request

Forwarding Sentinel's request — see below. Priya asked for a SIG questionnaire, telemetry samples, KMS rotation evidence, and a controls map that ties SIG/SOC2/ISO to our runtime/components. Target ship date is 2029-06-21 to their procurement team.

Action items I want to land quickly: 
- Jordan: can you own the control mapping sheet and SOC2/ISO excerpts?
- Mina: can SRE prepare a redacted telemetry bundle and explain retention & retention policy?
- I will confirm whether we need an NDA and assemble the HubSpot/Docs links for delivery.

Please reply here with what you can attach and realistic delivery dates. If any item requires legal / sales signoff (pricing or SLA exceptions), flag it.

Thanks — David
David Kim
Security & Compliance Manager
Redwood Inference
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Sat, 15 Jun 2029 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: RFI: telemetry & deployment boundary — artifact list request

David / Mina,

We can cover most of this with a small bundle. Proposed artifact set (I can zip and share a Drive folder):

- SOC2 excerpt (scope statement + relevant control narrative) — soc2-excerpt-2028.pdf (redacted).
- ISO 27001 scope statement and Annex A crosswalk.
- SIG mapping spreadsheet (I will populate controls -> component mapping) — file name: redwood-sig-crosswalk-20290615.xlsx.
- KMS rotation screenshots + Cloud KMS audit entries — kms-rotation-screenshots-20290615.pdf.
- High-level architecture diagram that marks "hosted" vs "dedicated" boundaries (png).

Questions:
- Do they need raw token-level logs or redacted csv/ndjson samples? Providing raw logs is sensitive; we prefer a redacted bundle plus an example query that reproduces an audit.
- Which environment are they evaluating (hosted API or dedicated deployment)? Some artifacts differ (e.g., customer-managed KMS proofs for Private/Dedicated).

Timeline: I can get the SOC2 excerpt, mapping spreadsheet, and KMS screenshots prepared and uploaded to Drive by 2029-06-18 EOD. I will coordinate with Mina for the telemetry sample.

Attachments (pending): redwood-sig-crosswalk-20290615.xlsx (draft), soc2-excerpt-2028.pdf (draft), kms-rotation-screenshots-20290615.pdf

— Jordan
Jordan Blake
Senior Security Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Sun, 16 Jun 2029 08:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: RFI: telemetry & deployment boundary — artifact list request

David / Jordan,

SRE can produce the telemetry deliverables with the following constraints and planned artifacts: 

Deliverables:
- Redacted log bundle (ndjson) containing service-level traces with latency/tokens fields; PII removed. File: telemetry-samples-20290616.zip (contains sample-query.txt and 3 rotated log files).
- Retention statement & retention-policy excerpt (S3 lifecycle + log-aggregate retention spreadsheet).
- Example PromQL/Elasticsearch query used to pull the latency traces and a short runbook for reproducing the sample.

Constraints & notes:
- Raw audit logs that include user-provided prompt content are restricted; we will provide redacted snapshots and an explicit redaction statement.
- For Dedicated/Private: if the customer requires proof of customer-managed KMS operations, we can provide the audit entries documented in kms-rotation-screenshots-20290615.pdf — but for hosted API the rotation is managed by Redwood and we provide rotation artifacts and attestation.

ETA: We will deliver the redacted telemetry bundle and runbook by 2029-06-20 16:00 PT. I will include checksums for the files and a small README indicating redaction rules.

Attachments: telemetry-samples-20290616.zip (redacted sample bundle)

— Mina
Mina Haddad
SRE Manager, Observability
Redwood Inference
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>
To: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@sentinelsys.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Alex Torres <alex.torres@finovate.co>
Date: Sun, 16 Jun 2029 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: RFI: telemetry & deployment boundary — artifact & control bundle request

Hi Priya / Alex,

Thanks for the clear ask. We will assemble the requested artifacts and post a Drive folder to the HubSpot ticket (HS-78921) once ready. Planned deliverables and owners: 

- redwood-sig-crosswalk-20290615.xlsx (Jordan) — maps SIG/SOC2/ISO controls to runtime/gateway/control-plane.
- soc2-excerpt-2028.pdf (Jordan) — redacted scope & control narrative.
- kms-rotation-screenshots-20290615.pdf (Jordan) — rotation proof / audit entries.
- telemetry-samples-20290616.zip (Mina) — redacted samples, query examples, and runbook.

We do not require an NDA for these redacted artifacts, but if Sentinel wants full unredacted logs or customer-managed KMS console access for Dedicated deployments we will need a standard NDA + documented access review (we can start that in parallel).

Planned delivery: 2029-06-20 EOD (we will try to ship earlier). If you need a tighter date, let us know and we can prioritize the SOC2/controls mapping earlier.

Drive folder (will contain artifacts): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aB2cD-telemetry-packets
HubSpot: https://hubspot.redwood.ai/ticket/HS-78921

Thanks,
David Kim
Security & Compliance Manager
Redwood Inference

We can cover most of this with a small bundle. Proposed artifact set (I can zip and share a Drive folder):

- SOC2 excerpt (scope statement + relevant control narrative) — soc2-excerpt-2028.pdf (redacted).
- ISO 27001 scope statement and Annex A crosswalk.
- SIG mapping spreadsheet (I will populate controls -> component mapping) — file name: redwood-sig-crosswalk-20290615.xlsx.

Timeline: I can get the SOC2 excerpt, mapping spreadsheet, and KMS screenshots prepared and uploaded to Drive by 2029-06-18 EOD. I will coordinate with Mina for the telemetry sample.

source.subject: BrightArc vendor onboarding — W-9 OTP failure and alternative proof
messages:
From: Sam Torres <s.torres@brightarc.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: BrightArc Accounting <accounting@brightarc.com>
Date: Wed, 1 May 2029 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: New vendor - W-9 upload for PO prep

Hi Ben,

We're ready to complete Redwood's vendor setup so procurement can issue the PO. I attached the signed W-9 (W-9_BrightArc_signed.pdf) but our finance team reports the DocuSign OTP flow failed when we attempted electronic authentication. The error on our side shows the OTP timed out before we could confirm.

Can you confirm what alternate documents you will accept so we can get the PO in your system? If needed we can provide a bank confirmation letter or an IRS CP 575 notice. Our EIN is 12-3456789 (company name: BrightArc Inc.; DBA: BrightArc Labs).

Looking to get this moving this week — we have an upcoming SOW kickoff tied to the PO.

Thanks,
Sam Torres
Director of Finance
BrightArc Inc.
Sam.Torres@brightarc.com

Attachments: W-9_BrightArc_signed.pdf
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sam Torres <s.torres@brightarc.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 1 May 2029 12:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: New vendor - W-9 upload for PO prep

Hi Sam — thanks for sending this over.

Two quick asks so we can progress:
1) Can you confirm the EIN exactly as printed on the W-9 and whether BrightArc Inc. is the legal name on the form (vs the DBA)? The tax team flagged a mismatch in an earlier upload where the DBA showed on the signature page but the EIN on the form matched the legal entity.
2) If the DocuSign OTP flow is failing on your side, we can accept one of the following as alternate evidence (please provide one):
   - A bank letter on bank letterhead confirming account ownership and EIN (scanned PDF, signed)
   - An IRS CP 575 or IRS EIN issuance letter (PDF)
   - A notarized affidavit confirming the EIN and signatory authority (signed + notary stamp)

I copied Kimberly (tax) to advise on the minimum acceptable fields. Monica from procurement will track the PO side. If we can get one of the alternate docs and the signed W-9, we can provisionally create a PO with a hold so the project kickoff isn't delayed.

Ben Carter
Senior Account Manager, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
From: Sam Torres <s.torres@brightarc.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: BrightArc Accounting <accounting@brightarc.com>
Date: Thu, 2 May 2029 08:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: New vendor - W-9 upload for PO prep

Ben — appreciate the quick reply.

Attaching a scanned bank confirmation letter (bank_letter_brightarc.pdf). Our treasury team pulled it together this morning. Note: the signed W-9 we uploaded uses the legal entity "BrightArc Inc." but the signature block printed the DBA (BrightArc Labs) beneath the signer's name. That was likely why your tax checker flagged the mismatch. The EIN on both the W-9 and the bank letter is shown as 12-3456789.

DocuSign flow: we attempted OTP via SMS to the signatory and the code expired. We tried again and it failed once more; we can coordinate a time window if you prefer a live OTP attempt.

If the bank letter is acceptable, please let me know and we'll move forward. If you need notarized affidavits instead, we can get that done but it will add 2-3 business days.

Thanks — Sam

--
Sam Torres
Director of Finance, BrightArc Inc.
Attachments: bank_letter_brightarc.pdf
From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 2 May 2029 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: FW: New vendor - W-9 upload for PO prep

Looping Jordan (procurement) and Kimberly (tax verification).

Jordan — please review the attached bank letter and the W-9 Sam provided. Tax flagged a name presentation mismatch (legal name vs DBA). If Kimberly confirms the bank letter plus the W-9 with matching EIN is sufficient, can procurement create a provisional PO and place it in "Pending - compliance" status? We would like to provision PO-72819 for $50,000 to avoid blocking the kickoff, but mark it as conditional until we receive either the notarized affidavit or an IRS issuance letter.

I attached the internal checklist we use for conditional releases (procurement_checklist.xlsx) — follow the steps on tab 2, and note we require the source of truth for the EIN (bank letter or IRS doc) before release.

Kimberly — can you confirm acceptance criteria? If the bank letter includes bank contact info, will tax accept it plus the W-9? If more is required, please list the minimum fields (bank name, account holder name, EIN, dated within 90 days, bank signatory + contact).

Thanks,
Monica Patel
Procurement Operations Manager
monica.patel@redwood.com
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sam Torres <s.torres@brightarc.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 3 May 2029 17:23:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: New vendor - W-9 upload for PO prep

Thanks everyone — quick update for next steps and timeline: 

1) Procurement has created a provisional PO: PO-72819 (link: https://app.redwood.example/po/PO-72819). Status: "Pending - compliance". Amount: $50,000.
2) Tax (Kimberly) confirmed that the attached bank letter plus the W-9 with matching EIN is sufficient for conditional release. Kimberly: please confirm in thread if you require anything else before we move the PO to "Awaiting Release".
3) Sam: if you can provide either a notarized affidavit or an IRS CP 575 within 3 business days, we will remove the conditional hold and complete PO issuance. Alternatively, if you want to retry the DocuSign OTP, let us know a 30-minute window and we'll coordinate a live session.

Sam — once we have one of the items above, procurement will finalize and move PO-72819 to issued. We appreciate the quick turnaround on this so the SOW kickoff isn't delayed.

Ben Carter
Senior Account Manager, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
Phone: (415) 555-0172

On Thu, May 2, 2029 at 11:05 AM Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com> wrote:
> Looping Jordan (procurement) and Kimberly (tax verification).
> 
> Jordan — please review the attached bank letter and the W-9 Sam provided.
> 
> -- Monica

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 2 May 2029 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: FW: New vendor - W-9 upload for PO prep

Looping Jordan (procurement) and Kimberly (tax verification).

Jordan — please review the attached bank letter and the W-9 Sam provided. Tax flagged a name presentation mismatch (legal name vs DBA). If Kimberly confirms the bank letter plus the W-9 with matching EIN is sufficient, can procurement create a provisional PO and place it in "Pending - compliance" status? We would like to provision PO-72819 for $50,000 to avoid blocking the kickoff, but mark it as conditional until we receive either the notarized affidavit or an IRS issuance letter.

I attached the internal checklist we use for conditional releases (procurement_checklist.xlsx) — follow the steps on tab 2, and note we require the source of truth for the EIN (bank letter or IRS doc) before release.

Kimberly — can you confirm acceptance criteria? If the bank letter includes bank contact info, will tax accept it plus the W-9? If more is required, please list the minimum fields (bank name, account holder name, EIN, dated within 90 days, bank signatory + contact).

Thanks,
Monica Patel
Procurement Operations Manager
monica.patel@redwood.com


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sam Torres <s.torres@brightarc.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 3 May 2029 17:23:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: New vendor - W-9 upload for PO prep

Thanks everyone — quick update for next steps and timeline: 

1) Procurement has created a provisional PO: PO-72819 (link: https://app.redwood.example/po/PO-72819). Status: "Pending - compliance". Amount: $50,000.
2) Tax (Kimberly) confirmed that the attached bank letter plus the W-9 with matching EIN is sufficient for conditional release. Kimberly: please confirm in thread if you require anything else before we move the PO to "Awaiting Release".
3) Sam: if you can provide either a notarized affidavit or an IRS CP 575 within 3 business days, we will remove the conditional hold and complete PO issuance. Alternatively, if you want to retry the DocuSign OTP, let us know a 30-minute window and we'll coordinate a live session.

Sam — once we have one of the items above, procurement will finalize and move PO-72819 to issued. We appreciate the quick turnaround on this so the SOW kickoff isn't delayed.

Ben Carter
Senior Account Manager, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
Phone: (415) 555-0172

source.subject: Pilot timing change — reprioritizing inference work
messages:
From: Sanjay Patel <sanjay@idexa.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Alyssa Torres <alyssa@idexa.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Nov 2028 10:12:00 -0800
Subject: Need to move our inference pilot window

Hey Ben,

Appreciate the walkthrough last week. We just locked down a product launch for early December and the engineering org has been pulled into launch support (and several folks are traveling). Given that, we need to deprioritize the inference pilot for the remainder of the quarter.

Can we pause the demo and circle back in mid-January? I don’t want to lose the thread — we like what Redwood showed — but the timing is just impossible right now.

If it helps, I attached a one-pager about our launch schedule.

Thanks,
Sanjay
Director of Platform Engineering
Idexa Health

Attachments: Idexa-Launch-Plan.pdf (attachment stub)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Patel <sanjay@idexa.com>
Cc: Alyssa Torres <alyssa@idexa.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Nov 2028 12:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Need to move our inference pilot window

Hi Sanjay — thank you for the update and congrats on the launch. Totally understandable; launch weeks are always a treadmill.

Two quick options so we don’t lose momentum:
- Option A: Pause the active demo and I’ll set a soft reminder for the week of Jan 15 to rebook a 30–45 min refresh demo with your team.
- Option B: Keep a short 20-minute async sync in early Dec for stakeholders who are local (no deep engineering time) — I can do a focused exec summary and next steps doc.

I’ve attached the Redwood Quickstart guide and a suggested re-engagement plan. If you prefer A, say the word and I’ll update the HubSpot plan and close out the current demo calendar invite.

Ben
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachments: redwood-quickstart-guide.pdf, reengagement-plan.md (attachment stubs)

From: Alyssa Torres <alyssa@idexa.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Sanjay Patel <sanjay@idexa.com>
Date: Wed, 29 Nov 2028 09:34:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Need to move our inference pilot window

Thanks, Ben. After chatting with Sanjay and PM, we should go with Option A. The engineers are fully committed to the launch and we don’t have reliable backups.

Please schedule the rebook for mid-January — Jan 16–20 would be ideal for our team. If you can lock a few tentative slots I’ll confirm after the launch countdown is over.

Alyssa
Product Ops Manager
Idexa Health

P.S. We don’t need the async sync — would prefer a clean break and a single re-engagement once things stabilize.

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 30 Nov 2028 14:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: Need to move our inference pilot window

FYI forwarding Idexa’s ask. Key points: prospect confirmed deprioritization due to product launch and travel; they want a rebook window Jan 16–20.

Action items for us:
- Marissa: please mark deal DEAL-48921 as "postponed - prospect priority" in HubSpot and set a follow-up task for Jan 10 to reach back out. Include the launch plan PDF in the record.
- Jordan: hold tentative demo slots Jan 16/17/18 10:00–11:00 PT on my calendar but mark them as tentative and don’t send invites to the prospect yet. We’ll confirm after Alyssa gives the all-clear.
- Ben (me): I’ll update the re-engagement plan in our shared drive and strip the deep technical workshop from the original scope — low-burn until they re-engage.

Quoted message below for context.

-----Original Message-----
From: Sanjay Patel <sanjay@idexa.com>
Sent: Tue, Nov 28, 2028 at 10:12 AM
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Alyssa Torres <alyssa@idexa.com>
Subject: Need to move our inference pilot window

(See original email above)

Attachments: Idexa-Launch-Plan.pdf

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 Dec 2028 15:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Need to move our inference pilot window

Thanks, Ben — handled.

I updated HubSpot: DEAL-48921 status set to "On Hold - Customer reprioritized" and added a note with the Jan 16–20 tentative window and the launch plan attachment. Follow-up task created for Jan 10 assigned to Ben.

Jordan — I booked the three tentative slots on the shared AE calendar as requested (they show as busy/tentative). I also attached a short one-pager to the HubSpot timeline with suggested next steps for the Jan re-engagement (low lift: exec recap, success criteria review, one engineering spike to validate sample prompts).

If anything changes on their side pre-launch, I’ll flag it immediately.

—Marissa
Senior Sales Ops Manager | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com

source.subject: Request: Additional trial credits for Medlytics RFP bake-off
messages:
From: Emma Liu <emma@medlytics.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 31 Oct 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: RFP bake-off — request for extra eval capacity

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the team is excited to include Redwood in our RFP bake-off. A couple of asks from our evaluation committee before we finalize the shortlist:

- Can Medlytics get an extra 500k token credit pool for a two-week spike? We need to run parallel end-to-end scenarios against two competitor stacks.
- Will Redwood allow a one-week trial extension after the initial 30-day sandbox to finish the bench runs?
- Any chance to surface per-route latency numbers for long-context pipelines (8k+)?

If procurement needs a formal SOW or statement on data handling for the trial, loop us in and we can expedite.

Best,
Emma
Emma Liu
Head of ML Engineering, Medlytics

---
Attachment: evaluation-outline.pdf (pdf)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Emma Liu <emma@medlytics.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 01 Nov 2028 08:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: RFP bake-off — request for extra eval capacity

Hi Emma — great to hear the team is moving forward. Short answers up front:

- We can provide a temporary top-up of credits for the bake-off. I need final approval from sales ops for the size and review with product for the latency extract you asked about.
- We can extend the trial window by 7 days after the sandbox period.

I'll pull together a concise approval note and an operations playbook for the Medlytics bench so your team can run the long-context tests without friction.

Can you confirm the target start date for the spike and whether the runs will be in us-west region? That helps sizing.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: request-summary.txt (txt)

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 01 Nov 2028 10:13:00 -0700
Subject: RE: RFP bake-off — request for extra eval capacity

Folks — looping in for approvers and ops. Notes:

- Medlytics is a mid-sized healthtech prospect; RFP expects a head-to-head with two incumbents. High chance of expansion if Redwood wins the accuracy/latency PR.
- Request: 500k tokens for 2 weeks + 7-day extension.
- Risk: health data inference — they indicate synthetic/test data but procurement may push for SOC-2 evidence. We have SOC-2 + optional ephemeral logs; should be fine but legal might flag an SOW.

Proposal: Approve a staged top-up — 250k initial release, additional 250k after a short checkpoint and review of usage patterns. Include RD-run latency dump for 8k sequences from our staging infra.

If you want me to draft the formal approval memo, I can. Jordan — can you confirm we have the 8k long-context latency report available by EOD tomorrow?

-Neha
Neha Kapoor
Director, Sales Operations | Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: medlytics-risk-notes.md (txt)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 02 Nov 2028 09:01:00 -0700
Subject: RE: RFP bake-off — request for extra eval capacity

Neha — I pulled the staging run logs. Summary for the approval memo:

- 8k-context synthetic run (gpt-like encoder-decoder stack) median token latency: 42ms/token on p4d with our batching profile; p99 ~ 120ms when concurrency > 12.
- We can export a trimmed CSV with per-route breakdown and kv-cache hit rates.
- Ops notes: we should enable prefix caching and set max-batch to 16 for the spike to control cost.

Aditya — I approve the staged top-up plan. Release 250k now; hold the remaining 250k pending the checkpoint which will be a short usage review after 7 days. I also attached the latency CSV for customer transparency.

-Jordan
Jordan Blake
Head of Sales Engineering | Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: long-context-latency-8k.csv (csv)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Emma Liu <emma@medlytics.com>, Procurement <procure@medlytics.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 02 Nov 2028 15:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: RFP bake-off — request for extra eval capacity

Hi Emma / Procurement team —

Thanks for the patience. Per our internal approvals below, Redwood will provide the following for Medlytics' bake-off:

- Initial credit top-up: 250,000 tokens released immediately to your sandbox.
- Secondary top-up: 250,000 tokens available after a short checkpoint (usage review) within 7 days.
- Trial extension: 7 extra days after the standard 30-day sandbox to run the remaining bench tests.
- Attachments: we shared a latency export for 8k-context runs (p99 and median) and ops recommendations for batching/prefix caching.

Next steps for procurement: if you need a brief SOW or signature to document the extension and credit terms, I can circulate a one-page addendum today. Otherwise, confirm the target start for the spike and we will provision the initial credits.

Looking forward to seeing the bench results.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Phone: (415) 555-0193

---
Attachments: MAL-Statement-of-Work.pdf, benchmark-usage-report.csv

Next steps for procurement: if you need a brief SOW or signature to document the extension and credit terms, I can circulate a one-page addendum today. Otherwise, confirm the target start for the spike and we will provision the initial credits.

Looking forward to seeing the bench results.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Phone: (415) 555-0193

source.subject: FjordCommerce: funnel lockdown — sustained long-tail latency & 429s
messages:
From: Priya Shah <ops@fjordcommerce.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Amit Rao <amit@fjordcommerce.com>
Date: 2028-07-26T08:14:22-07:00
Subject: Unexpected 429s + huge tail on checkout pipeline

Jordan —

We started seeing a surge of 429s and multi-second tail completions on our checkout personalization calls around 07:40 UTC. Traffic pattern looks normal on our edge but Redwood API replies are taking 5–12s on the 95–99th percentile and 2% of requests are 429-ing. This is impacting user checkout completion.

Attached: a short request sample and a handful of traces (we redacted PII). Can your team confirm whether this is a routing/concurrency cap or an internal queueing/backpressure event on your side?

We're getting pressure from ops and product — need a quick status and a mitigation path.

Thanks,
Priya
Priya Shah
Head of Platform Reliability, FjordCommerce
[attachment: diagnostics/fjord_request_sample.log (45KB)]

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Priya Shah <ops@fjordcommerce.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-26T08:36:05-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected 429s + huge tail on checkout pipeline

Priya — thanks for the quick heads-up and the logs. We are pulling traces now. Preliminary notes: we see a noticeable in-flight accumulation on the model pool serving the "fc-personalize-v1" variant starting at 07:38 UTC. Token-level latency isn't elevated for short requests, but long requests (likely longer context / retries) are accumulating and pushing queueing into the p99 range.

Action items I am kicking off:
- Looping in David (runtime) for kernel/buffer saturation checks.
- Marissa to coordinate an immediate customer-safe mitigation (routing to dedicated nodes / transient concurrency bump).
- We will provide a putative mitigation within 30 mins and a root-cause readout within 3 hours.

Quick ask: Priya, can you confirm your request size distribution for the affected endpoints (avg tokens / % of >1k token requests)? That helps rule out customer-side prompt storms.

—Jordan
Jordan Blake
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
[attachment: redwood_trace_20280726.json (120KB)]

From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-26T09:01:42-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected 429s + huge tail on checkout pipeline

Jordan / Marissa — quick runtime debug notes based on the traces:
- Observed sustained queue depth increase on pool: hostset=dedicated-eu-1-prod? No — affected pool is "shared-gpu-a" in us-west.
- Concurrency caps hit on 4 nodes (cap at 64 inflight each); controllers are rejecting extra in-flight with soft 429s upstream.
- KV cache snapshot shows a growing number of stale prefix entries for fc-personalize-v1; cache churn appears limited but KV lookup latency spiked (~10ms -> ~120ms) during the window.
- OOMs are not present; no GPU kernel timeouts in the sampled traces.

Short-term mitigations I recommend now:
1) Bump concurrency cap for fc-personalize-v1 on shared-gpu-a to 96 (temp) and enable extra burst slots.
2) Route FjordCommerce keyspace to a warm dedicated slice if they can accept temporary dedicated routing (will reduce cross-tenant interference).
3) Purge stale KV prefixes for the variant and re-warm cache (we can push a cache rehydrate job).

I'll prepare the diagnostic tar (kv_snapshot_0726.tar.gz) and a concise ops playbook. If Jordan wants me on a call with Fjord ops in 15, I'm ready.

—David
David Kim
Senior Backend Engineer, Serving Runtime
[attachment: kv_snapshot_0726.tar.gz (2.1MB)]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Priya Shah <ops@fjordcommerce.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-26T09:13:59-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected 429s + huge tail on checkout pipeline

Priya — joining. We can stand up a temporary dedicated slice for FjordCommerce in ~20 minutes if you approve (it will require a KV prefix migration and a short routing flip). That buys stable latency while we continue to debug. If you prefer not to move traffic, we can apply David's concurrency bump immediately which should relieve the 429s at the cost of higher transient token spend.

Proposed mitigation plan (sequence):
- T+0m: Apply 96 concurrency cap to fc-personalize-v1 on shared-gpu-a (David to execute).
- T+5m: Monitor function p95/p99 for 10m window; revert if error rate increases.
- T+20m: If still elevated, execute dedicated routing to warm slice (route keys -> dedicated-fjord-1).
- T+60m: Full post-mortem with timeline and mitigation cost estimate.

Priya, do you prefer immediate concurrency bump or go straight to dedicated slice? Let me know so we can act.

—Marissa
Director, Customer Success
Redwood Inference

From: Priya Shah <ops@fjordcommerce.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-26T09:22:11-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected 429s + huge tail on checkout pipeline

Thanks all — please apply the concurrency bump now. We want the fastest path that reduces checkout failure. We'll accept a short-term cost increase. I'll be on the monitoring channel and can provide request-size distribution in 10 minutes.

On another note: our head of engineering is copying Amit into this thread; if this recurs we may need an exec-level readout on capacity policies and whether fallback to dedicated is covered by our contract.

—Priya
Priya Shah
Head of Platform Reliability, FjordCommerce

On Mon, Jul 26, 2028 at 09:01 AM David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com> wrote:
> Jordan / Marissa — quick runtime debug notes based on the traces:
> - Observed sustained queue depth increase on pool: hostset=dedicated-eu-1-prod? No — affected pool is \"shared-gpu-a\" in us-west.
> - Concurrency caps hit on 4 nodes (cap at 64 inflight each); controllers are rejecting extra in-flight with soft 429s upstream.
> - KV cache snapshot shows a growing number of stale prefix entries for fc-personalize-v1; cache churn appears limited but KV lookup latency spiked (~10ms -> ~120ms) during the window.
> - OOMs are not present; no GPU kernel timeouts in the sampled traces.
>
> Short-term mitigations I recommend now:
> 1) Bump concurrency cap for fc-personalize-v1 on shared-gpu-a to 96 (temp) and enable extra burst slots.
> 2) Route FjordCommerce keyspace to a warm dedicated slice if they can accept temporary dedicated routing (will reduce cross-tenant interference).
> 3) Purge stale KV prefixes for the variant and re-warm cache (we can push a cache rehydrate job).
>
> I'll prepare the diagnostic tar (kv_snapshot_0726.tar.gz) and a concise ops playbook. If Jordan wants me on a call with Fjord ops in 15, I'm ready.
>
> —David

source.subject: Clarify: what gets deleted vs retained (requests vs generated content)
messages:
From: Anita Rao <anita.rao@brightbank.com>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.ai>
Cc: security@brightbank.com
Date: Tue, 18 Jul 2028 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: RFI — default log retention: requests vs generated content

Diego —

Thanks for taking this call earlier. As part of BrightBank's procurement security review we need a concise, written answer to how Redwood handles default log retention for interactive requests. Specifically: what you store by default for (1) raw user requests/prompts, (2) model-generated outputs/responses, and (3) streaming/intermediate tokens (KV cache, partial outputs). We also need to understand default retention windows, whether any of this is persisted to long-term telemetry stores, and the erasure path if we request deletion.

Could you provide an example statement we can include in our SOC questionnaire and any supporting artifact (e.g., a one-page matrix)? If there are enterprise options (shorter retention, no retention, residency controls), please call them out with how they are contracted.

Best,
Anita Rao
Security Manager — BrightBank
[attachment: BrightBank_RFI_20280718.pdf (application/pdf)]

From: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.ai>
To: Anita Rao <anita.rao@brightbank.com>
Date: Tue, 18 Jul 2028 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: RFI — default log retention: requests vs generated content

Anita —

Thanks for the clear questions. Below is a concise summary you can drop into the questionnaire; I also attached a short matrix PDF with examples. TL;DR: we separate 'requests' (raw inputs), 'generated outputs' (final model answers), and transient runtime artifacts (streamed tokens/KV cache). Default hosted behavior and enterprise options are called out.

Official summary (copyable):
- What we store by default:
  • Requests/prompts: By default, the hosted Redwood API retains the raw request payload (user prompt and metadata) for 45 days for troubleshooting, abuse detection, and quality monitoring.
  • Generated outputs/responses: Final model-generated outputs are retained by default for 45 days as part of the request record; outputs used for caching (prefix/KV caches) are ephemeral and are not written to long-term telemetry stores unless tied to a persisted artifact (e.g., embeddings index or customer opt-in).
  • Streaming/intermediate tokens & KV cache: Streaming tokens and per-request KV cache are transient in the runtime and are not persisted to the telemetry store by default; they are held in-memory for the duration of the session and purged after completion.
- Long-term telemetry: Aggregated metrics and anonymized telemetry (latency histograms, token counts) are stored in aggregated analytics systems with indefinite retention by default; these do not contain raw prompt content.
- Erasure and deletion path: Customers may request erasure of request/response records via the Console or API. For hosted accounts, deletion is performed within the 45-day retention window and prioritized under enterprise SLA when requested; we maintain an audit trail of the deletion action (timestamp, requester) but do not retain the deleted payload.
- Enterprise options: For Dedicated and Private deployments, customers can request shorter default retention or disable retention entirely via contract (addendum). Data residency controls (region-specific storage and key management) are available as part of Private offerings.

Notes/Examples in attached matrix: mapping of "request" vs "derived artifact" (e.g., embeddings, reranker indices) and their default storage. Let me know if BrightBank needs a custom retention addendum and we’ll route to Legal.

Attachments: BrightBank_RFI_Answers_20280718.pdf

—
Diego Fernandez
Head of Sales — Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-0193

source.subject: Marketplace: data locality & record lifecycle accountability — Cloudfolio onboarding
messages:
From: Priya Singh <priya.singh@cloudfolio.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, legal@cloudfolio.com
Date: Sat, 14 Jul 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Marketplace onboarding — data lifecycle & deletion responsibilities

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the onboarding call yesterday. As Cloudfolio prepares its listing, Legal and Ops need a short Q&A we can attach to the contract. Two quick items we can’t leave ambiguous:

1) Deletion trigger ownership — when a customer terminates or requests data deletion via Cloudfolio, who (Cloudfolio or Redwood) is responsible for executing deletion across persisted storage and any caching layers? We need a step-by-step flow to include in the SLA.

2) Multi-cloud locality — our customers will choose regions across AWS us-west-2, GCP us-central1, and Azure eastus. We need confirmation that deletion will be carried out in the data’s home region(s) and that logs/audit evidence will be provided per-region.

Attaching our initial list of contract requirements for reference. Can you provide 1–2 bullet paragraphs per item we can hand to our legal team? Also let us know if Redwood needs any contract language specific to marketplace listing or if you’ll accept Cloudfolio as the single point of contact for deletion requests.

Appreciate a quick turnaround — our legal lead wants to close the listing by the end of next week.

Thanks,
Priya Singh
Head of Platform Operations
Cloudfolio

Attachments: onboarding_requirements.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Priya Singh <priya.singh@cloudfolio.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 14 Jul 2028 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace onboarding — data lifecycle & deletion responsibilities

Hi Priya —

Thanks — understood. Below are concise answers you can drop into the Q&A for legal. I’ve also attached an updated SLA excerpt (Cloudfolio_Data_SLA_draft_v2.pdf) with suggested language and an architecture sketch (deletion_flow_v1.png).

Short answers you can include now:
- Deletion trigger ownership: Cloudfolio is the primary requester (single point of contact). Redwood executes the deletion across our managed runtime and any persistent storage we control. We provide a deletion confirmation with per-region audit entries to Cloudfolio.
- Multi-cloud locality: For hosted marketplace listings, data persists only in the region selected at customer creation. Redwood will execute deletion within the same region(s). For Dedicated/Private deployments we can tie deletion behavior to the customer’s deployment model — language in the SLA is different there (see attached).

Next steps:
1) Jordan — can your team confirm the list of endpoints (API/webhook) we’ll expose for deletion requests and the expected JSON payload?
2) Marissa — can you add brief technical notes on our retained cache vs persisted storage and the expected completion window per region?

If you want, I can convene a 30-minute sync with Legal + Ops next week to agree on wording.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Partnerships
Redwood Inference

Attachment: Cloudfolio_Data_SLA_draft_v2.pdf

On Sat, Jul 14, 2028 at 09:12 AM Priya Singh <priya.singh@cloudfolio.com> wrote:
> [original message]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Priya Singh <priya.singh@cloudfolio.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 14 Jul 2028 12:25:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace onboarding — data lifecycle & deletion responsibilities

Hi Priya — adding technical detail to what Ben outlined so legal has clear, testable commitments.

Operational model (hosted marketplace listing):
- Ingestion and ephemeral model runtime: inference requests may produce transient artifacts (KV cache, model context) that are kept in GPU-local memory or ephemeral stores for the duration of the session and are not persisted beyond configured TTLs (default 24 hours) unless explicitly stored by the customer. Those ephemeral artifacts are deleted by the runtime immediately after TTL expiry or session termination.

- Persistent storage: any customer-uploaded files or logs are written to region-specific object storage (e.g., S3 in us-west-2, GCS in us-central1, Azure Blob in eastus). Deletion request flow for persisted objects:
  1) Confirm request origin (Cloudfolio admin webhook or signed API request).
  2) Mark records for deletion in metadata index (fast), enqueue region-specific deletion job.
  3) Execute object store deletion + tombstone propagation to CDN/edge caches.
  4) Run verification job that checks object absence and record hash mismatch (audit proof).
  5) Emit per-region audit entry (timestamp, object ids, job id, deletion status).

- Cross-region considerations: if a customer has replicas in multiple regions, Redwood will delete replicas in each region where data is stored. Our SLA language will define "per-region completion" windows (we propose: 30 days for full verification across all persisted replicas; faster for non-replicated single-region objects).

- Proof of deletion: we can provide a signed audit bundle (JSON lines) containing object ids, deletion job ids, timestamps, and verification hashes. For marketplace listings we propose delivering this via secure S3 link and a signed email to Cloudfolio ops. For Dedicated/Private customers, proof-of-deletion can be delivered to the customer’s configured audit endpoint.

- Exceptions & retention holds: Redwood will honor court orders/retention holds. The SLA needs language on how those holds affect deletion timelines. We’ll also need to capture whether Cloudfolio will act as the custodian of legal holds or if Redwood needs to escalate holds through a legal contact.

Clock/latency expectations:
- Fast-path (single-region, no legal hold): deletion job scheduled within 1 hour, bulk verification complete within 48 hours for small datasets, up to 30 days for large replicated datasets.

I can convert the bullets above into contractual language (two paragraphs + definitions) once we settle the per-region completion window.

-Marissa
Sr. Product Manager, Platform
Redwood Inference

On Sat, Jul 14, 2028 at 10:03 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> [Ben's reply with attachments]

From: Cloudfolio Ops <ops@cloudfolio.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Singh <priya.singh@cloudfolio.com>, legal@cloudfolio.com
Date: Sat, 14 Jul 2028 15:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace onboarding — data lifecycle & deletion responsibilities

Thanks Marissa — that is helpful. Two follow-ups from Ops/Legal: 

1) Contractual SLA ask: Cloudfolio needs explicit language that Redwood will complete deletion and provide verification within 30 days for all persisted data across regions, unless a legal hold is in place. Can Redwood accept this as a marketplace default? If not, propose alternative ranges.

2) Proof format: we prefer a signed JSON-lines audit bundle and an API endpoint where Cloudfolio can POST a deletion request and then poll the job status. Can you share the request/response shape? If you prefer we can own the deletion webhook and translate it to Redwood’s API; either way we need a test run before listing goes live.

We’re ready to take your two-paragraph contractual wording and propose a redline. Priya would like a test deletion on a non-production test tenant next Monday (17th) — can Redwood support that?

Thanks,
Cloudfolio Ops

> On Jul 14, 2028, at 12:25 PM, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com> wrote:

preview:
From: Priya Singh <priya.singh@cloudfolio.com>

Date: Sat, 14 Jul 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Marketplace onboarding — data lifecycle & deletion responsibilities

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the onboarding call yesterday. As Cloudfolio prepares its listing, Legal and Ops need a short Q&A we can attach to the contract. Two quick items we can’t leave ambiguous:

1) Deletion trigger ownership — when a customer terminates or requests data deletion via Cloudfolio, who (Cloudfolio or Redwood) is responsible for executing deletion across persisted storage and any caching layers? We need a step-by-step flow to include in the SLA.

2) Multi-cloud locality — our customers will choose regions across AWS us-west-2, GCP us-central1, and Azure eastus. We need confirmation that deletion will be carried out in the data’s home region(s) and that logs/audit evidence will be provided per-region.

Attaching our initial list of contract requirements for reference. Can you provide 1–2 bullet paragraphs per item we can hand to our legal team? Also let us know if Redwood needs any contract language specific to marketplace listing or if you’ll accept Cloudfolio as the single point of contact for deletion requests.

Thanks,
Priya Singh
Head of Platform Operations
Cloudfolio

Subject: Re: Marketplace onboarding — data lifecycle & deletion responsibilities

Hi Priya —

Thanks — understood. Below are concise answers you can drop into the Q&A for legal. I’ve also attached an updated SLA excerpt (Cloudfolio_Data_SLA_draft_v2.pdf) with suggested language and an architecture sketch (deletion_flow_v1.png).

Short answers you can include now:
- Deletion trigger ownership: Cloudfolio is the primary requester (single point of contact). Redwood executes the deletion across our managed runtime and any persistent storage we control. We provide a deletion confirmation with per-region audit entries to Cloudfolio.
- Multi-cloud locality: For hosted marketplace listings, data persists only in the region selected at customer creation. Redwood will execute deletion within the same region(s). For Dedicated/Private deployments we can tie deletion behavior to the customer’s deployment model — language in the SLA is different there (see attached).

Next steps:
1) Jordan — can your team confirm the list of endpoints (API/webhook) we’ll expose for deletion requests and the expected JSON payload?
2) Marissa — can you add brief technical notes on our retained cache vs persisted storage and the expected completion window per region?

Best,

On Sat, Jul 14, 2028 at 09:12 AM Priya Singh <priya.singh@cloudfolio.com> wrote:
> [original message]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Priya Singh <priya.singh@cloudfolio.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 14 Jul 2028 12:25:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace onboarding — data lifecycle & deletion responsibilities

Hi Priya — adding technical detail to what Ben outlined so legal has clear, testable commitments.

Operational model (hosted marketplace listing):
- Ingestion and ephemeral model runtime: inference requests may produce transient artifacts (KV cache, model context) that are kept in GPU-local memory or ephemeral stores for the duration of the session and are not persisted beyond configured TTLs (default 24 hours) unless explicitly stored by the customer. Those ephemeral artifacts are deleted by the runtime immediately after TTL expiry or session termination.

- Persistent storage: any customer-uploaded files or logs are written to region-specific object storage (e.g., S3 in us-west-2, GCS in us-central1, Azure Blob in eastus). Deletion request flow for persisted objects:
  1) Confirm request origin (Cloudfolio admin webhook or signed API request).
  2) Mark records for deletion in metadata index (fast), enqueue region-specific deletion job.
  3) Execute object store deletion + tombstone propagation to CDN/edge caches.
  4) Run verification job that checks object absence and record hash mismatch (audit proof).
  5) Emit per-region audit entry (timestamp, object ids, job id, deletion status).

- Cross-region considerations: if a customer has replicas in multiple regions, Redwood will delete replicas in each region where data is stored. Our SLA language will define \"per-region completion\" windows (we propose: 30 days for full verification across all persisted replicas; faster for non-replicated single-region objects).

- Proof of deletion: we can provide a signed audit bundle (JSON lines) containing object ids, deletion job ids, timestamps, and verification hashes. For marketplace listings we propose delivering this via secure S3 link and a signed email to Cloudfolio ops. For Dedicated/Private customers, proof-of-deletion can be delivered to the customer’s configured audit endpoint.

- Exceptions & retention holds: Redwood will honor court orders/retention holds. The SLA needs language on how those holds affect deletion timelines. We’ll also need to capture whether Cloudfolio will act as the custodian of legal holds or if Redwood needs to escalate holds through a legal contact.

Clock/latency expectations:
- Fast-path (single-region, no legal hold): deletion job scheduled within 1 hour, bulk verification complete within 48 hours for small datasets, up to 30 days for large replicated datasets.

I can convert the bullets above into contractual language (two paragraphs + definitions) once we settle the per-region completion window.

-Marissa
Sr. Product Manager, Platform
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Session fragmentation on supplier portal blocking PO approvals - help needed
messages:
From: Sarah Tan <sarah.tan@aperturemfg.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 05 Jul 2028 16:12:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Onboarding - portal access and PO visibility

Hi Ben,

We completed the Coupa invite flow this morning, but our finance team can't see new POs in the supplier portal. The UI shows us as 'logged in' (we get a successful SSO redirect) but the PO list is empty. If we try a second login immediately, we get routed into a different tenant view with a different org name in the header — seems like session affinity/page sharding.

Attached are: a short trace of the SSO redirect (sso-redirect-trace.txt), and a CSV export of the pending POs (pending-po-list.csv) with the PO numbers that are invisible. We also uploaded our W9 (w9-scan.pdf) during onboarding.

This is blocking our AP approval cycle; three POs (PO-9482, PO-9483, PO-9489) need urgent acknowledgement to avoid payment delays. Can you check if there is a tenant-mapping or cookie partitioning issue on your side?

Thanks,
Sarah Tan
Accounts Payable Lead
Aperture Manufacturing

---

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Tan <sarah.tan@aperturemfg.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 05 Jul 2028 16:43:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Onboarding - portal access and PO visibility

Hi Sarah —

Thanks for the detailed notes and the traces, that helps. Quick read: looks like the SSO redirect yields two different session cookies for the same browser (we suspect a cookie partitioning scenario when the customer has third-party cookie blocking + our multi-tenant redirector).

Immediate plan:
- I opened SUP-4821 in our internal tracker and attached your trace.
- Jordan, can you run a tenant affinity check for aperturemfg.com and confirm if the load balancer is setting more than one shard cookie for the redirector?
- Marissa, please verify whether the W9 upload completed into the expected S3 path for Aperture Manufacturing — if the profile is partially created that could also hide PO records.

Sarah, two workarounds while we investigate: use an incognito window and re-open the portal link (this forces a fresh set-cookie handshake), or clear third-party cookie blockers for our domain. I know that's not ideal for a corporate laptop, so we'll push a fix if it's on our routing side.

I'll follow up within 2 business hours with initial findings. Apologies for the interruption to your AP cadence.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sarah Tan <sarah.tan@aperturemfg.com>
Date: Wed, 05 Jul 2028 18:01:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Onboarding - portal access and PO visibility

Ben / Marissa —

I pulled logs for the Aperture redirect flow and confirmed two Set-Cookie headers being emitted during the auth handoff from our redirector: one cookie for _rwd_shard and another for _rwd_affinity. Chrome's partitioned cookie behavior can treat those as separate contexts when third-party protections are enabled, which would explain the UI switching between tenant views on subsequent hits.

Immediate mitigation I tried from ops: added a short-lived sticky cookie (ttl=30s) on the redirect endpoint to bias the flow to a single shard. I deployed the change to the staging redirector and replicated the flow with a simulated customer browser: PO list appears consistently.

Next steps:
- Promote sticky cookie change to production after a quick canary (I scheduled SUP-4821-canary for 19:00 UTC tonight).
- Marissa, if the W9 upload landed in a different path because the profile was created under the alternate shard, we may need to rebind the profile objects to the correct tenant id (small DB migration).
- Ben, after the canary I'll ask support to re-invite Aperture with a fresh link that forces a new session.

Sarah, if you can try the incognito test and reply with results that will speed validation; otherwise we will push the change and send you a validated link.

-Jordan
Production Engineer, Redwood Inference

---

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Tan <sarah.tan@aperturemfg.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 06 Jul 2028 10:03:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Onboarding - portal access and PO visibility

Hi Sarah — quick update:

Jordan validated a staging fix and we promoted a canary for the sticky-cookie change (SUP-4821). We also reprocessed your W9 entry and it is now bound to the Aperture tenant id; Marissa confirmed the file landed in the correct S3 path.

Can you please: 1) try the new portal invite link I just sent (separate email with subject "Aperture — portal access (fresh session)"); and 2) confirm whether PO-9482/9483/9489 appear in your PO queue once you click through? If anything still looks off, forward a screenshot and we'll escalate to an ops rollback/inspection.

We're keeping SUP-4821 open until 48 hours after you confirm successful access. Apologies again for the disruption — we appreciate the fast turn here.

Best,
Ben
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

---

Hi Sarah — quick update:

Jordan validated a staging fix and we promoted a canary for the sticky-cookie change (SUP-4821). We also reprocessed your W9 entry and it is now bound to the Aperture tenant id; Marissa confirmed the file landed in the correct S3 path.

Can you please: 1) try the new portal invite link I just sent (separate email with subject "Aperture — portal access (fresh session)"); and 2) confirm whether PO-9482/9483/9489 appear in your PO queue once you click through? If anything still looks off, forward a screenshot and we'll escalate to an ops rollback/inspection.

We're keeping SUP-4821 open until 48 hours after you confirm successful access. Apologies again for the disruption — we appreciate the fast turn here.

Best,
Ben
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

---
text:
From: Sarah Tan <sarah.tan@aperturemfg.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 05 Jul 2028 16:12:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Onboarding - portal access and PO visibility

Hi Ben,

We completed the Coupa invite flow this morning, but our finance team can't see new POs in the supplier portal. The UI shows us as 'logged in' (we get a successful SSO redirect) but the PO list is empty. If we try a second login immediately, we get routed into a different tenant view with a different org name in the header — seems like session affinity/page sharding.

Attached are: a short trace of the SSO redirect (sso-redirect-trace.txt), and a CSV export of the pending POs (pending-po-list.csv) with the PO numbers that are invisible. We also uploaded our W9 (w9-scan.pdf) during onboarding.

This is blocking our AP approval cycle; three POs (PO-9482, PO-9483, PO-9489) need urgent acknowledgement to avoid payment delays. Can you check if there is a tenant-mapping or cookie partitioning issue on your side?

Thanks,
Sarah Tan
Accounts Payable Lead
Aperture Manufacturing

---



From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Tan <sarah.tan@aperturemfg.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 05 Jul 2028 16:43:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Onboarding - portal access and PO visibility

Hi Sarah —

Thanks for the detailed notes and the traces, that helps. Quick read: looks like the SSO redirect yields two different session cookies for the same browser (we suspect a cookie partitioning scenario when the customer has third-party cookie blocking + our multi-tenant redirector).

Immediate plan:
- I opened SUP-4821 in our internal tracker and attached your trace.
- Jordan, can you run a tenant affinity check for aperturemfg.com and confirm if the load balancer is setting more than one shard cookie for the redirector?
- Marissa, please verify whether the W9 upload completed into the expected S3 path for Aperture Manufacturing — if the profile is partially created that could also hide PO records.

Sarah, two workarounds while we investigate: use an incognito window and re-open the portal link (this forces a fresh set-cookie handshake), or clear third-party cookie blockers for our domain. I know that's not ideal for a corporate laptop, so we'll push a fix if it's on our routing side.

I'll follow up within 2 business hours with initial findings. Apologies for the interruption to your AP cadence.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

---



From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sarah Tan <sarah.tan@aperturemfg.com>
Date: Wed, 05 Jul 2028 18:01:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Onboarding - portal access and PO visibility

Ben / Marissa —

I pulled logs for the Aperture redirect flow and confirmed two Set-Cookie headers being emitted during the auth handoff from our redirector: one cookie for _rwd_shard and another for _rwd_affinity. Chrome's partitioned cookie behavior can treat those as separate contexts when third-party protections are enabled, which would explain the UI switching between tenant views on subsequent hits.

Immediate mitigation I tried from ops: added a short-lived sticky cookie (ttl=30s) on the redirect endpoint to bias the flow to a single shard. I deployed the change to the staging redirector and replicated the flow with a simulated customer browser: PO list appears consistently.

Next steps:
- Promote sticky cookie change to production after a quick canary (I scheduled SUP-4821-canary for 19:00 UTC tonight).
- Marissa, if the W9 upload landed in a different path because the profile was created under the alternate shard, we may need to rebind the profile objects to the correct tenant id (small DB migration).
- Ben, after the canary I'll ask support to re-invite Aperture with a fresh link that forces a new session.

Sarah, if you can try the incognito test and reply with results that will speed validation; otherwise we will push the change and send you a validated link.

-Jordan
Production Engineer, Redwood Inference

---

Date: Mon, 25 Jun 2028 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Coordinating Day 0: orientation access, cross-team coverage, and joins

Hi Samir / Priya,

Thanks again for choosing Redwood — excited to get week-zero started. To make Day 0 (orientation) frictionless, below are the proposed slots, a short agenda, the expected attendee coverage from both sides, and the hosting/join links. Please confirm which slot works for you and whether you want us to own the host role for the session or hand it off to your SRE lead.

Proposed slots (60 min):
- Tue Jul 3, 09:00–10:00 PT / 12:00–13:00 ET
- Tue Jul 3, 13:00–14:00 PT / 16:00–17:00 ET
- Wed Jul 4, 08:00–09:00 PT / 11:00–12:00 ET

Quick agenda (30–60 min):
1) Welcome & objectives (5)
2) Account intro and security/access expectations (10)
3) Day 0 runbook and walk-through of Console + SSO setup (20)
4) Roles / on-call & escalation lanes (10)
5) Open items & next steps (5)

Suggested attendees (we recommend 6–8):
- From Avery Health: Product owner, SRE lead (SSO owner), Security contact
- From Redwood: Camila (CSM / program manager), Ben (Onboarding lead), Jordan (Platform engineer)

Host options: we can host (Redwood Zoom + record) or we can join your bridge — let us know preference.

Prework for attendees:
- Quick SSO checklist (attached)
- Add attendees to the Day 0 roster (attached .csv)

Meeting links (if Redwood hosts):
- Zoom: https://redwood.zoom.us/j/87654321
- Recording & notes doc will be shared in the Drive folder after the session.

Attachments: orientation-runbook.pdf, day0-attendee-roster.csv, sso-checklist.docx

If none of the slots work, send 2 options and include the timezone. Happy to split the orientation into a short 30 min executive + 30 min technical breakouts if that helps with schedules.

Thanks,

Camila Reyes
Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0184

--
Redwood Inference — production-ready LLM inference.

source.subject: Finalizing renewal true-up and payment routing (HelixHealth)
messages:
From: Samantha Price <samantha.price@helixhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Jun 2028 10:15:00 +0000
Subject: Renewal true-up — usage variance and PO

Ben —

Hope you’re well. As we’re closing the quarter and preparing for the renewal on July 1, we pulled our usage report and see a variance versus the PO (PO #4571) that needs a final true-up. I attached the Helix_Usage_Q2.xlsx with the token breakdown and a short summary tab — rough delta is ~€12,400 before VAT.

Two asks so we can close the book this week:
- Can Redwood issue a final true-up invoice (net of the already-applied credits) that we can route to our accounts payable?
- Confirm which legal entity you’ll invoice for VAT (Redwood Inc. or Redwood EU entity) and provide the invoice remit details (IBAN/SWIFT) so we can raise payment.

We’re on standard 30-day terms and prefer wire for end-of-term settlements. I’ve cc’d our billing address in the header.

Thanks — happy to jump on a quick call if helpful.

Samantha Price
Head of Finance, HelixHealth
attachments: Helix_Usage_Q2.xlsx (xlsx)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Price <samantha.price@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Jun 2028 13:02:11 +0000
Subject: Re: Renewal true-up — usage variance and PO

Thanks Samantha — received and taking this on. Looping in Karthik from billing and Naomi from finance so we can produce the final paperwork and confirm VAT remit flow. Jordan, FYI for account context (we’ll want to lock the renewal once invoices are issued).

Karthik — can you confirm the true-up math against the attached sheet and whether we should issue two items (final true-up invoice + credit memo for any previously applied proration)? Also need to know which legal entity to bill for EU VAT.

Happy to coordinate a 20-min sync if needed.

— Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Lead — Redwood Inference
attachments: (none)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Jun 2028 15:28:45 +0000
Subject: RE: Renewal true-up — usage variance and PO

On Fri, Jun 23, 2028 at 13:02, Ben Carter wrote:
> Karthik — can you confirm the true-up math against the attached sheet...

I pulled the Helix_Usage_Q2.xlsx and ran the reconciliation. Numbers look consistent with your summary — net delta ~€12,385.70 before VAT. Proposed billing approach:

1) One final true-up invoice (Invoice A) for the delta amount.
2) One credit memo (Credit 1) reflecting the prorated credit we applied in April so the net reflects what Samantha flagged.

Tax/VAT notes: HelixHealth is EU-registered (you listed a VAT ID in your PO). We should invoice Redwood Ireland (Redwood Inference EU Ltd) to apply reverse-charge where applicable — but we need Helix to confirm VAT ID & residency. If they prefer Redwood US entity, we’ll include VAT lines and use our Irish bank for EUR settlement to avoid FX routing for them.

I’ll draft the Invoice A (PDF) and share a draft here. Naomi — please prep the IBAN (HSBC IE) and the final remit text (include SWIFT/BIC and our invoice contact).

Karthik
Senior Billing Manager, Redwood Inference
attachments: (will attach draft invoice)

From: Samantha Price <samantha.price@helixhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@helixhealth.com>
Date: Sat, 24 Jun 2028 09:04:20 +0000
Subject: Re: Renewal true-up — usage variance and PO

Thanks — VAT details below.

Company: HelixHealth Ltd
VAT ID: GB999888777
Billing contact: billing@helixhealth.com
PO: 4571 (attached)

We confirm preference for an invoice issued to HelixHealth Ltd (UK VAT) and wire to your EU account is fine. We require invoice PDF + a matching PO number on the invoice for our AP team. Net30 is fine.

Attached: PO_4571.pdf

Samantha
attachments: PO_4571.pdf (pdf)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Price <samantha.price@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 25 Jun 2028 11:20:03 +0000
Subject: Re: Renewal true-up — usage variance and PO

Samantha — appreciate the quick confirmation. Karthik and Naomi will finalize the invoice and credit memo today.

Operational notes for HelixHealth AP:
- Final documents you’ll receive: Invoice A (true-up) and Credit 1 (proration).
- Invoice will reference PO 4571 and include the Helix VAT ID as provided.
- Payment routing: HSBC IE / Redwood Inference EU Ltd — we’ll confirm IBAN in the invoice footer.
- Payment terms: Net30 from invoice date; please route payments with the invoice number in the wire reference.

Jordan and I will close the renewal after invoices are issued. Expect a DocuSign/AR link with the PDF bundle in the next few hours.

— Ben
attachments: Final_Invoice_Draft.pdf (preview)

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>

From: Samantha Price <samantha.price@helixhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Jun 2028 10:15:00 +0000
Subject: Renewal true-up — usage variance and PO

Ben —

Hope you’re well. As we’re closing the quarter and preparing for the renewal on July 1, we pulled our usage report and see a variance versus the PO (PO #4571) that needs a final true-up. I attached the Helix_Usage_Q2.xlsx with the token breakdown and a short summary tab — rough delta is ~€12,400 before VAT.

Two asks so we can close the book this week:
- Can Redwood issue a final true-up invoice (net of the already-applied credits) that we can route to our accounts payable?
- Confirm which legal entity you’ll invoice for VAT (Redwood Inc. or Redwood EU entity) and provide the invoice remit details (IBAN/SWIFT) so we can raise payment.

We’re on standard 30-day terms and prefer wire for end-of-term settlements. I’ve cc’d our billing address in the header.

Thanks — happy to jump on a quick call if helpful.

Samantha Price
Head of Finance, HelixHealth
