source.subject: POC onramp: API + streaming integration checkpoints
messages:
From: Lina Torres <lina.torres@brightbridge.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 21 Jun 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick follow-up from earlier — POC objectives and questions

Thanks again for the demo this morning. Couple quick clarifications from our side as we sketch the trial: 

- Primary success criteria for us are: (1) intent recognition accuracy for support routing (we operate a multi-intent classifier + rerank), (2) 95th p95 generation latency under 300ms for short prompts (<= 256 tokens), and (3) clear observability for per-route token spend.

- We want to test both standard REST calls and streaming for long-response scenarios (agent workflows). Important: tool calling must be isolated to the trial environment; we can provide a staging webhook.

Next actions we need from Redwood to finalize scope: a short POC plan that shows the integration checkpoints (SDK routes, streaming endpoints, tool-call webhook handshake), owners, and a 2-week timeline. Also sharing our system overview (attached).

Lina
Ops Lead, BrightBridge
[Attachment: BrightBridge-Sys-Overview.pdf (application/pdf)]
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Lina Torres <lina.torres@brightbridge.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 21 Jun 2028 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick follow-up from earlier — POC objectives and questions

Lina — thanks for the clear ask and the system doc. I’ve sketched a concise POC onramp below; will drop a formal sheet by EOD. High-level: 2-week technical trial, focus on API + streaming + tool-calling through a staging webhook.

POC checkpoints (short):
- Week 0 (pre-kick): credentials + network (VPC allowlist for staging IPs), HubSpot kickoff link. Owner: Alex (Redwood) / BrightBridge ops
- Day 1: REST smoke tests (/v1/generate for sync, /v1/embeddings for similarity). Owner: Marissa (SE) / BB dev
- Day 2–4: Streaming integration (/v1/stream) for agent-style conversations + client-side reconnect behavior. Owner: Karthik (infra/stream) / BB dev
- Day 5–7: Tool-calling handshake (staging webhook, signing header) and fail-open fallback test. Owner: Jordan (solutions) / BB ops
- Week 2: Acceptance run and success-matrix evaluation (latency, accuracy, cost). Owner: Alex & Marissa / BB PM

Success criteria draft (for acceptance):
- Intent routing: top-1 intent accuracy >= 86% on the provided eval set
- Streaming: p95 token-delivery latency <= 300ms under trial load (burst 50qps)
- Cost: average per-response token cost within target band (we'll baseline during run)

Deliverables I’ll attach tonight: MS Excel success matrix, curl examples for SDK and streaming, and a small sample Node client for reconnects.

Quick Qs for your engineers: 1) Do you have an allowlist IP range or prefer token- or DNS-based allowlisting? 2) Is the staging webhook reachable behind a static IP?

—Alex
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 21 Jun 2028 11:28:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick follow-up from earlier — POC objectives and questions

Adding a couple implementation notes for the team and for Lina: 

- SDK routes: recommend shipping with the official Redwood JS SDK (v1.4.0+). Key calls we will surface in examples: 
  * client.generate({ model, prompt, max_tokens, streaming: false }) -> /v1/generate
  * client.stream({ model, prompt }) -> /v1/stream (Server-sent events / chunked transfer)
  * client.callTool({ tool, input, callbackUrl }) -> triggers our tool-calling flow; we will sign callbacks with x-redwood-signature header.

- For streaming reconnects we’ll provide a sample token-refresh approach and an incremental-kv cache header so clients can resume without repeating context where possible.

- Observability: we will enable per-route token counts and attach a short trace id (x-rdwd-trace) to every request for correlating with your telemetry.

I’ll prepare the poc-success-matrix.xlsx with explicit test vectors (intent eval set, latency scenarios, cost thresholds).

Marissa
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
From: Lina Torres <lina.torres@brightbridge.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 22 Jun 2028 08:47:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick follow-up from earlier — POC objectives and questions

Thanks — that looks good and pragmatic. Quick answers to your questions: 
- Allowlist: we can open a /24 on our staging VPC for Redwood staging IPs; we’ll provide IPs by Monday.
- Webhook: staging URL is reachable but sits behind our corporate proxy; we can provide a static egress IP.

A couple of asks from our engineering lead: 
1) We need a signed sample callback and the header verification snippet in Python (our stack is Python + FastAPI).
2) Can you also include a short test harness that runs the streaming scenario with a 50 qps burst and emits p95/p50 metrics to stdout?

If you can get the sample artifacts and success matrix by Monday, we can kick the trial on Tue/Wed next week.

Lina
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Lina Torres <lina.torres@brightbridge.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 25 Jun 2028 09:05:00 -07:00
Subject: POC artifacts: success matrix + samples (attached)

Lina — attaching the initial artifacts per our plan. Short notes: 
- poc-success-matrix.xlsx contains test vectors, pass/fail thresholds and owner columns (Redwood/BB). Please review the intent eval set located in the shared Drive link.
- Attached a Python snippet for verifying x-redwood-signature and a small Node-based streaming harness that simulates the 50qps burst and prints p50/p95. The harness uses chunked transfer emulation and reconnect logic.

Next steps: 
- BrightBridge provides IP allowlist + confirmation of staging webhook reachability (tomorrow)
- We finalize kickoff call (30 min) once IPs are confirmed — propose Wed 26 Jun 10:00am PT

Thanks — Alex
[Attachment: poc-success-matrix.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)]
From: Lina Torres <lina.torres@brightbridge.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Jun 2028 17:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: POC artifacts: success matrix + samples (attached)

Confirmed — our networking team provided the /24 and static egress info (details in the Drive link). Wed 26 Jun 10:00am PT works for the kickoff. We reviewed the matrix and have one small tweak: increase intent accuracy threshold to >=88% for top-1 on the eval set (we have a larger baseline now).

We’ll also attach a short list of 12 edge-case prompts to the Drive folder for the intent eval. Looking forward — Lina

From: Lina Torres <lina.torres@brightbridge.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Jun 2028 17:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: POC artifacts: success matrix + samples (attached)

Confirmed — our networking team provided the /24 and static egress info (details in the Drive link). Wed 26 Jun 10:00am PT works for the kickoff. We reviewed the matrix and have one small tweak: increase intent accuracy threshold to >=88% for top-1 on the eval set (we have a larger baseline now).

We’ll also attach a short list of 12 edge-case prompts to the Drive folder for the intent eval. Looking forward — Lina
text:
From: Lina Torres <lina.torres@brightbridge.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 21 Jun 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick follow-up from earlier — POC objectives and questions

Thanks again for the demo this morning. Couple quick clarifications from our side as we sketch the trial: 

- Primary success criteria for us are: (1) intent recognition accuracy for support routing (we operate a multi-intent classifier + rerank), (2) 95th p95 generation latency under 300ms for short prompts (<= 256 tokens), and (3) clear observability for per-route token spend.

- We want to test both standard REST calls and streaming for long-response scenarios (agent workflows). Important: tool calling must be isolated to the trial environment; we can provide a staging webhook.

Next actions we need from Redwood to finalize scope: a short POC plan that shows the integration checkpoints (SDK routes, streaming endpoints, tool-call webhook handshake), owners, and a 2-week timeline. Also sharing our system overview (attached).

Lina
Ops Lead, BrightBridge
[Attachment: BrightBridge-Sys-Overview.pdf (application/pdf)]

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Lina Torres <lina.torres@brightbridge.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 21 Jun 2028 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick follow-up from earlier — POC objectives and questions

Lina — thanks for the clear ask and the system doc. I’ve sketched a concise POC onramp below; will drop a formal sheet by EOD. High-level: 2-week technical trial, focus on API + streaming + tool-calling through a staging webhook.

POC checkpoints (short):
- Week 0 (pre-kick): credentials + network (VPC allowlist for staging IPs), HubSpot kickoff link. Owner: Alex (Redwood) / BrightBridge ops
- Day 1: REST smoke tests (/v1/generate for sync, /v1/embeddings for similarity). Owner: Marissa (SE) / BB dev
- Day 2–4: Streaming integration (/v1/stream) for agent-style conversations + client-side reconnect behavior. Owner: Karthik (infra/stream) / BB dev
- Day 5–7: Tool-calling handshake (staging webhook, signing header) and fail-open fallback test. Owner: Jordan (solutions) / BB ops
- Week 2: Acceptance run and success-matrix evaluation (latency, accuracy, cost). Owner: Alex & Marissa / BB PM

Success criteria draft (for acceptance):
- Intent routing: top-1 intent accuracy >= 86% on the provided eval set
- Streaming: p95 token-delivery latency <= 300ms under trial load (burst 50qps)
- Cost: average per-response token cost within target band (we'll baseline during run)

Deliverables I’ll attach tonight: MS Excel success matrix, curl examples for SDK and streaming, and a small sample Node client for reconnects.

Quick Qs for your engineers: 1) Do you have an allowlist IP range or prefer token- or DNS-based allowlisting? 2) Is the staging webhook reachable behind a static IP?

—Alex
Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 21 Jun 2028 11:28:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick follow-up from earlier — POC objectives and questions

Adding a couple implementation notes for the team and for Lina: 

- SDK routes: recommend shipping with the official Redwood JS SDK (v1.4.0+). Key calls we will surface in examples: 
  * client.generate({ model, prompt, max_tokens, streaming: false }) -> /v1/generate
  * client.stream({ model, prompt }) -> /v1/stream (Server-sent events / chunked transfer)
  * client.callTool({ tool, input, callbackUrl }) -> triggers our tool-calling flow; we will sign callbacks with x-redwood-signature header.

- For streaming reconnects we’ll provide a sample token-refresh approach and an incremental-kv cache header so clients can resume without repeating context where possible.

- Observability: we will enable per-route token counts and attach a short trace id (x-rdwd-trace) to every request for correlating with your telemetry.

I’ll prepare the poc-success-matrix.xlsx with explicit test vectors (intent eval set, latency scenarios, cost thresholds).

Marissa
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Lina Torres <lina.torres@brightbridge.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 22 Jun 2028 08:47:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick follow-up from earlier — POC objectives and questions

Thanks — that looks good and pragmatic. Quick answers to your questions: 
- Allowlist: we can open a /24 on our staging VPC for Redwood staging IPs; we’ll provide IPs by Monday.
- Webhook: staging URL is reachable but sits behind our corporate proxy; we can provide a static egress IP.

A couple of asks from our engineering lead: 
1) We need a signed sample callback and the header verification snippet in Python (our stack is Python + FastAPI).
2) Can you also include a short test harness that runs the streaming scenario with a 50 qps burst and emits p95/p50 metrics to stdout?

If you can get the sample artifacts and success matrix by Monday, we can kick the trial on Tue/Wed next week.

Lina


From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Lina Torres <lina.torres@brightbridge.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 25 Jun 2028 09:05:00 -07:00
Subject: POC artifacts: success matrix + samples (attached)

Lina — attaching the initial artifacts per our plan. Short notes: 
- poc-success-matrix.xlsx contains test vectors, pass/fail thresholds and owner columns (Redwood/BB). Please review the intent eval set located in the shared Drive link.
- Attached a Python snippet for verifying x-redwood-signature and a small Node-based streaming harness that simulates the 50qps burst and prints p50/p95. The harness uses chunked transfer emulation and reconnect logic.

Next steps: 
- BrightBridge provides IP allowlist + confirmation of staging webhook reachability (tomorrow)
- We finalize kickoff call (30 min) once IPs are confirmed — propose Wed 26 Jun 10:00am PT

Thanks — Alex
[Attachment: poc-success-matrix.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)]

From: Lina Torres <lina.torres@brightbridge.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Jun 2028 17:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: POC artifacts: success matrix + samples (attached)

Confirmed — our networking team provided the /24 and static egress info (details in the Drive link). Wed 26 Jun 10:00am PT works for the kickoff. We reviewed the matrix and have one small tweak: increase intent accuracy threshold to >=88% for top-1 on the eval set (we have a larger baseline now).

We’ll also attach a short list of 12 edge-case prompts to the Drive folder for the intent eval. Looking forward — Lina

source.subject: Renewal QBR: demo cadence, procurement gate, and succession hosts
messages:
From: Elena Ruiz <elena@stellardata.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@stellardata.com>; Ravi Singh <ravi@stellardata.com>
Date: 2028-06-18T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Renewal QBR + roadmap demo — sequencing and procurement window

Hi David,

Thanks again for the continued partnership — Stellar Data is excited to sync on the renewal QBR and the roadmap demo. We need to fold in procurement and legal timelines this cycle because our renewal has a firm cutover: procurement requires a signed PO by July 8 to keep current pricing.

Can we schedule a 90-minute session the week of June 26 to:
- walk the roadmap highlights for execs (20–25m)
- run the technical demo broken into two live segments + an ops deep-dive (60m)
- confirm procurement/contract milestones and next steps (10m)

Preferable windows: Wed June 28 10:00–11:30 PT or Thu June 29 13:00–14:30 PT. If those don't work, please propose alternates.

Attendees we need from your side: account exec + technical lead + an ops/runbook owner — procurement and our legal team will join the procurement segment.

Attached: high-level ask and the procurement checklist we use internally.

Thanks,
Elena Ruiz
Head of Platform Partnerships
Stellar Data
[Attachment: procurement-checklist.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)]

From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@stellardata.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>; Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>; Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>; Procurement <procurement@stellardata.com>
Date: 2028-06-18T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal QBR + roadmap demo — sequencing and procurement window

Elena — thanks for the clear timeline and the procurement note. We can support a structured 90-minute QBR that meets the PO cutoff. Proposed plan:

Option A (preferred): Wed, Jun 28, 10:00–11:30 PT
- 10:00–10:25: Exec roadmap (David + Jordan)
- 10:25–11:05: Technical demo (split: feature walkthrough 20m + hands-on sample workload 20m) — Priyom to lead
- 11:05–11:15: Ops/runbook & integration checklist (Marissa)
- 11:15–11:30: Procurement/legal Q&A (I will remain for handoff)

We will prepare a short runbook and a split agenda so procurement can sign off on the milestone items live. I attached an internal runbook draft for your procurement team to review.

If procurement needs a separate sync before the QBR to unblock PO language, we can carve 30 minutes on Tue Jun 27. Let me know.

Cheers,
David Kim
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: qbr-demo-runbook-draft.pdf (application/pdf)]

From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>; Elena Ruiz <elena@stellardata.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>; Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-06-18T12:23:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal QBR + roadmap demo — sequencing and procurement window

Thanks David — I can own the technical demo and the runbook handoff. A couple of practical suggestions:

1) Split the tech portion into two 25-min blocks rather than one 40m block: first 25m for the public roadmap + core platform walkthrough, then a 25m ‘live ops’ demo that reproduces a customer workflow end-to-end. This improves attention and gives procurement a clearer ops artifact.
2) We'll designate a primary and a backup host — primary (Priyom), backup (Marissa). If the live workload is sensitive we will also prepare a short recorded fallback that we can play if connectivity/credentials take too long.
3) Attaching the split-agenda doc and the preliminary runbook.

Elena — if Stellar's legal wants to pre-send redlines or a sample SOW, please drop it in the DocuSign link David mentioned and we can confirm acceptance criteria ahead of the QBR.

- Priyom
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: qbr-demo-split-agenda.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document)]

On Tue, Jun 18, 2028 at 10:05 AM David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com> wrote:
> Elena — thanks for the clear timeline and the procurement note. We can support a structured 90-minute QBR that meets the PO cutoff. Proposed plan:
>
> Option A (preferred): Wed, Jun 28, 10:00–11:30 PT
> - 10:00–10:25: Exec roadmap (David + Jordan)
> - 10:25–11:05: Technical demo (split: feature walkthrough 20m + hands-on sample workload 20m) — Priyom to lead
> - 11:05–11:15: Ops/runbook & integration checklist (Marissa)
> - 11:15–11:30: Procurement/legal Q&A (I will remain for handoff)
>
> We will prepare a short runbook and a split agenda so procurement can sign off on the milestone items live.

From: Procurement <procurement@stellardata.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>; Elena Ruiz <elena@stellardata.com>
Cc: Ravi Singh <ravi@stellardata.com>
Date: 2028-06-19T08:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal QBR + roadmap demo — sequencing and procurement window

David/Elena — thank you. A few procurement constraints from our side:

• PO deadline: July 8 to keep current pricing band; otherwise procurement must re-run budget approvals.
• Contract changes: we only need a one-page SOW attachment for the pilot and any redline summary by June 30 to hit internal review cycles.
• SOC2 evidence: please provide an SOC2 AOC link or an upcoming SOC2 report date.

If you can get the SOW and a redline summary uploaded to the DocuSign link by June 30, we can route for signature and get a PO to finance the first committed month by July 6.

Best,
Stellar Data Procurement Team
[Attachment: procurement-timelines.pdf (application/pdf)]

On Tue, Jun 18, 2028 at 09:12 AM Elena Ruiz <elena@stellardata.com> wrote:
> Thanks again for the continued partnership — Stellar Data is excited to sync on the renewal QBR and the roadmap demo.

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@stellardata.com>; Procurement <procurement@stellardata.com>; David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>; Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-06-21T16:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal QBR + roadmap demo — sequencing and procurement window

Elena / procurement team — locking in Wed Jun 28, 10:00–11:30 PT for the QBR. Final notes and attachments:

- Confirmed agenda attached (split by exec / tech / ops / procurement).
- Runbook attached (finalized based on Priyom's notes). Primary host: Priyom Das; backup host: Marissa Cole.
- DocuSign envelope created for the SOW; link below. Please upload any redlines by Jun 30 so we can incorporate them before the signature cycle.

DocuSign: https://redwood.app/docusign/abc123
HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/4821

Next steps (action owners):
- Elena / Stellar: upload SOW redlines by Jun 30 (Procurement)
- David: runbook & final attendee list (by EOD Jun 24)
- Priyom: prepare recorded fallback for the live workload (by EOD Jun 27)
- Marissa: finalize runbook and sharing permissions (done)

If procurement still wants a short prep call, we can do Tue Jun 27, 09:00–09:30 PT; otherwise we'll handle the legal/procurement segment inside the QBR.

Thanks all — looking forward to it.

—
Marissa Cole
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: qbr-demo-runbook.pdf (application/pdf); procurement-checklist.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)]

> Option A (preferred): Wed, Jun 28, 10:00–11:30 PT
> - 10:00–10:25: Exec roadmap (David + Jordan)
> - 10:25–11:05: Technical demo (split: feature walkthrough 20m + hands-on sample workload 20m) — Priyom to lead
> - 11:05–11:15: Ops/runbook & integration checklist (Marissa)
> - 11:15–11:30: Procurement/legal Q&A (I will remain for handoff)
>
> We will prepare a short runbook and a split agenda so procurement can sign off on the milestone items live.


From: Procurement <procurement@stellardata.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>; Elena Ruiz <elena@stellardata.com>
Cc: Ravi Singh <ravi@stellardata.com>
Date: 2028-06-19T08:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal QBR + roadmap demo — sequencing and procurement window

David/Elena — thank you. A few procurement constraints from our side:

• PO deadline: July 8 to keep current pricing band; otherwise procurement must re-run budget approvals.
• Contract changes: we only need a one-page SOW attachment for the pilot and any redline summary by June 30 to hit internal review cycles.
• SOC2 evidence: please provide an SOC2 AOC link or an upcoming SOC2 report date.

If you can get the SOW and a redline summary uploaded to the DocuSign link by June 30, we can route for signature and get a PO to finance the first committed month by July 6.

Best,
Stellar Data Procurement Team
[Attachment: procurement-timelines.pdf (application/pdf)]

> Thanks again for the continued partnership — Stellar Data is excited to sync on the renewal QBR and the roadmap demo.

Subject: Re: Renewal QBR + roadmap demo — sequencing and procurement window

Elena / procurement team — locking in Wed Jun 28, 10:00–11:30 PT for the QBR. Final notes and attachments:

- Confirmed agenda attached (split by exec / tech / ops / procurement).
- Runbook attached (finalized based on Priyom's notes). Primary host: Priyom Das; backup host: Marissa Cole.
- DocuSign envelope created for the SOW; link below. Please upload any redlines by Jun 30 so we can incorporate them before the signature cycle.

DocuSign: https://redwood.app/docusign/abc123
HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/4821

Next steps (action owners):
- Elena / Stellar: upload SOW redlines by Jun 30 (Procurement)
- David: runbook & final attendee list (by EOD Jun 24)
- Priyom: prepare recorded fallback for the live workload (by EOD Jun 27)
- Marissa: finalize runbook and sharing permissions (done)

If procurement still wants a short prep call, we can do Tue Jun 27, 09:00–09:30 PT; otherwise we'll handle the legal/procurement segment inside the QBR.

Thanks all — looking forward to it.

—
Marissa Cole
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: qbr-demo-runbook.pdf (application/pdf); procurement-checklist.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)]

messages:
From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Kirov <ekirov@stellarpay.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Jun 2028 09:06:00 -0700
Subject: Post-signature MAP: execution schedule, canary plan & upskill timeline

Elena — thanks again for the signature yesterday. I attached a first-pass MAP (MAP_StellarPay_v1.xlsx) that embeds the canary/migration and the training windows we discussed on the call. High-level asks here: 

- Please confirm the preferred canary target week (we've left a tentative window of Jul 10–14).
- Confirm the core infra contact for test data and load generation (we need a VPN user + ephemeral test keys).
- Any required security artifacts you need from us beyond the SOC2 checklist (attached).

Quick summary of our proposal (details in the spreadsheet):
- Pre-canary readiness: 2 dry runs in staging (7/3 and 7/6)
- Canary: 7/10–7/14 (traffic ramping: 10% -> 50% -> 100%) with automated rollback thresholds
- Training: two live sessions (7/6: engineering handoff; 7/9: support runbook + playbooks)

If those dates work, I'll update the MAP and convert to v2 for signature/commitments and drop in the HubSpot deal. I included Marissa and Ben on technical tasks and Jordan on success cadence.

Attachments: MAP_StellarPay_v1.xlsx, SOC2-evidence-checklist.pdf

Thanks — Connor
Connor O'Brien
Senior Solutions Lead | Redwood Inference
connor@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0198

From: Elena Kirov <ekirov@stellarpay.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Jun 2028 09:24:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-signature MAP: execution schedule, canary plan & upskill timeline

Connor — thanks, looks good at a glance. A few quick items: 

- The 7/10–7/14 window is fine for the canary, we prefer the ramp schedule you suggested.
- Can we move the engineering handoff training earlier in the day on 7/6 (we have an all-hands in the afternoon)?
- I'll share our onboarding roster (StellarPay_Onboard_Roster.csv) with the names who need access.
- Procurement is finishing an internal amendment today; I'll confirm if we need a DocuSign amendment for the slight timeline changes.

I'll loop Raj (in infra) once I have him free — cc'ing him should be fine. Please send the v2 MAP with explicit owners and acceptance criteria.

Thanks,
Elena
Elena Kirov
Head of Platform Integrations | StellarPay
ekirov@stellarpay.com

To: Elena Kirov <ekirov@stellarpay.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Jun 2028 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-signature MAP: execution schedule, canary plan & upskill timeline

Elena / team — quick technical alignment from our side: 

Proposed canary mechanics (we'll instrument on Redwood side):
- Canary window: 2028-07-10 to 2028-07-14
- Traffic ramp: day1 10% (smoke checks), day2 25%, day3 60%, day4 100%
- Automatic rollback triggers: 3xx/5xx spike > 1.5x baseline, latency p95 > 750ms for 10 consecutive minutes

Staging dry-runs we recommend:
- 2028-07-03: full smoke with simulated production tokens (no PI)
- 2028-07-06: runbook rehearsal + chaos scenario (db failover simulated)

Training: we can do the engineering handoff morning window on 7/6 09:30–11:00 PT and a separate support playbook session 7/9 13:00–14:30 PT. If the team needs a different timezone, we can record and do a second, shorter repeat.

For test data / load: please share a k8s testing namespace and a VPN account or an SFTP drop we can push load artifacts to. Also need the on-call rotation/contacts.

I'll add these as acceptance criteria in v2 of the MAP.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Director, Solutions Engineering | Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwoodinference.com

messages:
From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Sam Patel <sam.patel@healthsync.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 30 May 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: POC Bridge: access sprint — API keys, org onboarding, IP allowlist, VPC peering

source.subject: Post-demo: ZenithCare assurance checklist, DSQ invite & SOC 2 Type II availability
messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@zenithcare.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 08 Jun 2028 14:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: ZenithCare assurance checklist, DSQ invite & SOC 2 Type II availability

Hi Lena — great to meet earlier. Quick recap + suggested next steps from our side on security/compliance so your security team can triage: 

- Ask list from ZenithCare: SOC 2 Type II report (most recent period), bridge letter if the new report is in progress, and temporary access to our trust portal for evidence download.
- We can start the Vendor Security Questionnaire (DSQ) — attaching a prefilled template to accelerate (please confirm if you want it in a different format).
- Proposed owner at Redwood for intake: Jordan (cc'd) for portal invites and artifact uploads; Ben for any contract/PO questions; Marissa for compliance handoff.

If you can confirm the primary security contact, I’ll: (1) initiate the DSQ, (2) provision trust portal access for ZenithCare security, and (3) check Type II availability and share a bridge letter if the next Type II is pending.

Attachments: ZenithCare_DSQ_Template.xlsx (prefill), example Bridge_Letter_Redwood_Template.docx

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com
(415) 555-0198
From: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@zenithcare.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Desai <samir.desai@zenithcare.com>, security@zenithcare.com
Date: Fri, 08 Jun 2028 16:01:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo: ZenithCare assurance checklist, DSQ invite & SOC 2 Type II availability

Thanks Aditya — that was helpful. A couple notes from our security team: 

1) We need the last SOC 2 Type II report covering the 2026 period. If you already have a 2027/2028 report in progress, a signed bridge letter that covers control continuity until the new report is issued will work for initial procurement review.
2) Please provision trust portal access for our security mailbox (security@zenithcare.com) with download rights for artifacts.
3) We use a standard DSQ spreadsheet — can you send the completed template back and we’ll queue it for technical review?

Primary contact for the intake will be Samir Desai (copied). He’ll coordinate uploads to our internal tracker.

One ask: does Redwood have an S3 or SFTP fallback for evidence if the portal is not immediately accessible to us?

Appreciate the quick turnaround.
Lena
Director, IT Risk & Compliance
ZenithCare
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 08 Jun 2028 17:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: ZenithCare security intake — action items (SOC 2 + portal + DSQ)

Team — forwarding ZenithCare's asks. TL;DR: they want the latest SOC 2 Type II (2026 acceptable), bridge letter if newer report pending, trust-portal access for security@zenithcare.com, and a DSQ prefilled then returned. Samir is the intake lead.

Action items: 
- Ben: please confirm whether legal needs to sign confidentiality addendum before providing SOC report PDF; if yes, can you draft a short NDA or redaction plan?
- Marissa: can you pull the SOC2_TypeII_Redwood_2026.pdf and prepare the Bridge_Letter_Redwood_Template.docx for signing?
- Jordan: can you provision a trust portal invite for security@zenithcare.com (download rights) and create an S3 presigned link as fallback? If portal invite will take >24 hours, please provide S3 fallback.

Attachments: SOC2_TypeII_Redwood_2026.pdf (secure artifact) — note we should not upload full PDF to external until Ben confirms NDA/permission.

Thanks — want to get back to them by EOD Monday.
Aditya
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Samir Desai <samir.desai@zenithcare.com>, Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@zenithcare.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 10 Jun 2028 09:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo: ZenithCare assurance checklist, DSQ invite & SOC 2 Type II availability

Hi Samir / Lena — quick update and intake plan: 

1) SOC 2 Type II: We have our 2026 Type II available. Per internal policy we need a quick MDF (Mutual Disclosure Framework) acknowledgement for distribution of the full report. Ben is drafting a one-paragraph scoping acknowledgement; once signed I will share the PDF. For now I attached a redacted summary and the signed bridge letter template to this mail for your review.

[Attachment: SOC2_TypeII_Redwood_2026_summary.pdf (pdf)]
[Attachment: Bridge_Letter_Redwood_Template.docx (docx)]

2) Trust portal: Jordan has provisioned a temporary invite for security@zenithcare.com — access link below. Credentials and MFA setup are sent in a separate secure message from our trust system. Portal link: https://trust.redwood.com/zenithcare/invite/ACCESS-9a8b7c

3) DSQ: Attached is the prefilled DSQ template (ZenithCare_DSQ_Template.xlsx). Please review, mark any additional questions, and return at your convenience. If you prefer we can complete this collaboratively in a Zoom session.

4) S3 fallback: Jordan will generate a time-limited S3 presigned URL for artifact download as a contingency; Ben will confirm whether additional NDA language is required before we enable the full report download.

Next steps requested from ZenithCare: confirm Samir as primary intake owner and sign the one-paragraph distribution acknowledgement (I’ve copied Ben to produce). Once acknowledgement is signed we’ll share the full PDF and finalize portal rights.

Thanks — looking forward to moving this along.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com
(415) 555-0198

source.subject: Telemetry credits & pilot structure for CloudLytics (retention + tracing + dashboards)
messages:
From: Elena Morris <elena@cloudlytics.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: CloudLytics Procurement <procurement@cloudlytics.com>
Date: 2028-06-07T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Telemetry add-ons — retention / tracing / dashboards pricing and pilot

Connor — thanks for the call earlier. Following up with some concrete asks so my procurement and engineering teams can evaluate: 

- We need a price breakdown for the observability add-ons: extended retention (30/90/365 days), tracing sampling tiers, and the advanced dashboard pack (real-time widgets + custom visualizations).
- We're especially interested in a pilot: 90 days, up to 2M tokens/month ingestion for traces/metrics, and a temporary retention lift to 90 days. Would you offer trial credits and what would be the delta to move to paid?
- Please outline committed discounts for year-1 (if we commit to a 12-month reserved capacity + add-on purchase) and any seat/metadata surcharges.

Also attaching a one-page usage snapshot so you can see our current profile. Can you get back with a simple price matrix + a recommended pilot configuration we can share internally?

Thanks,
Elena
Elena Morris
Head of Platform
CloudLytics

Attachment: cloudlytics-usage-snapshot.xlsx (xlsx)

From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morris <elena@cloudlytics.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-06-07T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Telemetry add-ons — retention / tracing / dashboards pricing and pilot

Elena — great to connect. Appreciate the usage snapshot. High level answers up front, details attached.

1) Pilot proposal (recommended) — 90 days
   - Included credits: $25k credits applied to add-ons (covers retention bump to 90 days + tracing sampling to 2M tokens/mo)
   - Dedicated CS touch for onboarding (1 workshop), quarterly QBRs at pilot close
   - Conversion path: pilot credits applied to first invoice if you convert to a 12-month committed plan within 30 days of pilot end

2) Pricing matrix (summary) — see attached redwood-addon-pricing-v2.pdf for line items, but the short version:
   - Retention: Base 30-day included; 90-day add-on = $0.08 / GB-month; 365-day = $0.05 / GB-month (volume discounts apply)
   - Tracing: Metered by ingested span tokens; sampling tiers: standard (baseline), high-sample (+20%) and full-sample (+45%) relative to baseline per-token cost
   - Dashboards (advanced pack): flat license + per-active-dashboard over 50; includes secure sharing + custom widgets

3) Committed discount framing: 10% off add-ons for a 12-mo reserved commitment that meets your current snapshot predictability; 15% for an 18-24mo commit or >30% YoY spend growth guarantee.

I've CC'd Kimberly (pricing) and Jordan (customer success) to confirm details and add any concessions. Attaching the detailed pricing matrix and a sample SLA for the dashboard add-on.

Connor
Connor O'Brien
Account Exec, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-addon-pricing-v2.pdf (pdf)

From: Elena Morris <elena@cloudlytics.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: CloudLytics Procurement <procurement@cloudlytics.com>
Date: 2028-06-07T13:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Telemetry add-ons — retention / tracing / dashboards pricing and pilot

Connor — thanks for the quick turnaround. A couple of clarification notes from procurement/engineering after a quick read: 

- The $25k pilot credit — is that only for add-on charges (retention/tracing/dashboards) or can it be applied to base inference usage as well? Procurement prefers additive credits to be flexible.
- Can you share the expected per-GB delta estimate for our snapshot (we sent ~4TB/month of telemetry currently) — helps procurement model the line item for 90-day retention vs 365-day.
- On discounts: procurement is hoping to see a mid-point option: 12-month commitment at 12% (instead of 10%) in exchange for a revenue commit schedule (monthly ramp up) and a 60-day payment term. Are you able to entertain that concession?

Also, your SLA doc references an uptime objective for the dashboard API — can you confirm latency commitments for dashboard widget load under the advanced pack? Our product team will want that spelled out.

Best,
Elena

Quoted earlier message:
> From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
> Date: 2028-06-07T11:03:00-07:00
> Subject: Re: Telemetry add-ons — retention / tracing / dashboards pricing and pilot
>
> (see attached pricing matrix)

Attachment referenced: redwood-addon-pricing-v2.pdf (pdf)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morris <elena@cloudlytics.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-06-08T09:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Telemetry add-ons — retention / tracing / dashboards pricing and pilot

Elena — thanks, and congrats on the clear ask. I ran the numbers against your 4TB/mo profile and the attached spreadsheet includes two practical scenarios. Key points here: 

- Pilot credit scope: we can allow the $25k pilot credit to be applied across add-ons and base inference consumption (one pool), to make the trial easier from an ops standpoint.
- 90-day vs 365-day estimate (for 4TB/mo):
   • 90-day retention add-on delta ≈ $6,400/mo (approx $0.075/GB-month effective at your volume)
   • 365-day retention delta ≈ $9,200/mo (approx $0.057/GB-month effective)
- Discount concession: we can meet the 12% discount on add-ons for a 12-month committed path if CloudLytics agrees to a 3-phase revenue ramp (40% / 70% / 100% of committed run-rate across 3 quarters) and net-60 payment terms.
- Dashboard latency SLA: advanced pack guarantees 99.5% availability and median widget render < 600ms for dashboards with up to 20 concurrent active users. We can extend parallelism or a dedicated pool as a paid option if you need sub-250ms guaranteed.

Jordan will be your CS lead on onboarding and will draft the pilot runbook once we have sign-off. If this is acceptable, I'll prepare a formal SOW and an updated invoice template showing the credit application and the discount path.

Kimberly Park
Senior Pricing Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachment: pricing-scenarios-cloudlytics.xlsx (xlsx)

From: Elena Morris <elena@cloudlytics.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: CloudLytics Procurement <procurement@cloudlytics.com>
Date: 2028-06-09T16:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Telemetry add-ons — retention / tracing / dashboards pricing and pilot

Kimberly / Connor — this looks clear, and the pilot credit flexibility is appreciated. Procurement reviewed the revenue ramp and the payment term tradeoff internally. We're prepared to move forward on the 90-day pilot with the $25k pooled credit and the 12% add-on discount under the 12-month commit, subject to: 

1) A 30-day out clause after pilot conversion window (i.e., if our usage deviates >25% you allow a mid-term re-eval)
2) A signed pilot SOW with the onboarding workshop and explicit success metrics (dashboard load <600ms median, trace ingest <30s indexing latency, retention verification checks)

If you can produce a one-page SOW and an updated invoice template that applies the pilot credit as you described, Procurement will turn the PO within 10 business days. Jordan — please share an initial onboarding schedule and acceptance criteria when you can.

Thanks — Elena

Quoted earlier message:
> From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
> Date: 2028-06-08T09:45:00-07:00
> Subject: Re: Telemetry add-ons — retention / tracing / dashboards pricing and pilot
>
> (see attached pricing-scenarios-cloudlytics.xlsx)

Date: 2028-06-07T13:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Telemetry add-ons — retention / tracing / dashboards pricing and pilot

Connor — thanks for the quick turnaround. A couple of clarification notes from procurement/engineering after a quick read: 

- The $25k pilot credit — is that only for add-on charges (retention/tracing/dashboards) or can it be applied to base inference usage as well? Procurement prefers additive credits to be flexible.
- Can you share the expected per-GB delta estimate for our snapshot (we sent ~4TB/month of telemetry currently) — helps procurement model the line item for 90-day retention vs 365-day.
- On discounts: procurement is hoping to see a mid-point option: 12-month commitment at 12% (instead of 10%) in exchange for a revenue commit schedule (monthly ramp up) and a 60-day payment term. Are you able to entertain that concession?

Also, your SLA doc references an uptime objective for the dashboard API — can you confirm latency commitments for dashboard widget load under the advanced pack? Our product team will want that spelled out.

Best,
Elena

Quoted earlier message:
> From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
> Date: 2028-06-07T11:03:00-07:00
> Subject: Re: Telemetry add-ons — retention / tracing / dashboards pricing and pilot
>
> (see attached pricing matrix)

Attachment referenced: redwood-addon-pricing-v2.pdf (pdf)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morris <elena@cloudlytics.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-06-08T09:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Telemetry add-ons — retention / tracing / dashboards pricing and pilot

Elena — thanks, and congrats on the clear ask. I ran the numbers against your 4TB/mo profile and the attached spreadsheet includes two practical scenarios. Key points here: 

- Pilot credit scope: we can allow the $25k pilot credit to be applied across add-ons and base inference consumption (one pool), to make the trial easier from an ops standpoint.
- 90-day vs 365-day estimate (for 4TB/mo):
   • 90-day retention add-on delta ≈ $6,400/mo (approx $0.075/GB-month effective at your volume)
   • 365-day retention delta ≈ $9,200/mo (approx $0.057/GB-month effective)
- Discount concession: we can meet the 12% discount on add-ons for a 12-month committed path if CloudLytics agrees to a 3-phase revenue ramp (40% / 70% / 100% of committed run-rate across 3 quarters) and net-60 payment terms.
- Dashboard latency SLA: advanced pack guarantees 99.5% availability and median widget render < 600ms for dashboards with up to 20 concurrent active users. We can extend parallelism or a dedicated pool as a paid option if you need sub-250ms guaranteed.

Jordan will be your CS lead on onboarding and will draft the pilot runbook once we have sign-off. If this is acceptable, I'll prepare a formal SOW and an updated invoice template showing the credit application and the discount path.

Kimberly Park
Senior Pricing Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachment: pricing-scenarios-cloudlytics.xlsx (xlsx)

From: Elena Morris <elena@cloudlytics.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: CloudLytics Procurement <procurement@cloudlytics.com>
Date: 2028-06-09T16:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Telemetry add-ons — retention / tracing / dashboards pricing and pilot

Kimberly / Connor — this looks clear, and the pilot credit flexibility is appreciated. Procurement reviewed the revenue ramp and the payment term tradeoff internally. We're prepared to move forward on the 90-day pilot with the $25k pooled credit and the 12% add-on discount under the 12-month commit, subject to: 

1) A 30-day out clause after pilot conversion window (i.e., if our usage deviates >25% you allow a mid-term re-eval)
2) A signed pilot SOW with the onboarding workshop and explicit success metrics (dashboard load <600ms median, trace ingest <30s indexing latency, retention verification checks)

If you can produce a one-page SOW and an updated invoice template that applies the pilot credit as you described, Procurement will turn the PO within 10 business days. Jordan — please share an initial onboarding schedule and acceptance criteria when you can.

Thanks — Elena

source.subject: Invoice handoff — canonical CSV ordering & delivery handshake
messages:
From: Elena Marino <elena@lumos-supply.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 4 Jun 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quick question on where to send our invoices (CSV layout attached)

Hi Connor —

We're preparing to switch a batch of our smaller distributors over to your system and wanted to confirm the preferred intake for invoices. Currently we send a weekly consolidated CSV via email (one row per line item) and include PDFs for any exceptions.

Attached is a sample (sample_csv_v1.csv) showing our current column order: invoice_number, invoice_date, po, line_item, qty, unit_price, line_total, tax, currency. A couple of things we want to clarify before we push this to production:

- Do you require a specific column order or headers to guarantee correct PO matching?
- We sometimes include multiple PO numbers on one invoice (comma-separated). Do you prefer one row per PO or one row per line with a PO column that can hold multiple PO IDs?
- Is there an acknowledgement token we should include in the subject/header so you can reconcile our submission with your system?

We can also send via SFTP or your portal if that helps — happy to align.

Thanks — Elena
Elena Marino
Operations Manager, Lumos Supply
Attachments: sample_csv_v1.csv (text/csv)

From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
To: Elena Marino <elena@lumos-supply.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 4 Jun 2028 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick question on where to send our invoices (CSV layout attached)

Hi Elena —

Really appreciate the sample. Short answers up front: portal preferred for daily/near-real-time volumes; email batch is OK for weekly or low-volume suppliers. We have a couple of specific asks to make ingestion reliable:

1) Canonical columns we prefer (order not strictly required but headers must match exactly):
   - invoice_number
   - invoice_date (YYYY-MM-DD)
   - vendor_invoice_id (if different from invoice_number)
   - total_amount (in cents as integer)
   - currency (ISO 4217)
   - po_number (if multiple, semicolon-separated)
   - line_number
   - line_description
   - quantity
   - unit_price (in cents)
   - line_amount (in cents)
   - tax_amount (in cents)
   - tax_code
   - row_hash (MD5 of row for idempotency)

2) For invoices that reference multiple POs we prefer one invoice-level row (summary) and then separate line rows that include the PO reference per line. If you can't produce that, a single line-per-item file with a po_number column (semicolon-separated for multiple POs) will be accepted.

3) Subject token: include header or subject token "Redwood-ACK:<vendor-token>" on the email so our intake can reconcile batches. If you send via portal/SFTP, we can tie by vendor ID instead.

4) File naming: include vendor shortname + date + batch id (e.g., lumos_20280604_b1.csv).

I'm attaching a tiny canonical schema CSV that shows column names we parse (redwood_canonical_schema.csv). Could you try a test file with 3–5 invoices and send it back? Monica will validate the mapping and Jordan will confirm PO matching rules for multi-PO cases.

Thanks — Connor
Connor O'Brien
Senior Procurement Partner, Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood_canonical_schema.csv (text/csv)

From: Elena Marino <elena@lumos-supply.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 5 Jun 2028 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick question on where to send our invoices (CSV layout attached)

On Mon, Jun 4, 2028 at 11:05 AM Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com> wrote:
> Really appreciate the sample. Short answers up front: portal preferred for daily/near-real-time volumes; email batch is OK for weekly or low-volume suppliers. We have a couple of specific asks to make ingestion reliable:

Thanks for the clear guidance — this helps a lot. We're set up to export the line-per-item format fairly easily. A few follow-ups from our side after reviewing your schema:

- We currently send amounts as decimals (e.g., 1234.56). Converting to cents is doable but we'll need a short mapping window.
- For row_hash, do you have a preferred algorithm or salt? We can generate MD5 hashes but want to confirm any salt you expect for idempotency across retries.
- We attach PDFs for any credit/exception lines — will Redwood keep PDF attachments on the record and pass them through to AP reviewers?

Attached is a sample batch (lumos_batch_example.csv) and a representative PDF (invoice_1234.pdf). We'll run a quick conversion to cents and include row_hash per your spec if you confirm hash details.

Best — Elena
Elena Marino
Operations Manager, Lumos Supply
Attachments: lumos_batch_example.csv (text/csv), invoice_1234.pdf (application/pdf)

From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
To: Elena Marino <elena@lumos-supply.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 5 Jun 2028 13:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick question on where to send our invoices (CSV layout attached)

Elena —

This looks great, and the example files are exactly the kind of test we need. A few final clarifications so Monica can sign off:

- row_hash: MD5 is fine. Please compute MD5 over the canonicalized row string (concatenate invoice_number|line_number|total_amount|currency) with no salt for our initial test. If we move to automated retries we'll ask you to include a vendor-salt field.
- PDFs: yes — include PDFs when there are non-standard line items or credits. We'll keep them attached to the invoice record and surface them to AP.
- Multiple PO rules: Jordan confirmed we will accept semicolon-separated PO values in the po_number column for the email batch flow; for portal submissions we prefer explicit PO-item mapping.
- ACK token: please put a short token in the subject like "Redwood-ACK:lumos-202806" and also include an X-Redwood-Ack header with the same token if your mail system supports it. That prevents false positives on retries.
- Currency/amounts: cents as integers is required for automated matching.

Next steps:
1) Send a test file with 3 invoices using the schema in redwood_canonical_schema.csv and subject token "Redwood-ACK:lumos-202806".
2) Monica will validate and reply with a pass/fail and any mapping notes.
3) If all good, we'll enable a weekly-email intake for your vendor id and schedule a short onboarding call to confirm SLA.

Thanks for being flexible — we prefer portal for scale but email batching works fine for this profile.

Connor
Connor O'Brien
Senior Procurement Partner, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference — Procurement Ops
\n

source.subject: Enterprise access onboarding — mutual action plan & SSO/SCIM kickoff
source.messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Wong <alex.wong@infrabank.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 21 May 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Enterprise access onboarding — mutual action plan & SSO/SCIM kickoff

Hi Alex —

Thanks again for the time today. Per our call, I wanted to share a short mutual action plan and clarify the access-control items we'll need to unblock the pilot. Attached: InfraBank_MutualActionPlan_20280521.pdf (high-level milestones + owners).

Priority items for the first 2 weeks:
- SSO/SAML: Metadata exchange + test IdP; we recommend test bind using the RelayState flow.
- SCIM: Confirm core attributes and whether you want group sync (we usually start with users-only).
- IP allowlist: Provide 2-3 CIDR ranges for your egress so we can whitelist.
- Service accounts & org policies: Define initial service account owners and a list of needed API scopes.

Suggested next steps:
1) Please confirm SSO contact + test IdP endpoint (who at InfraBank will run the test).
2) Share a sample user CSV or SCIM attributes if you have a schema preference (I can share a template).
3) Procurement: let me know the PO/contract timeline so we can align the go-live.

I cc'd Marissa (CS lead) and Priyom (implementation) so we can do a direct handoff once you confirm the SSO contact. If it helps, we can do a 30-min kickoff this week — propose Thu 11:00am PT / Fri 9:00am PT.

Best,
Ben
Ben Carter | Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0191

Attachment: InfraBank_MutualActionPlan_20280521.pdf

-----Original Message-----
From: Alex Wong <alex.wong@infrabank.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samira Patel <samira.patel@infrabank.com>, Procurement <procurement@infrabank.com>
Date: Mon, 21 May 2028 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Enterprise access onboarding — mutual action plan & SSO/SCIM kickoff

Ben —

Thanks for the summary and for putting this together. A few quick answers:
- SSO contact: Samira Patel (cc'd) will run the IdP tests. Her team prefers SAML 2.0 with SHA-256 signing.
- SCIM: we want users + department attribute, group sync is optional for phase 1. I'll attach a sample CSV.
- IP allowlist: we need your outbound IP ranges for our security team; they require CIDR ranges and an expected maintenance window for changes.
- Procurement: timeline is ~2-3 weeks; procurement requested that a final MA + SOW be attached to the DocuSign package.

Can you also confirm whether Redwood supports attribute-based group mapping (role -> group) or if that's something we need to handle from the IdP side?

Happy to take a kickoff Thu 11:00am PT.

Thanks,
Alex
Alex Wong | Director of Identity & Access
InfraBank
alex.wong@infrabank.com | +1 (212) 555-0123

Attachment: infra_sample_users.csv

-----Original Message-----
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Wong <alex.wong@infrabank.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Procurement <procurement@infrabank.com>
Date: Tue, 22 May 2028 11:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Enterprise access onboarding — mutual action plan & SSO/SCIM kickoff

Alex — thanks, this is perfect. Quick answers inline and attachments below.

> Can you confirm whether Redwood supports attribute-based group mapping (role -> group) or if that's something we need to handle from the IdP side?

- Service accounts & org policies: Define initial service account owners and a list of needed API scopes.

Suggested next steps:
1) Please confirm SSO contact + test IdP endpoint (who at InfraBank will run the test).

3) Procurement: let me know the PO/contract timeline so we can align the go-live.

I cc'd Marissa (CS lead) and Priyom (implementation) so we can do a direct handoff once you confirm the SSO contact. If it helps, we can do a 30-min kickoff this week — propose Thu 11:00am PT / Fri 9:00am PT.

source.subject: Post-discovery: lock technical runbook session (60-90m) & agenda
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Gomez <alex.gomez@atriahealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 22 May 2028 16:02:00 -0700
Subject: Post-discovery: lock technical runbook session (60-90m) & agenda

Thanks again for the call earlier — good to meet you and the Atria team. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we can get your ML/infra folks in a room with our ops/serving engineers.

Recap (short):
- Main objective: validate serving pattern for patient-screening pipeline (low-latency, inference at up to 15 QPS) and review data flows + security constraints.
- Must-haves: VPC deployment option, KMS integration, retention/audit log detail for compliance team.
- Nice-to-have: warm-cache strategies and batching thresholds to optimize cost.

Proposal: 60–90 minute technical runbook session with our infra engineers + your ML/infra leads. Agenda (draft):
1) 10m — Goals & current infra overview (Atria)
2) 20m — Redwood runtime & architecture mapping to your flow
3) 25–35m — Deep dive: KV cache, batching, quantization tradeoffs
4) 10–15m — Security/compliance: KMS, logs, isolation model
5) 10m — Action items & mutual next steps

Please confirm:
- Preferred duration (60 / 75 / 90 minutes)
- Up to three proposed time windows next week (include timezone)
- Which Atria attendees should be present (titles/roles) — we'll map Redwood roster accordingly.

I'll attach a short runbook stub and a SOC2 summary we often get asked for; happy to add any preread notes you want the team to see before the session.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter | Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 415-555-0182
redwood.ai

Attachments: redwood_tech_runbook_v1.pdf (stub), soc2-summary.pdf (stub)

From: Alex Gomez <alex.gomez@atriahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 23 May 2028 08:11:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-discovery: lock technical runbook session (60-90m) & agenda

Ben — thanks, this is exactly the level we were hoping for. A few quick answers:

1) Duration: 75 minutes sounds right.
2) Availability (PDT): Mon 2028-05-29 09:00–11:00, Tue 2028-05-30 13:00–15:00, Wed 2028-05-31 10:00–12:00. Any of those work for you?
3) Attendees from our side: me (Lead ML Eng), Priya Shah (Infra Lead) <priya.shah@atriahealth.com>, and our security engineer (scope gating) — I'll pull a name after I check with procurement.

We also have a short SOC2 questionnaire that procurement will circulate; I'm attaching it here so you can eyeball any evidence points we might ask for during the session. If you can confirm whether the runbook will include KMS integration patterns and audit log retention examples, that will help Priya prioritize questions.

Small ask: we prefer the call to be recorded and a short transcript available for the compliance folks. Is that OK?

Appreciate it — please lock a time and send a calendar invite.

— Alex
Alex Gomez | Lead ML Engineer, Atria Health
alex.gomez@atriahealth.com

Attachment: atria_soc2_questions.xlsx (procurement stub)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Gomez <alex.gomez@atriahealth.com>, Priya Shah <priya.shah@atriahealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, procurement@atriahealth.com
Date: Thu, 23 May 2028 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-discovery: lock technical runbook session (60-90m) & agenda

Alex — great, locking 75 minutes works. I can take Tue 2028-05-30 at 13:00 PDT (your window) and will send the invite from our calendar.

Quick logistics:
- Scheduled: Tue, 30 May 2028, 13:00–14:15 PDT (75m) — Calendar invite to follow with Zoom link + recording enabled.
- Redwood roster attending: Ben Carter (AE), Lauren Bishop (Solutions Engineer — runtime/quantization), Jordan Blake (Senior Platform Engineer — serving runtime & ops).
- We'll prepare the runbook to include KMS integration patterns, audit-log examples, and recommended retention configurations for regulated PII flows.

I attached a short runbook stub (redwood_tech_runbook_v1.pdf) that maps to the agenda above. If you can confirm the final security attendee name and whether procurement wants any additional artifacts (SAML SSO docs, IP allowlist guide), we'll queue Jordan and Lauren to prepare those sections specifically.

Recording/transcript: yes — we'll enable recording and provide a machine transcript plus brief highlights to make compliance handoff easier.

Next steps for us:
- I'll send the calendar invite and HubSpot update.
- Jordan will prepare a short infra diagram + retention table for the session.
- Lauren will include cost/throughput tradeoff notes keyed to your 15 QPS target.

Thanks — Ben

---- Forwarded message ----
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 22 May 2028 16:02:00 -0700
Subject: Post-discovery: lock technical runbook session (60-90m) & agenda
To: Alex Gomez <alex.gomez@atriahealth.com>

(Original message quoted for reference)

- Main objective: validate serving pattern for patient-screening pipeline (low-latency, inference at up to 15 QPS) and review data flows + security constraints.
- Must-haves: VPC deployment option, KMS integration, retention/audit log detail for compliance team.
- Nice-to-have: warm-cache strategies and batching thresholds to optimize cost.

Proposal: 60–90 minute technical runbook session with our infra engineers + your ML/infra leads. Agenda (draft):
1) 10m — Goals & current infra overview (Atria)
2) 20m — Redwood runtime & architecture mapping to your flow
3) 25–35m — Deep dive: KV cache, batching, quantization tradeoffs
4) 10–15m — Security/compliance: KMS, logs, isolation model
5) 10m — Action items & mutual next steps

Please confirm:
- Preferred duration (60 / 75 / 90 minutes)
- Up to three proposed time windows next week (include timezone)
- Which Atria attendees should be present (titles/roles) — we'll map Redwood roster accordingly.

I'll attach a short runbook stub and a SOC2 summary we often get asked for; happy to add any preread notes you want the team to see before the session.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter | Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 415-555-0182
redwood.ai

Attachments: redwood_tech_runbook_v1.pdf (stub), soc2-summary.pdf (stub)

source.subject: Advanced observability packaging and tiered discount proposal
messages:
From: David Kim <david@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Liu <sarah.liu@nimbushealth.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 01 May 2028 09:11:00 -0700
Subject: Advanced observability packaging and tiered discount proposal

Sarah —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Based on our conversation and the usage profile you shared, I pulled together a proposal for the observability add-ons (retention, tracing, advanced dashboards) that aims to balance predictability for Nimbus while keeping unit economics reasonable for Redwood. Highlights attached (nimbus-obs-pricing-v2.pdf):

- Tiered retention windows: 30/90/365 days mapped to three slab prices with clear volume thresholds.
- Tracing surcharge: metered by ingested spans with a per-million-span band; optional seat-based visualization for heavy dashboard consumers.
- Advanced dashboards: base package (10 dashboards) + incremental seats; includes one-time visualization migration credit for pilot.
- Discount mechanics: commit-based discount schedule (3% / 7% / 12%) tied to annual token/embedding spend; separate add-on credits apply to first 6 months of retention.

Two asks from our side to make a clean commercial:
1) A deployment commitment window (12 months) so we can price the retention slabs without a heavy contingency surcharge.
2) Permission to run a 6-week prefix-caching pilot that reduces KV costs during ramp (we would apply a one-time pilot credit to offset initial ingestion).

If this looks close, we can prepare a short SOW and a redline-friendly term sheet.

Cheers,
David

--
David Kim
Director, Strategic Accounts | Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 415-555-0199

Attachments: nimbus-obs-pricing-v2.pdf (summary table), sow-template.docx (SOW skeleton)

From: Sarah Liu <sarah.liu@nimbushealth.com>
To: David Kim <david@redwood.com>
Cc: Nimbus Health Procurement <procurement@nimbushealth.com>
Date: Wed, 02 May 2028 11:07:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Advanced observability packaging and tiered discount proposal

David —

Thanks for the clear summary and the PDF. Quick initial thoughts after reviewing with procurement and infra: we like the retention slab approach but have specific needs around clinical audit retention (some datasets must be kept 3+ years; other telemetry can be 90 days). Two things we need to understand:

1) Can the 365-day slab be scoped so that only PII-free telemetry lands there (we cannot send raw PHI to host longer than 90 days without additional controls)?
2) On tracing: the per-million-span band looks fine, but we have seasonal burst patterns (end of fiscal quarter) and need a smoothing clause or temporary burst cap rather than immediate overage billing.

Commercial ask from procurement: to get to our CFO, we need a single blended add-on discount rather than a commit ladder — CFO prefers a predictable blended uplift applied to our total MRR. Is Redwood open to a blended discount if we commit to a 12-month spend floor?

If you can sketch the blended math and a burst-smoothing concession, we can move to a draft SOW.

Regards,
Sarah Liu
Head of Platform Engineering, Nimbus Health
Phone: +1 617-555-0112

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: David Kim <david@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 03 May 2028 08:42:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Nimbus asks — blended discount + burst smoothing (internal)

Forwarding Nimbus' notes. My read: CFO wants predictability; procurement wants simplicity. I ran a quick model (see bullets below) and flagged two levers we can offer without eroding margin too much:

- Offer a 10% blended add-on discount if Nimbus signs a 12-month spend floor at 85% of our recommended usage forecast (we'll need HubSpot deal line item to lock it).
- Implement a burst-smoothing policy: allow two burst events per quarter up to 2x baseline without immediate overage; over that, charge 50% of published overage rate for one quarter while we work on shaping.
- For clinical-audit retention: propose a technical control: an isolation namespace for PII datasets with customer-managed encryption + additional logging; price that as a 1.3x multiplier on the 365 slab for those datasets (covers compliance overhead).

I think the pilot credit idea is a good negotiation chip — suggest we offer a one-time 25% ingestion-credit for the first 90 days if they sign the spend floor.

Jordan — can you run margin impact and craft the blended math as a short line item table? I want to keep this succinct for Sarah and procurement.

- Kim

--
Kimberly Park
VP, Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference

From: David Kim <david@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Liu <sarah.liu@nimbushealth.com>, Nimbus Health Procurement <procurement@nimbushealth.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 04 May 2028 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Advanced observability packaging and tiered discount proposal — blended discount and burst smoothing proposal

Sarah —

Appreciate the fast feedback and the procurement perspective. We can absolutely move to the blended route to make CFO life easier. Summary of the commercially actionable proposal below: 

- Blended add-on discount: 10% off observability add-ons (retention + tracing + advanced dashboards) for a 12-month term in exchange for a 85% annual spend floor tied to the forecast in HubSpot (DEAL-4982).
- Burst smoothing: allow two burst events per quarter up to 2x baseline ingestion without charging overage; a third event in a rolling quarter will trigger overage billing at 50% of our standard overage rate for one billing cycle while we coordinate mitigation.
- Clinical audit (PII) retention: support a PII-isolated namespace with customer-managed keys; this will live on the 365 slab but with a 1.3x compliance multiplier.
- Pilot credits: one-time ingestion credit equal to 25% of first 90 days' billed ingestion for retention/tracing as a sign-on concession.

Next steps I propose:
1) If you confirm, we'll prepare a short SOW and a simple term sheet (SOW template attached).
2) Jordan and I will add the blended-line math into the HubSpot deal so procurement can preview the invoice impact.

If this is acceptable, can procurement confirm an internal sign-off timeline so we can align legal?

Thanks,
David

--
David Kim
Director, Strategic Accounts | Redwood Inference
Attachments: sow-template.docx (draft), nimbus-obs-pricing-v2.pdf (updated)

source.subject: Hosted API trial kickoff — endpoints, rates, and quickstart steps
messages:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Sara Lim <sara@clearspring.ai>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 21 Apr 2028 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Hosted API quick trial — endpoint map & next steps

Hi Sara — thanks again for the demo this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we keep momentum: 

1) Quickstart scope (what we propose to provision for Clearspring trial):
   - Hosted sandbox endpoints (streaming + non-streaming)
   - 2 sandbox API keys, 30-day trial credits, and a per-endpoint rate slice for your integration tests
   - Prefix/KV prefetch guidance for conversational flows (we’ll share example configs)

2) Clarifying questions we need from you to size the sandbox properly: 
   - Typical requests/sec during peak and steady state (TPS), and expected burst behavior
   - Median input tokens and expected generated tokens per call (helps pick model/endpoint)
   - Any regulatory constraints (data residency / retention / log access) for test data

3) Proposed next steps / timeline:
   - You share the sample traffic profile (CSV fine) — I’ll pass it to Monica for capacity mapping
   - We provision sandbox + keys within 48 hours of receipt
   - Kickoff call to confirm endpoint mapping and test plan (30 mins)

Attachments: quickstart-map-20280421.pdf (ops map), sample-traffic-profile.csv (blank template)

If this looks good I’ll provision the trial and copy Monica to run the performance mapping. Do you have availability Tuesday or Wednesday for a short kickoff?

Thanks,
Jordan
Jordan Blake
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

From: Sara Lim <sara@clearspring.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 21 Apr 2028 11:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Hosted API quick trial — endpoint map & next steps

Jordan — great recap, thanks. A few answers and one ask from our side: 

- Traffic: steady 5 TPS across our critical endpoint, expected daily spikes up to ~18–22 TPS for ~5–10m windows (campaign bursts).
- Tokens: average payload ~350 input tokens, we expect ~600–900 generated tokens during longer completions.
- Residency: we must keep production data in the US; for the trial we can use synthetic or redacted samples.
- Security: SOC 2 + evidence required; procurement will request the SOC 2 report and our security questionnaire answers before any paid contract.

We’ll complete the sample-traffic-profile.csv and send back by EOD Monday. For kickoff, Tuesday 9:30–10:00 PDT works for our engineering lead (I copied procurement separately).

Also, does the Hosted offering support private VPC peering if we decide to move away from hosted in production? We’d like to understand migration paths (hosted -> dedicated -> private) and what triggers are typical.

Thanks,
Sara Lim
Head of Platform, Clearspring AI
sara@clearspring.ai

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 21 Apr 2028 13:05:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Hosted API quick trial — endpoint map & next steps

Monica/Vanessa — forwarding Sara’s reply (traffic profile + SOC 2 ask). Key asks: 5 TPS steady, spikes to 20 TPS, ~350/800 token profile. They want to know migration path options (hosted -> dedicated -> private) and we’ll need to provide SOC2 evidence for procurement. Can you advise: 

- Recommended sandbox endpoint configuration (model family + streaming vs buffered) given the token profile
- Suggested rate slice to provision in sandbox to mirror production bursts (I’m thinking a baseline 10 TPS with burst to 25 TPS for short windows)
- Expected lead time if they require a dedicated capacity path later (rough calendar days)

I’ve attached my quickstart map and the traffic CSV template they’ll return. Planning to confirm provisioning once Monica signs off.

Thanks — Jordan

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Apr 2028 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Hosted API quick trial — endpoint map & next steps

Thanks for the context. Recommendation based on the sample numbers: 

- Sandbox endpoints: provide one streaming endpoint and one non-streaming endpoint. For Clearspring profile (350 input / 800 output), use a Gen2 medium endpoint (balanced throughput-latency) for initial tests; if they see consistent >10 TPS we upgrade to Gen2 large or shard across two endpoints.
- Rate slice: provision baseline 10 TPS with allowed short-term bursts to 30 TPS (we can enforce burst windows via rate policies). That matches their 20 TPS spikes and gives headroom for tests.
- Model selection: start with an 8-16B open model we’ve validated for streaming; propose quantized variant to lower cost for heavy evals.
- Migration path: Hosted -> Dedicated can be done with a data-plane reservation; typical lead time 2–6 weeks (depending on capacity commitments). Private VPC/on-prem requires security review + deployment package; plan 6–12 weeks for enterprise customers. We’ll surface exact SLA/lead times in a one-pager for procurement.
- SOC2: attach the SOC 2 summary link (I’ll send the signed report through secure portal). For the trial, we can provide redacted evidence and standard questionnaire responses.

If that sounds right I’ll trigger sandbox provisioning once Jordan confirms kickoff time.

Monica Patel
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0199
