From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Li <sarah.li@halcyonfinance.com>, Timothy O'Neill <tim.oneill@halcyonfinance.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Feb 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Region pinning artifacts and evidence handoff

Hi Sarah / Tim,

Thanks again for the conversation today. As discussed, Halcyon's Dedicated tenancy will be pinned to a single AWS region (us-west-2) and you asked for concrete artifacts we can provide to satisfy your compliance checklist around physical boundary, backup scope, and secure deletion.

What we'll provide (next 3 business days unless you ask for expedited delivery):
- An updated region pinning runbook (attached) that captures the tenancy binding, routing rules, and operational controls. (region_pinning_runbook_v2.pdf)
- Network topology diagram showing host aggregates & VPC boundaries (png attached).
- Tenant-level erase/wipe log produced after a test purge (we'll run a scrub and share CSV evidence).
- Snapshot/backup policy excerpt confirming cross-region replication settings and retention windows.

Quick clarifying questions from our side so we prepare exactly what you need:
1) For the tenant wipe evidence, do you need the raw event timestamps + operator id, or an HMAC-signed digest of the log?
2) Any specific retention window you want captured in the runbook (e.g., 7-day snapshots vs 30-day)?
3) Are there any additional third parties (auditors) we should copy on delivery?

I copied Vanessa and Jordan from our Platform Ops & Solutions teams; they'll coordinate the purge and generate the wipe artifact. We'll attach the runbook draft in this thread for your review.

Regards,
Marcus Lin
Solutions Engineering | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.ai
+1 415-555-0182

Attachment: region_pinning_runbook_v2.pdf

From: Sarah Li <sarah.li@halcyonfinance.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Cc: Timothy O'Neill <tim.oneill@halcyonfinance.com>, Halcyon Security <security@halcyonfinance.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Feb 2028 11:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Region pinning artifacts and evidence handoff

Marcus —

Thanks, this covers most of what we need. A couple of quick asks before you run the purge:
- We prefer the wipe log to include both a machine-readable CSV (timestamps, resource ids, operator id) and an SHA256 digest file so we can archive it in our records.
- Snapshot/backup policy: please confirm that cross-region replication is disabled for Halcyon's tenancy and provide the policy excerpt in the runbook.
- Also confirm whether KMS keys for this tenancy are customer-managed or Redwood-managed, and the KMS region.

If you can include the CSV + hash and the runbook update, we can sign off from the security team and close the open item in HS-98765.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

— Sarah Li
Head of Infrastructure Security | Halcyon Finance
sarah.li@halcyonfinance.com

Subject: Re: Region pinning artifacts and evidence handoff — run, purge, and artifacts

Hi Sarah / Tim —

Following up with the items Marcus outlined. We executed a scrub/test purge on a staging copy of Halcyon's tenancy today and produced the artifacts you requested. Summary:

1) Region and isolation:
- Tenancy is anchored to us-west-2 (availability zone spread within that region).
- No compute or persistent volumes for this tenancy are provisioned outside us-west-2; ephemeral caches are ephemeral and cleared on termination.

2) Backups / replication:
- Cross-region replication is disabled for customer snapshots for this tenancy. Automatic snapshots are retained for 7 days by default (configurable per contract).
- KMS: Keys are customer-managed via your supplied KMS key (alias: alias/halcyon-dedicated-usw2). KMS region: us-west-2.

3) Wipe evidence delivered:
- tenant_wipe_log_HAL-20280217.csv (attached) — machine-readable rows with timestampUTC, resource_id, action, operator_id.
- tenant_wipe_log_HAL-20280217.sha256 — sha256 digest of the CSV (attached entry in drive link).
- network_topology_us-west-2.png (attached) — topology showing tenancy VPCs and host aggregates.

Operation notes and controls:
- The purge was run via our Dedicated orchestration path (no human access to raw disks). Operator id in the CSV is an internal ops service account id (svc-dedicated-wipe).
- For greater auditability we can provide a signed attest statement (timestamped) from our Platform Ops team; let us know if you want Redwood's attestation on letterhead.

Next steps we need from Halcyon to finalize the artifact package:
- Confirm you accept the CSV + hash as-is, or request additional fields.
- Confirm the retention window you want reflected in the runbook (we currently document 7-day snapshot retention).
- Indicate whether you need a formal attestation (PDF) or if the drive link + CSV+hash suffice.

Attachments: tenant_wipe_log_HAL-20280217.csv, region_pinning_runbook_v2.pdf (updated minor sections), network_topology_us-west-2.png

Thanks — happy to hop on a short call if you'd like to walk the artifacts.

— Vanessa Ortiz
Platform Operations | Redwood Inference
vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai

source.subject: MSA redline: interim access & emergency relief (TileVan)
messages:
From: Elena Morales <elena.morales@tilevan.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Mark Rivers <mark.rivers@tilevan.com>
Date: 2028-01-14T09:22:00-08:00
Subject: TileVan - redlines re: governing law & injunctive relief

Hi Ava,

Attached is TileVan's redline of the MSA (TileVan_Draft_MSA_redline.docx). Our legal team is taking a firm position on two points that are non-negotiable for our board:

1) Governing law: TileVan requires New York law to govern the agreement.
2) No mandatory arbitration as to claims for injunctive relief — our counsel needs an express carve so we can seek emergency relief in state or federal court in New York.

We are happy to consider arbitration for ordinary disputes, but we need a carveout so that we can preserve the ability to obtain temporary and injunctive relief quickly for IP and data protection issues.

Please confirm whether Redwood can accept New York law for this account or propose an acceptable compromise.

Also attaching a short cover note from our procurement team describing the timeline: they need an executed MSA by 01/26 to meet a launch window.

Thanks,
Elena Morales
Senior Counsel, TileVan
Attachments: TileVan_Draft_MSA_redline.docx
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@tilevan.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-01-14T11:08:00-08:00
Subject: Re: TileVan - redlines re: governing law & injunctive relief

Elena — thanks for the redline and the timeline. We're excited to get TileVan onboard. A few quick thoughts from our side:

- Governing law: Redwood typically asks for Delaware law for corporate predictability and consistency across our contracts. If TileVan insists on New York, we'd like to propose a middle-ground: Delaware law with consent to interim injunctive relief in New York courts (i.e., venue for emergency relief only).

- Arbitration / injunctive carve: We can accept a narrowly tailored carveout that permits either party to seek temporary injunctive relief in court (to preserve evidence or stop immediate harm), provided the carveout is limited to: (a) preservation/forensic actions, (b) immediate stop-the-bleed measures, and (c) not a free-for-all for merits disputes. We would expect those proceedings to be limited in scope and subject to an expedited meet-and-confer protocol.

I've attached a short proposal (RDW-eta-emergency-carve-proposal.docx) with draft language that aims to preserve TileVan's commercial need for quick emergency relief while keeping arbitration as the primary mechanism for final disputes.

If that compromise is acceptable, we can flag limited edits and circulate a clean copy for signatures. If TileVan cannot accept Delaware at all, please confirm and we can escalate internally.

Best,
Ava Chen
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference
Attachments: RDW-eta-emergency-carve-proposal.docx
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-01-15T09:34:00-08:00
Subject: internal: suggested redline language and escalation plan

Team — following Ava's reply to TileVan, I reviewed their redline and our proposed carve. Key points / suggested edits: 

1) Narrow the injunctive carve to "emergency injunctive relief necessary to preserve confidential data, prevent exfiltration, or immediately enjoin use of infringing materials." Avoid broad language like "any equitable relief."

2) Add a 24-hour notice + mandatory teleconference requirement before filing for emergency relief unless notice would frustrate the relief (i.e., ex parte permitted where necessary). This shows good-faith attempt to avoid surprises and is often acceptable to enterprise customers.

3) Limit expedited discovery tied to the injunctive motion: emergency disclosure only as to the threatened harm, with a 7-day production timeline and tightly scoped custodians. 

4) If TileVan flatly refuses Delaware law, propose Delaware law but allow choice of New York for interim relief proceedings (venue-only carve). That preserves Redwood precedents while respecting their preference for NY courts in emergencies.

5) Commercial fallback: if they remain immovable on both governing law and an expansive injunctive carve, we should consider an escalation to the CRO for a deal exception (the launch timing is attractive, but legal exposure needs guardrails).

I'll draft exact contract language for the carveout and the procedural guardrails; Ava, do you want me to send that directly to Elena or should we loop Karthik to soften commercial framing?

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

title: MSA redline: interim access & emergency relief (TileVan)
preview:
From: Elena Morales <elena.morales@tilevan.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Mark Rivers <mark.rivers@tilevan.com>
Date: 2028-01-14T09:22:00-08:00
Subject: TileVan - redlines re: governing law & injunctive relief

Hi Ava,

Attached is TileVan's redline of the MSA (TileVan_Draft_MSA_redline.docx). Our legal team is taking a firm position on two points that are non-negotiable for our board:

1) Governing law: TileVan requires New York law to govern the agreement.
2) No mandatory arbitration as to claims for injunctive relief — our counsel needs an express carve so we can seek emergency relief in state or federal court in New York.

We are happy to consider arbitration for ordinary disputes, but we need a carveout so that we can preserve the ability to obtain temporary and injunctive relief quickly for IP and data protection issues.

Please confirm whether Redwood can accept New York law for this account or propose an acceptable compromise.

Also attaching a short cover note from our procurement team describing the timeline: they need an executed MSA by 01/26 to meet a launch window.

Thanks,
Elena Morales
Senior Counsel, TileVan
Attachments: TileVan_Draft_MSA_redline.docx


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@tilevan.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-01-14T11:08:00-08:00
Subject: Re: TileVan - redlines re: governing law & injunctive relief

Elena — thanks for the redline and the timeline. We're excited to get TileVan onboard. A few quick thoughts from our side:

- Governing law: Redwood typically asks for Delaware law for corporate predictability and consistency across our contracts. If TileVan insists on New York, we'd like to propose a middle-ground: Delaware law with consent to interim injunctive relief in New York courts (i.e., venue for emergency relief only).

- Arbitration / injunctive carve: We can accept a narrowly tailored carveout that permits either party to seek temporary injunctive relief in court (to preserve evidence or stop immediate harm), provided the carveout is limited to: (a) preservation/forensic actions, (b) immediate stop-the-bleed measures, and (c) not a free-for-all for merits disputes. We would expect those proceedings to be limited in scope and subject to an expedited meet-and-confer protocol.

I've attached a short proposal (RDW-eta-emergency-carve-proposal.docx) with draft language that aims to preserve TileVan's commercial need for quick emergency relief while keeping arbitration as the primary mechanism for final disputes.

If that compromise is acceptable, we can flag limited edits and circulate a clean copy for signatures. If TileVan cannot accept Delaware at all, please confirm and we can escalate internally.

Best,
Ava Chen
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference
Attachments: RDW-eta-emergency-carve-proposal.docx


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-01-15T09:34:00-08:00
Subject: internal: suggested redline language and escalation plan

Team — following Ava's reply to TileVan, I reviewed their redline and our proposed carve. Key points / suggested edits: 

1) Narrow the injunctive carve to "emergency injunctive relief necessary to preserve confidential data, prevent exfiltration, or immediately enjoin use of infringing materials." Avoid broad language like "any equitable relief."

2) Add a 24-hour notice + mandatory teleconference requirement before filing for emergency relief unless notice would frustrate the relief (i.e., ex parte permitted where necessary). This shows good-faith attempt to avoid surprises and is often acceptable to enterprise customers.

3) Limit expedited discovery tied to the injunctive motion: emergency disclosure only as to the threatened harm, with a 7-day production timeline and tightly scoped custodians. 

4) If TileVan flatly refuses Delaware law, propose Delaware law but allow choice of New York for interim relief proceedings (venue-only carve). That preserves Redwood precedents while respecting their preference for NY courts in emergencies.

5) Commercial fallback: if they remain immovable on both governing law and an expansive injunctive carve, we should consider an escalation to the CRO for a deal exception (the launch timing is attractive, but legal exposure needs guardrails).

I'll draft exact contract language for the carveout and the procedural guardrails; Ava, do you want me to send that directly to Elena or should we loop Karthik to soften commercial framing?

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference


From: Elena Morales <elena.morales@tilevan.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Mark Rivers <mark.rivers@tilevan.com>, procurement@tilevan.com
Date: 2028-01-16T16:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: TileVan - redlines re: governing law & injunctive relief

Ava / Kimberly — thanks for the quick turn. We reviewed RDW-eta-emergency-carve-proposal.docx with our GC. The compromise is acceptable in principle; the board will only sign off if the carve language explicitly permits emergency relief in New York courts and does not require preliminary arbitration filings before seeking an injunction.

We are comfortable with Delaware law as the governing law for the contract body if the emergency-relief venue is New York and the carve is worded to allow ex parte relief where necessary. One small edit requested: preserve the right to seek interim relief for alleged misappropriation of confidential information without requiring a 24-hour meet-and-confer where notice would prejudice the relief.

Attached is a corrected redline that accepts your edits except for that one change (TileVan_Accepted_Redline.docx). If Redwood can accept that narrowly tailored exception on notice, TileVan can proceed to signature and procurement will issue the PO within 48 hours after countersignature.

Please advise next steps — if you accept, we'll route the countersignature through DocuSign and expedite the PO.

Regards,
Elena Morales
Senior Counsel, TileVan
Attachments: TileVan_Accepted_Redline.docx

source.subject: Sponsor intro & exec brief — slot realignment + evening option
messages:
From: Samantha Cole <samantha.cole@greenline.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: Leo Martinez <leo.martinez@greenline.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Jan 2028 15:42:00 -0500
Subject: Intro + exec demo — Greenline CIO attendance?

Hi Avery,

Thanks for the quick connect yesterday. We're excited to evaluate Redwood for a founder-facing assistant and some internal search use-cases. Our CIO (Marion Patel) is keen to join a short exec briefing so she can evaluate fit, but her schedule next week is tight.

Two windows that might work for Marion:
- Tue 16 Jan, 10:00–10:30 AM ET
- Tue 16 Jan, 7:00–7:30 PM ET (she's traveling but has a gap)

If neither works we'll need a short alternative — she prefers 20–30 minute slots with one technical lead and one product sponsor on the call. Can your team accommodate either time?

Also, please include a short agenda and any pre-read.

Best,
Samantha Cole
Senior Director, Strategic Procurement
Greenline Systems
[phone redacted]

On Thu, Jan 12, 2028 at 3:00 PM Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Samantha — great speaking earlier. Looping in Ben (SE) and Jordan (CS) to keep the exec side tight.
>
> —Avery

From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Cole <samantha.cole@greenline.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Leo Martinez <leo.martinez@greenline.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Jan 2028 17:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Intro + exec demo — Greenline CIO attendance?

Thanks Samantha — appreciate the options. Ben and Jordan: can you join Tuesday for a focused 25-minute exec brief + 10 minute Q&A? We can compress the pitch to a 20-minute highlight + 15 minute discussion if Marion prefers a tighter window.

Proposed standby plan:
- Primary: Tue 16 Jan, 13:00 PT / 16:00 ET — 30 minutes
- Evening backup: Tue 16 Jan, 22:00 PT / 01:00 ET (next day for us) — we know that's late; Samantha, understand if the 7pm ET option is the target instead.

Attaching a one-page agenda and a short exec deck you'll find useful.

Attachments: [Attachment: redwood_demo_agenda.pdf (PDF)] [Attachment: exec-brief-deck.pptx (PPTX)]

Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
avery.johnson@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0183

From: Samantha Cole <samantha.cole@greenline.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: Leo Martinez <leo.martinez@greenline.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 13 Jan 2028 09:21:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Intro + exec demo — Greenline CIO attendance?

Avery — thanks for the options and the materials. Quick update from Marion's EA (cc Leo): she can do the 7:00–7:30 PM ET slot on Tue 16 Jan but only if we keep it to 25 minutes and include a hard leave-behind for procurement (contract/pricing summary + SOC2 highlight).

If your team can compress to 20–25 minutes and confirm the SOC2 highlights, we'll take the 7pm ET slot. Otherwise, she can do the daytime 10am if needed but that's less likely.

Leo — can you drop a calendar hold for 7:00 PM ET if that's what we land on?

Thanks,
Samantha

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Cole <samantha.cole@greenline.com>
Cc: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Leo Martinez <leo.martinez@greenline.com>
Date: Fri, 13 Jan 2028 11:37:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Intro + exec demo — Greenline CIO attendance?

Hi Samantha — Ben here (SE). We can absolutely tighten the run and prioritize the SOC2/high-level compliance slide and the procurement leave-behind. Proposed 22-minute flow:
- 00:00–03:00: Quick intros (Avery + Marion)
- 03:00–12:00: Product highlights focused on latency, private deployment, and SLA options (me)
- 12:00–18:00: How we handle data isolation and KMS/HSM integrations (Jordan covers)
- 18:00–22:00: Next steps + procurement ASK (Avery)

I'll remove demos that add time and we'll deliver the procurement summary as a one-pager in the meeting chat. Confirming we'll include the SOC2 snapshot slide.

Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Cole <samantha.cole@greenline.com>
Cc: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Leo Martinez <leo.martinez@greenline.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Jan 2028 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Intro + exec demo — Greenline CIO attendance?

Thanks all — Jordan here. I'll be the technical backup and will run the KMS/HSM/Private deployment slides to keep the call tight. Avery, if you get pulled last minute I'll lead.

Confirming the final slot: Tue, 16 Jan 2028 at 7:00 PM ET / 4:00 PM PT — 25 minutes. I've created the event and added an agenda item in the calendar note.

Calendar invite: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=gl-demo-20280116

On Mon, Jan 16, 2028 at 12:05 PM Samantha Cole <samantha.cole@greenline.com> wrote:
> Confirmed from Marion's side — we'll be on the 7pm ET slot. Leo: thanks for the hold.
>
> —Samantha

From: Samantha Cole <samantha.cole@greenline.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Leo Martinez <leo.martinez@greenline.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Jan 2028 13:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Intro + exec demo — Greenline CIO attendance?

Perfect — thanks everyone. Two small asks before the call tomorrow night (attached where applicable):
- Please add a one-pager procurement summary to the meeting chat (pricing bands + renewal terms).
- Include a SOC2 snapshot (one slide) and a link to the compliance pack.

I've uploaded our vendor review template here for reference: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1GL-example-agenda

Looking forward to it.

Best,
Samantha Cole
Senior Director, Strategic Procurement
Greenline Systems

source.subject: DPA: controller/processor split, SCCs and proposed transfer addendum
messages:
From: Timothy Rivas <trivas@healthsol.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Dec 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: DPA redline questions and SCCs

Jordan —

Thanks for sending the DPA redline. A few quick clarifying questions from our side before we move to legal tidy-up:

1) Controller/Processor split: section 2(a) in your draft still reads as if HealthSol is controller and Redwood is processor, but our dataflow team flagged a few product features (notably the model fine-tuning pipeline) that might look like co-processing. Can you confirm whether Redwood will accept the processor-only role and the standard processor-delegation language in section 2?

2) SCCs: we need the standard EU Commission SCCs attached as an exhibit. Can Redwood sign with the processor-to-processor fields filled? Any required annotations we should expect?

3) Transfer addendum: our privacy counsel would prefer an explicit transfer addendum that maps hosting regions to subprocessor lists and fallback routing (esp. for EU traffic). Do you have a template or precedent we can start from?

Attached: HealthSol_DPA_redline_v1.pdf — our in-house redlines (high level).

Appreciate a quick reply and, if helpful, we can hop on a 30 min call next week.

Best,
Timothy Rivas
Legal Counsel
HealthSol Inc.

---
Attachment: HealthSol_DPA_redline_v1.pdf (PDF, 135 KB)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
To: Timothy Rivas <trivas@healthsol.com>, Olivia Chen <olivia@healthsol.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Dec 2027 12:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: DPA redline questions and SCCs

Thanks Timothy — good questions. Looping in Ben (Privacy) and Laura (Legal Ops) to confirm specifics and propose edits. High level answers below so you have context before legal reviews: 

- Controller/Processor: Redwood accepts the processor role for hosted API use and will remain a processor for ingestion/generation features. For fine-tuning, we treat model training triggers as a processor activity under our standard security safeguards; Ben, please confirm whether the wording in 2(a) needs tightening or an explicit carveout for training pipelines.

- SCCs: We can accept the EU Commission SCCs as an exhibit. We typically sign with the processor fields completed and include a short annex clarifying subprocessors and lawful transfer mechanisms (SCCs + appropriate technical measures). Laura — will our standard SCC exhibit be sufficient or should we include an alternative Annex I mapping?

- Transfer addendum: We have a transfer-addendum template that enumerates hosting regions, default routing, and a fallback routing policy for outages. Attaching a draft below (Redwood_transfer_addendum_draft.docx). Our suggested approach: attach SCCs as Exhibit A, subprocessor list as Exhibit B (live link), and a short routing policy in Exhibit C.

Would you like us to produce a redline that integrates your HealthSol comments into our template? We can do that and propose a one-hour sync early next week.

Jordan Blake
Head of Sales — Enterprise
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: Redwood_transfer_addendum_draft.docx (DOCX, 24 KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Dec 2027 11:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: DPA redline questions and SCCs — internal comments

Jordan / Laura — quick internal take after reviewing HealthSol's redline: 

1) Controller/Processor: Accept processor stance but add explicit language in §2(a)(ii) clarifying that any model training that results in retention of customer-provided personal data will be performed under processor instructions and customers can opt-out of training. Recommended text: "Processor will only use Customer Data for model training where Customer has expressly authorized such use in writing; Processor shall not otherwise incorporate Customer Data into model weights."

2) SCCs: We should provide the standard SCC annex, filled for processor role. We do not fill in the customer contact fields (left to customer). Also propose to reference our KMS/HSM controls in the technical measures section.

3) Subprocessors & routing: Recommend a short SLA clause that any changes to the subprocessor roster are communicated 30 days in advance and that for EU-origin traffic, Redwood's default hosting is eu-west-1 with fallback to us-west-2 only under emergency failover; include an operational runbook as a linked exhibit.

I attached my annotated redline (Redline_v2_legal_comments.pdf) with inline accept/reject suggestions. If Legal Ops agrees, we can merge into Redwood_transfer_addendum_draft.docx and share back to HealthSol.

Ben Carter
Privacy Lead, Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: Redline_v2_legal_comments.pdf (PDF, 42 KB)

From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: Timothy Rivas <trivas@healthsol.com>, Olivia Chen <olivia@healthsol.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Dec 2027 15:30:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: DPA redline questions and SCCs

Timothy / Olivia — attaching a consolidated draft that (a) incorporates HealthSol's high-level comments, (b) includes Redwood's proposed processor-only language for training opt-ins, and (c) attaches the SCCs as Exhibit A. Highlights below:

- We accepted most of your minor redlines and added an explicit opt-in for training in §4.5.
- Exhibit A: EU Commission SCCs (processor-to-processor filled where applicable).
- Exhibit B: Subprocessor list (live link in Exhibit; snapshot included on signoff).
- Exhibit C: Transfer Addendum — includes region mapping, default routing (eu-west-1), and emergency failover to us-west-2 with customer notification and right to require a fallback to an EU-hosted standby if they pay for the standby capacity.

Next steps proposed: 
1) HealthSol to review the consolidated draft and flag any remaining material asks (e.g., liability caps tied to transfer mechanisms).
2) If acceptable, we prepare the DocuSign packet with Exhibit snapshots and a live subprocessor link.
3) Schedule a 30–45 min legal alignment call to walk SCCs and the failover playbook.

Link to draft: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cHS-TransferAddendumDraft
DocuSign prep link: https://app.docusign.com/prepare/3f4g5h6

Regards,
Laura Bennett
Legal Operations
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: Redwood_consolidated_DPA_redline_20271220.pdf (PDF, 88 KB)

From: Timothy Rivas <trivas@healthsol.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Dec 2027 08:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: DPA redline questions and SCCs

Laura — thanks for the consolidated draft, that helps.

Quick ack: HealthSol legal will review the consolidated redline today. We are fine to sync Monday — can you do Monday 10:00 AM PT for a 30 min alignment on SCCs, the training opt-in language, and the failover playbook?

If you can send a proposed calendar invite with the DocuSign preview and drive link attached, we'll confirm.

Best,
Timothy
Legal Counsel, HealthSol Inc.

---
Attachment: (none)

source.subject: Quick follow-up: Redwood demo + deployment fit
messages:
From: Carmen Lee <carmen.lee@nextech.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.patel@nextech.ai>
Date: 2027-12-05T16:12:00-08:00
Subject: Redwood demo follow-up + a few clarifying Qs

Thanks again for the demo today — quick note while things are fresh. NexTech's engineering lead (Samir) and I discussed the three deployment modes you outlined (Hosted API, Dedicated reserved capacity, Private VPC/on-prem). A few items we need clarified before we can lock in a short trial: 

- Expected QPS for a hosted eval: we want to simulate production burst patterns (10–20 qps sustained with spikes to 80 qps for 2–5 minutes). What rate limits and burst allowances would you expect during a hosted trial?
- Endpoint strategy: can we get separate endpoints per environment (staging/prod) and per model variant, or is routing done via API key + model param?
- Data handling: any persistence or logs kept by Redwood during hosted eval? We need confirmation on log retention and redaction options.
- Quickstart access: our infra team will need a simple runbook/endpoint list, and ideally a sandbox API key with predictable limits.

Also attaching our SOW template and a small network diagram showing that we may want Private later for compliance. Timeline: we want to run an initial hosted trial next week (start 2027-12-08) if we can get the sandbox access + ratecap agreed. Can you confirm?

Thanks,
Carmen
Carmen Lee
Director of Platform Product
NexTech Analytics

Attachment(s): NexTech_SOW_template.pdf, network_diagram.png

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Carmen Lee <carmen.lee@nextech.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-12-05T18:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Redwood demo follow-up + a few clarifying Qs

Carmen — really appreciate the thoughtful write-up and the timeline. Quick recap and answers below; I also included Ben (AE) and Evelyn (Solutions) to scope the trial and Jordan (Support) for the sandbox setup.

Summary answers (high level):
- Hosted eval rate limits: default sandbox policy is 50 qps sustained per org with burst to 120 qps for short durations; we can temporarily raise to 200 qps for the trial window if you share expected traffic windows.
- Endpoints: we provide named endpoints per environment (staging/prod) in Hosted mode. You receive separate API keys scoped to each endpoint; model variant routing is via the endpoint configuration so you can pin a model or swap behind the same URL.
- Data handling: for Hosted evals we persist logs for 30 days by default; we can enable request redaction and restrict logs to metadata only for trial accounts on request (Evelyn will outline options).
- Quickstart: we will provision a sandbox org, two endpoints (staging/prod), and a trial API key. Expect a one-page runbook (auth, endpoints, typical cURL samples) and a checklist from us by EOD tomorrow.

Next steps I propose:
1) Carmen — confirm trial start date (you suggested 2027-12-08).
2) Samir — please share a short traffic profile (expected sustained qps, peak durations, average token length) for the trial period; that will let us tailor the temporary rate raise.
3) Ben/Evelyn/Jordan — I'll kick off provisioning once we have the traffic profile and Carmen's confirmation.

I attached a short quickstart checklist we use internally. If it's helpful, Ben can draft an estimated cost for the trial configuration (so procurement knows potential spend).

Best,
Aditya
Aditya Rao
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Attachment(s): redwood-quickstart-checklist.docx

From: Samir Patel <samir.patel@nextech.ai>

Summary answers (high level):
- Hosted eval rate limits: default sandbox policy is 50 qps sustained per org with burst to 120 qps for short durations; we can temporarily raise to 200 qps for the trial window if you share expected traffic windows.
- Endpoints: we provide named endpoints per environment (staging/prod) in Hosted mode. You receive separate API keys scoped to each endpoint; model variant routing is via the endpoint configuration so you can pin a model or swap behind the same URL.
- Data handling: for Hosted evals we persist logs for 30 days by default; we can enable request redaction and restrict logs to metadata only for trial accounts on request (Evelyn will outline options).
- Quickstart: we will provision a sandbox org, two endpoints (staging/prod), and a trial API key. Expect a one-page runbook (auth, endpoints, typical cURL samples) and a checklist from us by EOD tomorrow.

Next steps I propose:
1) Carmen — confirm trial start date (you suggested 2027-12-08).
2) Samir — please share a short traffic profile (expected sustained qps, peak durations, average token length) for the trial period; that will let us tailor the temporary rate raise.
3) Ben/Evelyn/Jordan — I'll kick off provisioning once we have the traffic profile and Carmen's confirmation.

I attached a short quickstart checklist we use internally. If it's helpful, Ben can draft an estimated cost for the trial configuration (so procurement knows potential spend).

Best,

source.subject: Re: ClarityHealth — post-call quickpack: benchmark links & recommended profiles
messages:
From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
To: Claire Huang <claire.huang@clarityhealth.com>, Carlos Mendez <carlos.m@clarityhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Nov 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quick recap + next artifacts from today’s discovery (models, perf links)

Hi Claire / Carlos — thanks again for the time today. Short recap and the items we promised to share so your engineering and clinical AI teams can triage model fit: 

1) Goal recap — clarify success metrics: throughput goal ~1.5k concurrent chats (peak), P95 latency target <= 450ms, and no regression on chart-extraction F1 (current baseline you mentioned ~0.87).

2) Suggested starting model family — Redwood suggests evaluating two variants: an accuracy-first 13B quantized fp16 path (recommended for clinical extraction quality) and a faster 7B int8 variant for higher TPS. Both are in the attached "redwood_tps-profile.pdf" with Redwood-verified TPS (tokens/sec) and latency percentiles measured on an equivalent GPU profile.

3) Benchmarks and prompt sets — sharing a small prompt-pack (clarity_prompt-pack-v1.jsonl) and a snapshot CSV of our synthetic throughput/latency sweep (benchmark-kpi-snapshot.csv). Links below and attached copies in this thread. These are the same artifacts we use internally to align ops decisions.

4) Quality/regression note — when switching quantization profiles we often see subtle extraction regressions (entity truncation on long notes). Recommended step: run the prompt-pack evaluation and compare the F1 on your ground truth subset before promoting a faster profile. We can help automate this as part of the pilot.

Next steps (suggested):
- You run the prompt-pack against the two model variants (we can provide a small runner).
- We provide a one-hour profiling session with Ben to walk the TPS/latency curves and safe rollout options (canary and fallback to the higher-accuracy model).
- Tentative pilot start: Nov 15 if that aligns with your infra schedule.

Drive + S3 links: https://drive.redwood.com/teams/clarityhealth/benchmarks/2027-11-02 and https://s3.redwood-inference.com/benchmarks/cl-2027/clarity_tps_snapshot.csv

Let me know if you want us to kick off the profiling session this week.

Best,
Avery
Avery Johnson
Senior Solutions — Redwood Inference
avery.johnson@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments:
- redwood_tps-profile.pdf (PDF summary: TPS by model variant, latency P50/P90/P95, GPU config)
- clarity_prompt-pack-v1.jsonl (prompt examples & evaluation schema)
- benchmark-kpi-snapshot.csv (raw sweep results)

From: Claire Huang <claire.huang@clarityhealth.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Nov 2027 17:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick recap + next artifacts from today’s discovery (models, perf links)

Thanks Avery — this is exactly what we needed. A couple quick clarifications: 

- Our infra target is the 1.5k concurrent chat *at P95* (spiky daytime load). Is the 7B int8 profile you mentioned meeting that in your sweep?
- For the quality check, our clinical team will run a 2k-note subset (de-identified) — is that a large enough slice to catch the entity truncation you mentioned?

Also looping Carlos (infra) so he can coordinate GPU sizing. We’d like Ben to run the profiling session with us next Wednesday (Nov 10) if that works on your side.

Appreciate it — Claire
Claire Huang
Director of Clinical NLP — ClarityHealth
claire.huang@clarityhealth.com

On Tue, Nov 2, 2027 at 15:12 Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com> wrote:
> (original message attached)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Claire Huang <claire.huang@clarityhealth.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Carlos Mendez <carlos.m@clarityhealth.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Nov 2027 09:23:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Quick recap + next artifacts from today’s discovery (models, perf links)

Claire — quick follow-up with the measured numbers you asked about. Pulled the rows from the sweep that match your target workload (batching ~8, sequence len 256 typical notes). Summary inlined and raw CSV attached. Full results + runner are on the Drive link.

Summary (7B int8 variant, Redwood-verified):
- Measured steady-state TPS: ~1,900 tokens/sec (approx. 1,450 concurrent chats at avg 1.3 tokens/ms)
- Latency P95: 410ms (P50: 180ms, P90: 360ms)
- 99th: 670ms under synthetic spike (we show fallback path recommendations in the profile)

13B accuracy-first (quantized fp16):
- TPS: ~850 tokens/sec (gives headroom for quality checks)
- Latency P95: 620ms

Notes: the 7B int8 meets your 1.5k P95 target in our baseline sweep. The primary risk is rare degradation on long clinical notes — we saw entity truncation on ~0.8% of synthetic long-form cases using the faster profile (we tracked that in the prompt-pack eval). For the 2k de-identified sample Claire mentioned we recommend running both profiles and comparing extraction F1 before switching live.

Ben will run the profiling session Wed Nov 10 at 10:00 PT (confirmed) — agenda: quick runner walkthrough, live sweep on a small replica, and recommended rollout/autoscale knobs.

--
Ben Carter
Solutions Engineer — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0123

Attachments referenced: benchmark-kpi-snapshot.csv (detailed sweep), redwood_tps-profile.pdf

From: Claire Huang <claire.huang@clarityhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: Carlos Mendez <carlos.m@clarityhealth.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Nov 2027 10:01:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick recap + next artifacts from today’s discovery (models, perf links)

Thanks Ben — that looks promising and aligns with what we hoped. I’ll have our clinical team run the 2k de-id set this week and post back with F1 numbers. Carlos will confirm infra sizing and join the profiling session on Nov 10.

A couple asks before the session:
- Can you include a short checklist for what infra metrics we should watch during the sweep (GPU util, NVMe throughput, queue depth)?
- Any recommended canary rollout thresholds you’ve used for similar customers (esp. when toggling int8 profiles)?

Claire

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Claire Huang <claire.huang@clarityhealth.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 8 Nov 2027 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Quick recap + next artifacts from today’s discovery (models, perf links)

Claire — attached is a short pre-call checklist and a recommended canary playbook snippet I’ll walk through on Nov 10. Key monitoring points (short):
- GPU util: target < 78% steady-state to allow headroom for spikes.
- NVMe: sustained writes for KV cache < 150 MB/s; watch for stalls > 200ms.
- Request queue depth: keep average < 12 per worker process; tail > 25 indicates need to scale.

Canary thresholds we use: start at 5% traffic, monitor extraction F1 delta (stop if drop > 0.02 absolute), P95 latency increase < 10% over baseline, and error rate < 0.2%. If any trigger trips, auto-fallback to 13B accuracy path and scale up dedicated workers.

Also uploaded a small runner and the checklist to the Drive folder: https://drive.redwood.com/teams/clarityhealth/benchmarks/2027-11-02

See you Wed Nov 10 @10:00 PT — Ben, Jordan and I will be on the call.

Ben

On Mon, Nov 8, 2027 at 06:12 Claire Huang <claire.huang@clarityhealth.com> wrote:
> (previous thread)

messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Nov 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: TLS: edge vs client-bridge — DNS delegation & cert ops for private deployment

Liam - thanks for the call earlier. Per Acme's private VPC deployment requirements, we want to nail down where TLS should terminate and how DNS for the private endpoints will be delegated. Attached is a rough architecture diagram (tls-architecture-v2.pdf) and an example CSR template (csr-example.txt).

Key questions to confirm so we can finalize the onboarding checklist:
- Termination point: do you require TLS to be terminated on your LB (customer-edge) for compliance, or can we accept TLS passthrough to Redwood's private bridge where we present (or accept) client certs?
- Client auth: if you prefer passthrough, can your LB forward raw TCP to Redwood, preserving SNI? If not, we need an alternate plan for mutual client TLS.
- DNS delegation: would you be comfortable delegating a subdomain (e.g., redwood-acme.internal.acmehealth) to your VPC resolver or to a delegated zone we manage? Any preference for CNAME vs split-horizon approach?
- Certificate issuance: do you want Redwood to manage certs via ACME (automated) or will you provide signed certs from your PKI? If the latter, we need the signing CA chain and preferred SAN policy (wildcards vs explicit SANs).
- Logging & audit: any specific requirements for TLS session logs, OCSP stapling, or certificate rotation windows?

Proposed next step: short technical sync (30m) this week with your networking and security leads. If you can reply with the preferred termination option and PKI stance, we’ll prepare the CSR mapping and a minimal config diff for your LB.

Thanks,
Ben
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
(Attachment: tls-architecture-v2.pdf; csr-example.txt)

From: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@acmehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: devops@acmehealth.com
Date: Tue, 2 Nov 2027 13:45:00 -0000
Subject: Re: TLS: edge vs client-bridge — DNS delegation & cert ops for private deployment

Ben — appreciate the diagram. Short answers from our side: we have a strict policy that TLS must terminate at the Acme edge for PHI-scoped traffic; that said, for lower-sensitivity endpoints we can consider passthrough to Redwood if the TCP path preserves SNI and client IP.

Our PKI: we operate an internal CA (vault-backed) and generally prefer signing CSRs ourselves. We can provide an intermediate CA chain and support short-lived certs (90 days max). OCSP is required for external-facing certs; for private endpoints we can accept OCSP stapling from Redwood, but we need a copy of the OCSP responder URL and proof of stapling in the first 3 rotations.

DNS: we can delegate a narrow subdomain to our resolver (delegate.redwood.acme.internal) or conversely accept a CNAME into our internal zone. Split-horizon is preferred so external records are not leaked.

We'll need an explicit list of FQDNs you plan to present (by env: staging/prod) so our security team can pre-authorize SANs.

Liam
Head of Network Security, Acme Health
(Attachment: none)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Nov 2027 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: TLS: edge vs client-bridge — DNS delegation & cert ops for private deployment

Quoting Liam's note above — a few technical follow-ups from infra: 

1) If Acme terminates TLS at their LB, we need a secure, authenticated backend channel (mTLS) between their LB and our private runtime. Can their LB present client certs to the Redwood bridge, or do we need to configure an IP allowlist + mutual-TLS?

2) SNI mapping: to preserve tenant routing we prefer a per-tenant FQDN (tenantX.redwood-acme.internal). Wildcards make routing harder for metrics and isolation; do they have a policy against issuing wildcards? If they can only sign wildcards from their CA, we'll need explicit SAN lists per tenant.

3) OCSP & stapling: we can staple OCSP from our side if Redwood holds the leaf certs, but if Acme controls issuance we need a test vector to validate stapling on production TLS handshakes. Please confirm their OCSP responder URL and whether they support stapling for internally-signed certs.

4) Rotation windows: propose 30-day staggered rotations for our presented certs so there's no mass expiry across tenant endpoints. Will their CA support automated renewal via an API, or will renewals be a manual CSR process?

I'll pull together a small matrix (mutual-tls-mapping.csv) that maps termination option -> required artifacts.

—Jordan
Infra Lead, Redwood Inference
(Attachment: mutual-tls-mapping.csv)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Nov 2027 14:20:00 -0800
Subject: Re: TLS: edge vs client-bridge — DNS delegation & cert ops for private deployment

Adding product/ops perspective: 

- Operational impact: if Acme insists on edge termination, our observability changes — we will not see token-level latency post-termination and will rely on their LB logs for request tracing. We'll need inbound logging hooks (structured) from Acme to map to Redwood request ids.

- SLA/Failover: confirm whether Acme will accept us failing back to a regional hosted fallback (non-private) in the event of VPC connectivity loss. This affects our continuity plan and whether we include a short TTL (60s) on delegated records.

- Compliance: Karthik, please confirm whether an internal intermediate CA (provided by Acme) requires HSM-backed key storage for us to accept their signed certs under our policy. If so, we'll need their crypto attestation.

Marissa
Director, Product Operations, Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 4 Nov 2027 16:40:00 -0800
Subject: Re: TLS: edge vs client-bridge — DNS delegation & cert ops for private deployment

Thanks everyone — summary and action items: 

1) Acme prefers edge termination for PHI-scoped endpoints; for non-PHI we may use TCP passthrough if SNI preserved. Liam to confirm list of PHI-scoped FQDNs by EOD Friday. (Owner: Liam)

2) PKI approach: Acme will sign CSRs using their internal CA and provide an intermediate chain. They will support 90-day certs; we request they expose an API or a documented manual renewal flow. (Owner: Acme + Karthik for policy confirmation)

3) DNS: Acme to delegate delegate.redwood.acme.internal via split-horizon. Redwood to provide initial FQDN list for staging/prod. (Owner: Ben)

4) OCSP and stapling: Acme to provide OCSP responder URL and test vector; Redwood will validate stapling in staging before prod cutover. (Owner: Jordan + Liam)

5) Ops & logging: Acme to share LB structured logs schema and sampling for request-id correlation. Redwood will prepare a minimal logging adapter. (Owner: Marissa + Acme DevOps)

Next step: schedule 30-minute technical sync Thursday 11/6 at 14:00 PT to walk through the CSR fields and DNS delegation steps. I've created a Jira ticket and uploaded the diagram/CSR template to the Drive folder (links below).

Links & artifacts:
- Diagram & CSR: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cACME-Private-Deploy
- Jira ticket: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/RI-ACME-8243

Attachments: tls-architecture-v2.pdf; csr-example.txt; mutual-tls-mapping.csv

Ben
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

Date: Tue, 2 Nov 2027 13:45:00 -0000
Subject: Re: TLS: edge vs client-bridge — DNS delegation & cert ops for private deployment

Ben — appreciate the diagram. Short answers from our side: we have a strict policy that TLS must terminate at the Acme edge for PHI-scoped traffic; that said, for lower-sensitivity endpoints we can consider passthrough to Redwood if the TCP path preserves SNI and client IP.

Our PKI: we operate an internal CA (vault-backed) and generally prefer signing CSRs ourselves. We can provide an intermediate CA chain and support short-lived certs (90 days max). OCSP is required for external-facing certs; for private endpoints we can accept OCSP stapling from Redwood, but we need a copy of the OCSP responder URL and proof of stapling in the first 3 rotations.

DNS: we can delegate a narrow subdomain to our resolver (delegate.redwood.acme.internal) or conversely accept a CNAME into our internal zone. Split-horizon is preferred so external records are not leaked.

We'll need an explicit list of FQDNs you plan to present (by env: staging/prod) so our security team can pre-authorize SANs.

Liam
Head of Network Security, Acme Health
(Attachment: none)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Nov 2027 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: TLS: edge vs client-bridge — DNS delegation & cert ops for private deployment

Quoting Liam's note above — a few technical follow-ups from infra: 

1) If Acme terminates TLS at their LB, we need a secure, authenticated backend channel (mTLS) between their LB and our private runtime. Can their LB present client certs to the Redwood bridge, or do we need to configure an IP allowlist + mutual-TLS?

2) SNI mapping: to preserve tenant routing we prefer a per-tenant FQDN (tenantX.redwood-acme.internal). Wildcards make routing harder for metrics and isolation; do they have a policy against issuing wildcards? If they can only sign wildcards from their CA, we'll need explicit SAN lists per tenant.

3) OCSP & stapling: we can staple OCSP from our side if Redwood holds the leaf certs, but if Acme controls issuance we need a test vector to validate stapling on production TLS handshakes. Please confirm their OCSP responder URL and whether they support stapling for internally-signed certs.

4) Rotation windows: propose 30-day staggered rotations for our presented certs so there's no mass expiry across tenant endpoints. Will their CA support automated renewal via an API, or will renewals be a manual CSR process?

I'll pull together a small matrix (mutual-tls-mapping.csv) that maps termination option -> required artifacts.

—Jordan
Infra Lead, Redwood Inference
(Attachment: mutual-tls-mapping.csv)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Nov 2027 14:20:00 -0800
Subject: Re: TLS: edge vs client-bridge — DNS delegation & cert ops for private deployment

Adding product/ops perspective: 

- Operational impact: if Acme insists on edge termination, our observability changes — we will not see token-level latency post-termination and will rely on their LB logs for request tracing. We'll need inbound logging hooks (structured) from Acme to map to Redwood request ids.

- SLA/Failover: confirm whether Acme will accept us failing back to a regional hosted fallback (non-private) in the event of VPC connectivity loss. This affects our continuity plan and whether we include a short TTL (60s) on delegated records.

- Compliance: Karthik, please confirm whether an internal intermediate CA (provided by Acme) requires HSM-backed key storage for us to accept their signed certs under our policy. If so, we'll need their crypto attestation.

Marissa
Director, Product Operations, Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 4 Nov 2027 16:40:00 -0800
Subject: Re: TLS: edge vs client-bridge — DNS delegation & cert ops for private deployment

Thanks everyone — summary and action items: 

1) Acme prefers edge termination for PHI-scoped endpoints; for non-PHI we may use TCP passthrough if SNI preserved. Liam to confirm list of PHI-scoped FQDNs by EOD Friday. (Owner: Liam)

2) PKI approach: Acme will sign CSRs using their internal CA and provide an intermediate chain. They will support 90-day certs; we request they expose an API or a documented manual renewal flow. (Owner: Acme + Karthik for policy confirmation)

3) DNS: Acme to delegate delegate.redwood.acme.internal via split-horizon. Redwood to provide initial FQDN list for staging/prod. (Owner: Ben)

4) OCSP and stapling: Acme to provide OCSP responder URL and test vector; Redwood will validate stapling in staging before prod cutover. (Owner: Jordan + Liam)

5) Ops & logging: Acme to share LB structured logs schema and sampling for request-id correlation. Redwood will prepare a minimal logging adapter. (Owner: Marissa + Acme DevOps)

Next step: schedule 30-minute technical sync Thursday 11/6 at 14:00 PT to walk through the CSR fields and DNS delegation steps. I've created a Jira ticket and uploaded the diagram/CSR template to the Drive folder (links below).

Links & artifacts:
- Diagram & CSR: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cACME-Private-Deploy
- Jira ticket: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/RI-ACME-8243

Attachments: tls-architecture-v2.pdf; csr-example.txt; mutual-tls-mapping.csv

Ben
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

messages:
From: Priya Shah <security@luminarahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aaron Park <aaron.park@luminarahealth.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Nov 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: CAIQ intake + evidence window request

Hi Ben,

Thanks for helping coordinate — we (Luminara Health) are kicking off our vendor CAIQ and SOC2 intake for the upcoming audit window. Can we schedule a 45-minute technical intake to review the CAIQ you've filled, confirm evidence handoff cadence, and validate encryption key ownership?

High-level asks for that call:
- Walkthrough of your completed CAIQ (we expect the filled worksheet).
- Sample logs for authentication and admin activity (last 90 days preferred).
- Confirmation of KMS/HSM usage and the person/team responsible for key custody.
- Retention/archival policy artifacts and egress controls.

We have availability Wed 11/3 10–12 PST or Thu 11/4 08–11 PST. If those don't work, propose alternatives. I attached a short intake checklist (see attachment: vendor-intake-checklist.pdf).

Priya Shah
Security Operations Lead
Luminara Health
security@luminarahealth.com

--
Attachment: vendor-intake-checklist.pdf (1 page PDF)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Shah <security@luminarahealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Nov 2027 17:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: CAIQ intake + evidence window request

Thanks Priya — helpful checklist. Wed 11/3 10:30 PST works for us; marking 45 minutes. Invites to follow.

Quick answers and artifacts I can attach ahead of the call:
- CAIQ (we'll push CAIQ_v2_filled.xlsx to the Drive link and attach a copy here).
- Log sampling: I'll include a tarball with anonymized auth/admin samples (log-samples.tar.gz).
- KMS/HSM: we use AWS KMS in a customer-isolated CMK configuration for hosted customers; I will route a short owner confirmation before the call.

Marissa will cover dataflow and log locations on the call; Jordan is the owner for key custody and will join to confirm access matrix.

Action items before Wed 10:30 PST:
1) Ben: upload CAIQ_v2_filled.xlsx and log-samples.tar.gz to shared Drive and attach.
2) Marissa: draft short notes on log retention and archival policy.
3) Jordan: confirm KMS usage and prepare encryption-key-access-matrix.pdf.

Ben Carter
Senior Account Exec | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

--
Attachments: CAIQ_v2_filled.xlsx; log-samples.tar.gz

From: Priya Shah <security@luminarahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aaron Park <aaron.park@luminarahealth.com>
Date: Tue, 02 Nov 2027 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: CAIQ intake + evidence window request

Ben — confirmed for Wed 11/3 10:30 PST.

Two quick clarifications before the call so we can use time efficiently:
1) For KMS/HSM, can you confirm whether the CMK is customer-managed or Redwood-managed for dedicated deployments? If customer-managed, we will want a brief runbook on rotation and access revocation.
2) For the log samples: please provide a README in the tarball describing which systems the samples map to (authN, admin, inference runtime) and the sampling window.

If procurement will need an updated MSA addendum for logging/retention, cc'ing Aaron so we can loop procurement in early.

Thanks,
Priya

On Mon, Nov 1, 2027 at 5:18 PM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Thanks Priya — helpful checklist. Wed 11/3 10:30 PST works for us; marking 45 minutes. Invites to follow.
> 
> Quick answers and artifacts I can attach ahead of the call: ...

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Shah <security@luminarahealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 02 Nov 2027 12:46:00 -0700
Subject: Re: CAIQ intake + evidence window request

Hi Priya / Ben —

Thanks for lining this up. A few prep notes so the call is productive:

Summary answers (short):
- KMS ownership: For Hosted API customers, Redwood manages the CMKs (we rotate per policy). For Dedicated and Private deployments, customers can supply/bring their CMK (customer-managed). Jordan (cc'd) owns the internal process and will confirm the specific deployment type for Luminara.
- Logs: We will provide anonymized samples for: auth (login/fail), admin actions (role changes, policy edits), and inference runtime (request traces). README will map file names to component.
- Retention: Default retention for auth/admin logs is 365 days in our analytics store; longer-term archival is available via S3 export upon request and documented in the attached retention_note.pdf.

Pre-call attachments (shared to the Drive link + attached):
- encryption-key-access-matrix.pdf — matrix of roles with KMS access and emergency contacts.
- CAIQ_v2_filled.xlsx — our completed CAIQ responses (redacted where necessary).
- log-samples.tar.gz (README included inside).

Suggested call agenda (45m):
1) 5m intro + scope confirmation
2) 15m CAIQ walkthrough (Ben/Marissa)
3) 10m KMS/HSM + key custody (Jordan)
4) 10m Logs + retention + sampling (Marissa)
5) 5m next steps / procurement asks

If you want, I can drop a short runbook excerpt showing rotation cadence and revocation steps into the Drive before the call.

Marissa Cole
Security Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com

--
Attachments: encryption-key-access-matrix.pdf

From: Aaron Park <aaron.park@luminarahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Shah <security@luminarahealth.com>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@luminarahealth.com>
Date: Wed, 03 Nov 2027 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Procurement note + quick confirmation before technical call

Hi all — looping in procurement formally. A couple quick procurement items to confirm so the technical discussion aligns with contracting:
- We may need a short MSA addendum referencing log retention SLAs and encrypted key custody for dedicated deployments. Is Redwood able to provide standard language for retention/export obligations?
- Will logs contain any PII in the samples you plan to provide? If so, we need that noted in the README and redaction steps documented.

Ben — can you confirm you'll share the Drive link and CAIQ copy by EOD today so procurement has what they need for preliminary review?

Appreciate it,
Aaron Park
Senior Sourcing Manager | Luminara Health
aaron.park@luminarahealth.com

On Tue, Nov 2, 2027 at 12:46 PM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Hi Priya / Ben —
> 
> Thanks for lining this up. A few prep notes so the call is productive: ...

--
Ben Carter
Senior Account Exec | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com

Final notes: Drive link + HubSpot record attached in thread; call invite sent for Wed 11/3 10:30 PST.

source.subject: VistaEdge: Architecture & Control Evidence Request
messages:
From: Alice Hunt <alice.hunt@vistaedge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood-inference.com>
Date: 2027-11-01T16:12:00Z
Subject: Request: architecture controls & evidence for SOC 2 / ISO mapping

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week — VistaEdge is moving quickly on the procurement side. Our InfoSec team is collecting product-level artifacts to complete our vendor risk assessment. We need the following by EOD Friday (2027-11-05):

- Control crosswalk mapping to SOC 2 and ISO 27001 (control IDs + short mapping notes)
- Latest SOC 2 report or bridge letter (PDF)
- High-level production network diagram and encryption at-rest/in-transit notes
- Any compensating/control design notes for external access and key management

Can you confirm you can share these under an NDA? If helpful, I attached a short intake template.

Attachments: vista_request_summary.pdf (application/pdf)

Thanks,
Alice Hunt
Senior Information Security Analyst
VistaEdge Health
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood-inference.com>
To: Alice Hunt <alice.hunt@vistaedge.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood-inference.com>
Date: 2027-11-01T17:03:00Z
Subject: Re: Request: architecture controls & evidence for SOC 2 / ISO mapping

Hi Alice — appreciate the clear list. We can absolutely assemble this and share under NDA.

Quick plan:

source.subject: Quick sync: usage surge & 30‑min walkthrough for BrightPath
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sara Nguyen <sara@brightpath.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-10-03T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Quick sync: usage surge & 30‑min walkthrough for BrightPath

Hi Sara —

Congrats on the uptick — I saw BrightPath crossed the 250k token threshold yesterday from the self-serve project. That’s a great signal you might benefit from a short walkthrough to review optimization levers (batching, prefix caching, and a quick quantization tradeoff we’ve seen help similar customers).

Would you be open to a 30‑minute call this week? I’m proposing two slots:
- Thu 10/07 10:00–10:30 PT
- Fri 10/08 14:00–14:30 PT

If neither works, feel free to pick a time on my calendar: https://calendly.com/redwood-demo/30min

I attached a one‑pager and a suggested agenda to make the call concrete. On the call we can: 
- review your current cost/latency profile
- show quick runtime knobs you can flip today
- talk about Dedicated vs sticking with self‑serve if you expect sustained growth

Best,
Ben Carter | SDR (Mid‑Market)
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555‑0128

Attachments: redwood-quickstart.pdf, proposed-agenda.txt

From: Sara Nguyen <sara@brightpath.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-10-03T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Quick sync: usage surge & 30‑min walkthrough for BrightPath

Ben —

Thanks for reaching out and noticing the spike. We did some experiments last week and are seeing both increased prompts and longer responses, so curious to learn quick wins. Friday 10/08 14:00 PT works for me. Can we include one of our infra engineers (I’ll cc infra@brightpath.ai) in case we need to talk about our VPC/network setup?

Also FYI — we have a short procurement window for reserved capacity next quarter; I’d love a sense of cost delta if we move to Dedicated.

Thanks,
Sara Nguyen
Head of ML Platform, BrightPath AI

sara@brightpath.ai

> Ben

Perfect — thanks Sara. I’ve scheduled Fri 10/08 14:00–14:30 PT and sent a Google Meet invite. Marissa, can you drop the one‑pager in the calendar invite and include a short list of sample prompts to bring on the call?

Ben —

Apologies for the shuffle — appreciate you being flexible.

title: Quick sync: usage surge & 30‑min walkthrough for BrightPath
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sara Nguyen <sara@brightpath.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-10-03T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Quick sync: usage surge & 30‑min walkthrough for BrightPath

Hi Sara —

Congrats on the uptick — I saw BrightPath crossed the 250k token threshold yesterday from the self-serve project. That’s a great signal you might benefit from a short walkthrough to review optimization levers (batching, prefix caching, and a quick quantization tradeoff we’ve seen help similar customers).

Would you be open to a 30‑minute call this week? I’m proposing two slots:
- Thu 10/07 10:00–10:30 PT
- Fri 10/08 14:00–14:30 PT

If neither works, feel free to pick a time on my calendar: https://calendly.com/redwood-demo/30min

I attached a one‑pager and a suggested agenda to make the call concrete. On the call we can: 
- review your current cost/latency profile
- show quick runtime knobs you can flip today
- talk about Dedicated vs sticking with self‑serve if you expect sustained growth

Best,
Ben Carter | SDR (Mid‑Market)
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555‑0128

Attachments: redwood-quickstart.pdf, proposed-agenda.txt

source.subject: Fwd: Onboarding invite link truncated when forwarded
messages:
From: ProcureCo Accounts Payable <ap@procureco.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-10-04T08:12:00-07:00
Subject: Onboarding invite - link appears truncated when forwarded

Hi Ben,

We received the supplier portal invite this morning but when our AP rep forwards the invite to the team the activation link gets cut off. Attached is a screenshot showing the email as it appears in Outlook (line wrap / truncation). We tried copying the link out of the email but the trailing query string is missing, which results in an error page on acceptance.

Attached: screenshot-truncated-link.png and our onboarding checklist (procureco-onboarding-plan.xlsx).

Can you confirm whether the invite URL is being wrapped or encoded in a way that breaks when forwarded? We're stuck on the PO submission step until the vendor can accept.

Thanks,
Tasha Lin
Accounts Payable
ProcureCo

----
ProcureCo Accounts Payable <ap@procureco.com>
Attachment: screenshot-truncated-link.png (image/png)
Attachment: procureco-onboarding-plan.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: ProcureCo Accounts Payable <ap@procureco.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-10-04T09:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Onboarding invite - link appears truncated when forwarded

Hi Tasha — thanks for the screenshot and the checklist. Sorry for the friction here.

Short answer: we have seen cases where mail clients (Outlook in particular) insert soft line breaks or hide long query strings when messages are forwarded, and that can strip the token portion at the end of our one-time acceptance link.

Immediate workarounds you can try:
- Instead of forwarding the original message, open the invite and click "Copy link" (if present) and paste into a new message.
- Ask the recipient to right-click the activation button and choose "Copy link address"—that usually preserves the full URL.
- We can also generate a replacement invite for the vendor contact directly from our portal (short-lived). If you want that, confirm the vendor contact email for the vendor account and I'll reissue.

I'm looping in Laura (AE) to confirm we can reissue a fresh invite and coordinate with your AP team so PO submission can proceed.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0183

> Hi Ben,
> 
> We received the supplier portal invite this morning but when our AP rep forwards the invite to the team the activation link gets cut off. Attached is a screenshot showing the email as it appears in Outlook (line wrap / truncation). >
> Attached: screenshot-truncated-link.png and our onboarding checklist (procureco-onboarding-plan.xlsx).
>
> Can you confirm whether the invite URL is being wrapped or encoded in a way that breaks when forwarded? We're stuck on the PO submission step until the vendor can accept.
>
> Thanks,
> Tasha Lin
> Accounts Payable
> ProcureCo

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, ProcureCo Accounts Payable <ap@procureco.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-10-04T10:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Onboarding invite - link appears truncated when forwarded

Thanks Ben — adding Jordan from product so we can get an engineering-verified workaround if reissuing isn't ideal.

Tasha, quick clarifying Qs:
- Which vendor record is this for? (company name + primary contact email)
- Did the vendor try the link in a browser (paste into address bar) and see an error page or a 400/invalid token message?

If you prefer, Jordan can SSH into our staging logs and confirm whether the token was received by our acceptance endpoint and where it was cut off. If the token is missing on our side, we should reissue.

Laura Bennett
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com

On Oct 4, 2027, at 09:05, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Tasha — thanks for the screenshot and the checklist. Sorry for the friction here.
> 
> Short answer: we have seen cases where mail clients (Outlook in particular) insert soft line breaks or hide long query strings when messages are forwarded, and that can strip the token portion at the end of our one-time acceptance link.
>
> Immediate workarounds you can try:
> - Instead of forwarding the original message, open the invite and click "Copy link" (if present) and paste into a new message.
> - Ask the recipient to right-click the activation button and choose "Copy link address"—that usually preserves the full URL.
> - We can also generate a replacement invite for the vendor contact directly from our portal (short-lived). If you want that, confirm the vendor contact email for the vendor account and I'll reissue.
>
> I'm looping in Laura (AE) to confirm we can reissue a fresh invite and coordinate with your AP team so PO submission can proceed.

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, ProcureCo Accounts Payable <ap@procureco.com>
Date: 2027-10-04T13:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Onboarding invite - link appears truncated when forwarded

Hi all — dug into the logs for the token in question. Summary: the acceptance token was generated and delivered intact from our SMTP relay. The mail client (Outlook web client / desktop) appears to be wrapping the href when the message is forwarded, converting the long query string into a line-broken fragment. In our case the token portion (query param "t=") is sometimes pushed to a new line and Outlook's forwarded message view inserts a hard ellipsis in the visible text.

What we can do immediately:
1) I can issue a new invite for the vendor with a shorter one-time token (shorter query params) which reduces chance of wrapping — validation TTL stays the same.
2) As a safer long-term fix, we'll update the invite email template to put the acceptance URL into a single <a href> button that also prints the full link in a preformatted code block underneath (so copy/paste preserves full URL even when forwarded). We'll aim to ship the template change into Prod next week; I'll add it to SUP-5921.

Tasha — if you confirm the vendor record:
Vendor name: Novacraft Supply
Primary contact: elena@novacraft.com
we'll reissue now and I will monitor the acceptance attempt in real time. If Novacraft still hits the error please forward the acceptance failure page screenshot and I'll capture the request/response trace.

- Jordan
Principal Product Engineer | Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com

On Oct 4, 2027, at 10:18, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks Ben — adding Jordan from product so we can get an engineering-verified workaround if reissuing isn't ideal.
> 
> Tasha, quick clarifying Qs:
> - Which vendor record is this for? (company name + primary contact email)
> - Did the vendor try the link in a browser (paste into address bar) and see an error page or a 400/invalid token message?
> 
> If you prefer, Jordan can SSH into our staging logs and confirm whether the token was received by our acceptance endpoint and where it was cut off. If the token is missing on our side, we should reissue.
