> Attached: screenshot-truncated-link.png and our onboarding checklist (procureco-onboarding-plan.xlsx).
>
> Can you confirm whether the invite URL is being wrapped or encoded in a way that breaks when forwarded? We're stuck on the PO submission step until the vendor can accept.
>
> Thanks,
> Tasha Lin
> Accounts Payable
> ProcureCo

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, ProcureCo Accounts Payable <ap@procureco.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-10-04T10:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Onboarding invite - link appears truncated when forwarded

Thanks Ben — adding Jordan from product so we can get an engineering-verified workaround if reissuing isn't ideal.

Tasha, quick clarifying Qs:
- Which vendor record is this for? (company name + primary contact email)
- Did the vendor try the link in a browser (paste into address bar) and see an error page or a 400/invalid token message?

If you prefer, Jordan can SSH into our staging logs and confirm whether the token was received by our acceptance endpoint and where it was cut off. If the token is missing on our side, we should reissue.

Laura Bennett
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com

On Oct 4, 2027, at 09:05, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Tasha — thanks for the screenshot and the checklist. Sorry for the friction here.
> 
> Short answer: we have seen cases where mail clients (Outlook in particular) insert soft line breaks or hide long query strings when messages are forwarded, and that can strip the token portion at the end of our one-time acceptance link.
>
> Immediate workarounds you can try:
> - Instead of forwarding the original message, open the invite and click "Copy link" (if present) and paste into a new message.
> - Ask the recipient to right-click the activation button and choose "Copy link address"—that usually preserves the full URL.
> - We can also generate a replacement invite for the vendor contact directly from our portal (short-lived). If you want that, confirm the vendor contact email for the vendor account and I'll reissue.
>
> I'm looping in Laura (AE) to confirm we can reissue a fresh invite and coordinate with your AP team so PO submission can proceed.

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, ProcureCo Accounts Payable <ap@procureco.com>
Date: 2027-10-04T13:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Onboarding invite - link appears truncated when forwarded

Hi all — dug into the logs for the token in question. Summary: the acceptance token was generated and delivered intact from our SMTP relay. The mail client (Outlook web client / desktop) appears to be wrapping the href when the message is forwarded, converting the long query string into a line-broken fragment. In our case the token portion (query param "t=") is sometimes pushed to a new line and Outlook's forwarded message view inserts a hard ellipsis in the visible text.

What we can do immediately:
1) I can issue a new invite for the vendor with a shorter one-time token (shorter query params) which reduces chance of wrapping — validation TTL stays the same.
2) As a safer long-term fix, we'll update the invite email template to put the acceptance URL into a single <a href> button that also prints the full link in a preformatted code block underneath (so copy/paste preserves full URL even when forwarded). We'll aim to ship the template change into Prod next week; I'll add it to SUP-5921.

Tasha — if you confirm the vendor record:
Vendor name: Novacraft Supply
Primary contact: elena@novacraft.com
we'll reissue now and I will monitor the acceptance attempt in real time. If Novacraft still hits the error please forward the acceptance failure page screenshot and I'll capture the request/response trace.

- Jordan
Principal Product Engineer | Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com

On Oct 4, 2027, at 10:18, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks Ben — adding Jordan from product so we can get an engineering-verified workaround if reissuing isn't ideal.
> 
> Tasha, quick clarifying Qs:
> - Which vendor record is this for? (company name + primary contact email)
> - Did the vendor try the link in a browser (paste into address bar) and see an error page or a 400/invalid token message?
> 
> If you prefer, Jordan can SSH into our staging logs and confirm whether the token was received by our acceptance endpoint and where it was cut off. If the token is missing on our side, we should reissue.

source.subject: Pricing safeguard: benchmark trigger mechanics and implementation
messages:
From: Elena Gross <elena.gross@acmehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@acmehealth.com>
Date: Mon, 5 Sep 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Pricing safeguard: benchmark trigger mechanics and implementation

Hi Ben / Marissa,

Thanks for sharing the draft order form. Our procurement team flagged the new 'benchmark trigger' language in Exhibit B and asked for a short write-up of how it would work operationally. Two quick questions:

- How is the benchmark measured (tokens / API calls / throughput)?
- If a trigger applies, is the adjustment retrospective or forward-looking, and how quickly would it show up on our invoices?

We also saw a potential penalty credit if usage exceeds the cap—can you confirm whether that credit offsets future invoices or is a one-time refund? Procurement would like this clarified before we loop in legal.

Attachment: procurement_rfi_questions.xlsx (stub)

Best,
Elena
Director, Procurement
Acme Health

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Gross <elena.gross@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 5 Sep 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing safeguard: benchmark trigger mechanics and implementation

Elena — thanks for the note. Short version below, attaching an annotated redline of Exhibit B for clarity. Happy to jump on a quick call if that helps.

- Measurement: monthly gross API token consumption (gen + system) aggregated per calendar month. Baseline is the committed monthly average in the order form.
- Trigger mechanics: if a month exceeds the benchmark by >25% the pricing floor steps up for subsequent full months until the 3-month moving average falls below the threshold. Changes are forward-looking (apply starting the next billing period).
- Credit: we provide a one-time offset credit to the next invoice if consumption significantly exceeds cap due to service issues (e.g., routing causing duplication). Normal over-usage does not result in cash refunds; it triggers the step-up mechanism instead.

Key trade-offs: forward-looking adjustments keep billing predictable; the moving average avoids ping-pong. The redline adds language for a maximum annual adjustment cap (no more than 18% increase in a 12-month window) and carveouts for scheduled maintenance.

Attachment: pricing_trigger_redline_v3.docx (stub)

Next step: propose a short call with procurement + legal (30 min) this week to align. Are you available Wed 9/7 or Thu 9/8?

Thanks,
Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Elena Gross <elena.gross@acmehealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 6 Sep 2027 08:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing safeguard: benchmark trigger mechanics and implementation

Adding a couple operational points from CS/RevOps perspective:

- We recommend the 3-month trailing average so temporary spikes (e.g., marketing campaigns) don't immediately re-price Acme.
- Proposal: first trigger is a 10% floor uplift; second consecutive trigger (within 6 months) increases to 15%; beyond that we escalate to a pricing review rather than automatic steps.
- Emergency override: Acme can request an exception for one month per contract year to avoid an automatic step (requires documented incident and joint review).

Jordan, FYI on the invoicing timing — we will implement changes at the invoice cycle boundary (first of next month) and the billing system will show an 'Adjustment due to benchmark trigger' line item.

Marissa
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Elena Gross <elena.gross@acmehealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 7 Sep 2027 10:37:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing safeguard: benchmark trigger mechanics and implementation

Elena — Karthik from Legal here. Per Ben's redline I reviewed and suggested edits to tighten definitions and limit operational exposure. Key legal points in the attached redline:

1) Definition clarity — 'Benchmark' must be defined as 'measured monthly gross inference tokens processed by Redwood's platform for Acme's tenant id(s) excluding test and sandbox tokens as reasonably determined by Redwood and reasonably documented to Acme.'
2) Carveouts — planned maintenance windows (>=24h notified) and force majeure events excluded from trigger calculation.
3) Audit & dispute — Acme has the right to request a one-time audit of a triggering month within 60 days; if audit shows Redwood measurement error >=5% then Redwood will adjust charges and issue a corrective credit.
4) Annual cap — no more than 18% aggregate floor uplift in any 12-month period.
5) Transition phrasing — specify adjustments are forward-looking and will be reflected in the first full invoice period following written notice.

I also limited language around 'penalty credit' — we propose 'invoice offset' capped at the greater of (actual measured cost impact OR $20,000) for operational errors attributable to Redwood. That avoids open-ended refunds.

Attachment: exhibit-b_pricing_benchmark_trigger_v1.pdf (redline stub)

Please review; happy to take comments or join the call if legal needs to be present.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

From: Timothy Sloane <timothy.sloane@acmelegal.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Elena Gross <elena.gross@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 8 Sep 2027 09:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing safeguard: benchmark trigger mechanics and implementation

Karthik — thanks for the redline. A few counterpoints from Acme's counsel:

- Measurement transparency: we need a daily usage report (CSV) available via SFTP within 48 hours of month-end for our internal reconciliation.
- The 18% annual cap is acceptable but we request a lower per-trigger uplift (first trigger 5%, second 10%).
- 'Invoice offset' language: Acme prefers a cash credit (applied to future invoices) with a minimum of $25,000 for verified platform-caused overcharges.
- Audit mechanics: our auditor should be allowed to run a read-only verification against the same metrics Redwood uses; Redwood may redact internal-only fields.

Attached is a marked-up version proposing the above. We'll need to align on sample reporting format before signing.

Attachment: acme_marked_pricing_trigger_comments.docx (stub)

Regards,
Timothy Sloane
Partner, Sloane & Partners (Counsel to Acme Health)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Gross <elena.gross@acmehealth.com>, Timothy Sloane <timothy.sloane@acmelegal.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 11 Sep 2027 15:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing safeguard: benchmark trigger mechanics and implementation

Thanks all — constructive thread. Quick summary and proposed next steps so we have alignment before legal redline swaps: 

1) Measurement & report: Redwood will provide a daily usage CSV via SFTP within 72 hours of month-end (we can shorten to 48h once the SFTP is established).
2) Trigger uplift: Redwood accepts a reduced first-trigger uplift of 7.5% and 12.5% on the second trigger within a rolling 6-month window; both subject to the 18% annual cap.
3) Invoice offset: we'll keep the invoice-offset model (applied to future invoices) with a minimum $20k cap; open to discussing a higher floor for enterprise customers on a case-by-case basis.
4) Audit: Acme may request a one-time audit per triggering year; Redwood will provide read-only data feeds for verification with reasonable redactions.

Calls to schedule: suggest Tue 9/14 10:00am PT — procurement + Acme legal + Redwood legal + CS. Ben will send a calendar invite.

Next deliverables: Ben to circulate an updated Exhibit B redline (incorporating Karthik's language + agreed parameter tweaks) by EOD Mon 9/13.

DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/packets/abc123-ACM
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/company/ACM-2027-11

Cheers,
Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Advanced dashboards carveout & tracing fee — counteroffer and pilot credit
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Singh <priya.singh@nimbushealth.com>, Raj Patel <raj@cloudprocure.io>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Aug 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Observability add‑ons: carveout approach + initial numbers attached

Hi Priya / Raj —

Thanks for the time yesterday. Attached is a concise packaging proposal that separates our core API commit from the observability add‑ons (retention, tracing ingest, and advanced dashboards) so you can see the unit economics clearly. Key points up front: 

- We can carve out the advanced dashboards from the seat-based entitlements so they won’t count against your primary user seats. That helps when clinical analytics seats spike.
- Tracing ingest will be metered; we can offer a capped tier for the pilot to limit surprise bills.
- We’ve included two commercial levers: a one‑time implementation credit and a volume discount tier on add‑ons that scales by average monthly ingest.

See pricing_grid_nimbus_v4.xlsx for the line‑item view and the attached SOW_Nimbus_Observability_Carveout_v2.pdf for scope.

Ask: can you confirm whether the carveout meets the procurement/risk needs (i.e., dashboards excluded from seat counts), and share a target annual add‑on spend so I can model the discount curve?

If it helps we can propose a 90‑day pilot with a $10k credit against the add‑ons while you exercise queries and trace patterns.

Thanks — I’ll stand by for the budgeting signal and will loop legal once we have alignment on carveout language.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Executive
Redwood Inference
(attachment: SOW_Nimbus_Observability_Carveout_v2.pdf)
(attachment: pricing_grid_nimbus_v4.xlsx)

From: Priya Singh <priya.singh@nimbushealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Raj Patel <raj@cloudprocure.io>
Date: Tue, 31 Aug 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Observability add‑ons: carveout approach + initial numbers attached

Ben —

Appreciate the quick turn. A few clarifying items from procurement and engineering: 

1) By “carve out the advanced dashboards”, we mean they should not be tied to our licensed clinician seats or push us into higher seat tiers during usage spikes. If dashboards are standalone SKU that’s ideal.

2) Tracing ingest: we like the idea of a capped pilot tier. Can you confirm what the cap looks like (GB/day) and the overage rate? We had something like 500GB/day in mind for initial POC.

3) Commercial ask: Nimbus has an internal guidance to get to at least a 20% discount on add‑ons for any new vendor at contract signing if we commit 12 months. If Redwood can’t reach 20% across the board, is there room for a split approach (20% on dashboards + 10% on tracing, or similar)?

4) We’ll need carveout language in the SOW that explicitly states dashboards will be invoiced separately and will not affect seat counts.

Happy to jump on a short call if it’s easier.

—Priya
Director, Procurement
Nimbus Health

(attachment: nimbus_commercial_requirements.pdf)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 31 Aug 2027 13:20:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Observability add‑ons: carveout approach + initial numbers attached

Folks — forwarding Priya's notes. Quick ask: procurement is pushing for 20% discount on add‑ons if Nimbus commits 12 months. They explicitly want dashboards carved out from seat math and a capped tracing pilot (500GB/day).

My initial thinking: 
- Dashboards: we can offer standalone dashboard SKU and give 20% off if they sign 12‑month commitment for dashboards AND the core commit stays at our standard rate.
- Tracing: instead of 20% off, propose a 10% discount + three months of pilot credits equivalent to $12k (to offset expected ingest costs while they tune instrumentation). Overage rate set at $0.08/GB after cap during pilot.

Pricing impact summary (back‑of‑envelope): 20% on dashboard list = ~$48k ARR reduction vs list; tracing concession = ~$9k ARR concession for first year assuming their pilot becomes steady. We can offset with a clause: 6‑month review to adjust discounts if usage materially exceeds projections.

Would love Marissa’s signoff on range and Jordan to confirm whether $0.08/GB is allowable on the tracing side. Also legal needs a clear carveout clause for the SOW — I can draft and send once we align.

Ben

On Tue, Aug 31, 2027 at 11:03 AM Priya Singh <priya.singh@nimbushealth.com> wrote:
> Ben —
> 
> Appreciate the quick turn. A few clarifying items from procurement and engineering: 
> 
> 1) By “carve out the advanced dashboards”, we mean they should not be tied to our licensed clinician seats or push us into higher seat tiers during usage spikes. If dashboards are standalone SKU that’s ideal.
> 
> 2) Tracing ingest: we like the idea of a capped pilot tier. Can you confirm what the cap looks like (GB/day) and the overage rate? We had something like 500GB/day in mind for initial POC.
> 
> 3) Commercial ask: Nimbus has an internal guidance to get to at least a 20% discount on add‑ons for any new vendor at contract signing if we commit 12 months. If Redwood can’t reach 20% across the board, is there room for a split approach (20% on dashboards + 10% on tracing, or similar)?
> 
> 4) We’ll need carveout language in the SOW that explicitly states dashboards will be invoiced separately and will not affect seat counts.
> 
> Happy to jump on a short call if it’s easier.
> 
> —Priya

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 1 Sep 2027 09:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Observability add‑ons: carveout approach + initial numbers attached

Ben —

Thanks for the context. I think we can meet them in the middle with a commercially clean offer that preserves economics: 

Proposal to present: 
- Advanced dashboards: standalone SKU, 20% discount off list if Nimbus signs a 12‑month commitment for the dashboards SKU. Contractually we’ll add an addendum that dashboards are invoiced separately and excluded from seat counts.

- Tracing ingest: capped pilot (500GB/day) with the following commercials: 10% discount on list price for the first 12 months + a pilot credit of $15,000 applied to the first invoice. Overage after pilot: $0.09/GB (slightly higher than Ben's spreadsheet but gives us margin). We’ll include a 6‑month review clause to revisit ingest economics.

- Implementation/onboarding: waive the first month of dashboard licensing (equivalent credit) if Nimbus executes within 14 days. That reduces friction for procurement.

Rationale: Hitting 20% on dashboards keeps procurement happy; the tracing concession is meaningful without setting a deep, long‑term price precedent. The $15k credit is within our deal desk limits for this ARR.

Jordan — confirm the $0.09/GB overage and that legal can create a succinct carveout addendum. If you approve, Ben, I’ll update the pricing_grid_nimbus_v4.xlsx (attached) and return an updated SOW for signature.

Marissa
VP, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
(attachment: pricing_grid_nimbus_v4_updated.xlsx)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Singh <priya.singh@nimbushealth.com>
Cc: Raj Patel <raj@cloudprocure.io>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Bcc: legal@redwoodinference.com
Date: Thu, 2 Sep 2027 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Final offer: dashboards carveout, tracing pilot credit, and next steps (DocuSign link)

Priya / Raj —

Thanks again for the candid asks. After a quick internal review we’re prepared to proceed with the following commercial package: 

1) Advanced dashboards: standalone SKU, 20% discount off list price with a 12‑month minimum commitment for dashboards. Dashboards are explicitly excluded from seat counts in the attached SOW addendum.

2) Tracing: 500GB/day capped pilot for 90 days. During the pilot we’ll apply a $15,000 credit toward tracing charges and provide 10% off list for months 1–12. Overage after the pilot will be billed at $0.09/GB. We’ll include a 6‑month usage review to confirm steady‑state pricing.

3) Implementation: first month of dashboard licensing waived (applied as credit) if the SOW is countersigned within 14 days.

Attachments: SOW_Nimbus_Observability_Carveout_v2.pdf (updated with carveout addendum), pricing_grid_nimbus_v4_updated.xlsx, implementation_plan.docx

Next steps: 
- Please confirm acceptance by replying to this thread. We will push the SOW to DocuSign (link below) once you confirm commercials.
- HubSpot deal updated: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/9876543210
- DocuSign (preview): https://www.docusign.com/en/sign/abcdef123456

If you’d prefer a quick 20‑minute call to walk through the SOW I can join with Marissa and Jordan.

Looking forward to getting Nimbus live.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Executive
Redwood Inference
(attachment: SOW_Nimbus_Observability_Carveout_v2.pdf)
(attachment: pricing_grid_nimbus_v4_updated.xlsx)
(attachment: implementation_plan.docx)

source.subject: Crestline RFI: ingestion checklist, KMS proofing, and production segmentation
messages:
From: Emily Park <emily.park@crestline.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
Cc: Arjun Patel <arjun.patel@crestline.com>, Crestline Procurement <procurement@crestline.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Aug 2027 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: RFI: KMS proofing + ingestion segmentation details

Hi Connor,

Thanks again for the call this morning. Procurement asked us to capture a short, clear set of artifacts so we can close security review for the POC. Specifically: 

- A diagram showing key lifecycle and KMS integration for customer-managed keys (if available).

From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>
To: Emily Park <emily.park@crestline.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 31 Aug 2027 08:44:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: RFI: KMS proofing + ingestion segmentation details

Hi Emily —

Thanks for the clear list. I’ve pulled together the artifacts and a concise write-up. Summary here, full artifacts attached.

KMS/keyflow (attachment: kms_keyflow_diagram.png):
- Redwood supports both provider-managed keys (default) and customer-managed keys (CMK) via customer KMS (AWS KMS / GCP KMS / Azure Key Vault).
- For CMK: we store only the key identifier and perform envelope encryption in our runtime. The actual DEK (data encryption keys) are ephemeral and sealed by the CMK; Redwood never has access to CMK root material.
- Key rotation: CMK rotation is controlled by the customer KMS policy; our runtime responds to a new key version identifier.

Production segmentation (attachment: prod-segmentation-matrix.pdf):
- Hosted API (redwood.ai): logical tenant isolation at the application layer with strict per-account namespaces, per-tenant KV cache namespaces, and metadata tagging for tracing; storage is multi-tenant with encryption-at-rest and per-account logical separation.

Subject: RE: RFI: KMS proofing + ingestion segmentation details

2) The audit log sample is helpful; could we get one example of a CloudTrail-like event that shows a requester identity mapping to a human user (redacted) so our IAM reviewer can validate traceability?

—Arjun

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Arjun Patel <arjun.patel@crestline.com>, Emily Park <emily.park@crestline.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 02 Sep 2027 15:05:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: RFI: KMS proofing + ingestion segmentation details

Arjun / Emily —

Quick update: Mina added a short HSM boundary note to the KMS diagram and a redacted traceability example to the audit log sample (see updated attachments). We also included an explicit line about DEK lifecycle: DEKs are generated per inference session, cached per-process for up to 30 seconds for KV cache warm-up, then purged; never written to disk outside of encrypted stores.

Connor will upload the final bundle to the Drive link and post the HubSpot record for traceability (HBSP-129483). If you confirm receipt we’ll finalize for contract attachment.

Thanks —

source.subject: Re: Docs CTA — technical onboarding slot for developer trial
messages:
From: Samir Patel <samir@buildforge.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, Sep 1, 2027 at 09:12 AM -0700
Subject: Interest from docs CTA — developer trial, request tech demo

Hi Jordan,

I followed the ‘Get started’ link in your docs and spun up a short trial with our engineering team. The quickstart was great — we hit a question about tokenization behavior with long sequences and would love a short technical walkthrough to confirm integration assumptions before we scale.

High-level: BuildForge is evaluating an embedding + generation pipeline for our code-assist product. We want to validate latency at 1k token windows, KV cache persistence across sessions, and any recommended batching settings.

Can someone from your engineering side do a 30–45 minute technical call this week? Our engineers are in PST (Palo Alto) and prefer afternoons. I’m including Priya (engineering lead) on this thread.

Thanks,
Samir Patel
Co-founder, BuildForge
samir@buildforge.ai
Attachment: trial-export-usage.csv (CSV stub)
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@buildforge.ai>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, Sep 1, 2027 at 10:03 AM -0700
Subject: Re: Docs CTA — technical onboarding slot for developer trial

Thanks Samir — glad the quickstart was useful. I’m Jordan on the SDR/technical intake team at Redwood.

Camila is our ML/serving engineer who usually runs these technical sessions; Vivek is handling scheduling and the handoff. Camila — can you do either of the windows below? Each session is 40 minutes (20m walk-through + 20m Q&A):

  - Thu Sep 2, 2027: 1:30pm–2:10pm PT
  - Thu Sep 2, 2027: 3:00pm–3:40pm PT
  - Fri Sep 3, 2027: 2:00pm–2:40pm PT

If none work, tell us two other 1-hour blocks and Vivek will send a calendar invite.

Prep for the call (if you have them ready, please attach):
  - a short sample request pattern (prompt + expected token lengths)
  - any perf traces from the trial run (we can help interpret)
  - desired SLOs for latency/throughput

Samir/Priya — would you prefer a brief sandbox walkthrough (live request from your environment) or a slide-based overview first?

Jordan Blake
Solutions Ops, Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.ai
Phone: +1 415-555-0102
From: Samir Patel <samir@buildforge.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Reddy <priya@buildforge.ai>
Date: Wed, Sep 1, 2027 at 11:08 AM -0700
Subject: Re: Docs CTA — technical onboarding slot for developer trial

On Wed, Sep 1, 2027 at 10:03 AM Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Camila is our ML/serving engineer who usually runs these technical sessions; Vivek is handling scheduling and the handoff. Camila — can you do either of the windows below?
>
>   - Thu Sep 2, 2027: 1:30pm–2:10pm PT
>   - Thu Sep 2, 2027: 3:00pm–3:40pm PT
>   - Fri Sep 3, 2027: 2:00pm–2:40pm PT

Thanks — 3:00pm PT on Thu Sep 2 works best for our engineers. We’ll do a live request from our sandbox so Camila can watch the latency and trace logs in real time. Attaching a small sample prompt and the CSV export from our trial run (token counts + timing).

Also, can you include any recommended batching defaults for gen + embed mixed workloads? That’s one area we want to tune.

Samir
Attachment: sample-prompt.txt (TXT stub), trial-export-usage.csv (CSV stub)
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@buildforge.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, Sep 2, 2027 at 11:45 AM -0700
Subject: Re: Docs CTA — technical onboarding slot for developer trial (confirmed)

Hi Samir / Priya —

Confirmed: Thu Sep 2, 2027, 3:00pm–3:40pm PT. I’ve sent a calendar invite (see attachment calendar-invite-buildforge.ics) with a short agenda and join link.

Quick agenda:
  1) 5m introductions + goals
  2) 10m review of your sandbox request pattern and trial traces
  3) 15m live run (we’ll watch KV cache and trace token latencies)
  4) 10m tuning recommendations (batching, max-simultaneous-requests, cache TTL)

A few practical notes for a smooth session:
  - If you can run the live request from a single controlled client, state the exact sequence length and concurrency you want measured.
  - Share any CI/test harness links in the Drive link Jordan included so I can pre-load traces.
  - I’ll bring a short checklist (attached buildforge-trial-details.pdf) with the metric names we’ll watch.

Looking forward — Camila
Camila Reyes
ML Infrastructure Engineer, Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.ai
Attachment: buildforge-trial-details.pdf (PDF stub), calendar-invite-buildforge.ics (ICS stub)

source.subject: KMS custodian proxy wiring & rotation checklist
messages:
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: MedSys Ops <ops@medsys.io>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Aug 2027 10:14:00 -0700
Subject: KMS custodian proxy wiring & rotation checklist

Hi Anita / MedSys team,

Thanks again for agreeing to the private deployment proof-of-concept. Before we schedule the first cutover window, we need to lock down how the customer-side KMS custodian proxy will be wired into the VPC and how rotation events will be executed end-to-end.

High-level asks from our side:
- Confirm an ops contact for network allowlist and firewall changes (we suggested Anita Rao).
- Confirm whether the KMS proxy will run in the same VPC as application hosts or in a separate management VPC/subnet.
- Provide an allowlist CSV (CIDRs and load-balancer IPs) we can use for our NAT/bastion rules.
- Agree on an initial rotation cadence (we propose quarterly automated rewrap with manual canary).

We've attached a quick diagram (placeholder) and a short checklist to start the conversation. Major constraints for compliance: HSM attestation windows must be preserved during rotation; operator separation (custodian vs. platform) should be enforced; audit logs must be forwarded to your SIEM endpoint.

Deliverable requested: a one-page network wiring confirmation and a target date for a canary rotation test (we're looking at Aug 30–Sep 3 window).

Attachments: kms-proxy-wiring-diagram.pdf (stub)

Thanks,
David Kim
Director, Private Deployments
Redwood Inference
From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, MedSys Ops <ops@medsys.io>
Date: Mon, 16 Aug 2027 14:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: KMS custodian proxy wiring & rotation checklist

Thanks David — looping in a few specifics from security so we can converge on an acceptance checklist. I've attached a worked rotation checklist (custodian-rotation-checklist.xlsx) with the steps we require for a minimal-compliance cutover. Key notes: 

- Transport: We require mutual TLS between Redwood's KMS proxy and the on-prem / VPC custodian proxy; TLS certs should be rotated independently of key-material rotations.
- Attestation: If you're using an HSM-backed KMS, provide attestation reports for the HSM firmware version and the attestation nonce window.
- Operator separation: Custodian operator accounts must not have direct access to the Redwood control-plane accounts. Provide a list of custodian operator emails for allowlisting.
- Rotation flow (high-level):
  1) Canary rewrap on dedicated canary key in staging.
  2) Promote to production key rewrap with monitoring hooks.
  3) Post-rotation telemetry snapshot + audit log export.
- Telemetry/audit: We need an export endpoint (syslog/HTTPS) for audit events and a retention SLA for 90 days for rotation events.

Ask for MedSys: can you confirm whether the custodian proxy must sit behind your internal load balancer or if we can deploy to a management subnet with a single public egress IP (preferred for simpler egress rules)?

Attachments: custodian-rotation-checklist.xlsx (stub)

—Naomi
Head of Product Security
Redwood Inference

On Mon, 16 Aug 2027 at 10:14 AM David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Anita / MedSys team,
> 
> Thanks again for agreeing to the private deployment proof-of-concept. Before we schedule the first cutover window, we need to lock down how the customer-side KMS custodian proxy will be wired into the VPC and how rotation events will be executed end-to-end.
From: MedSys Ops <ops@medsys.io>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Aug 2027 09:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: KMS custodian proxy wiring & rotation checklist

Hi David / Naomi / team,

Thanks — Anita here (ops lead). We're OK with the management-subnet approach you prefer; it reduces blast radius for app traffic. I've attached our allowlist CSV with the VPC CIDRs and the single NAT egress IP we can publish.

Quick answers:
- We'll host the custodian proxy in a management subnet behind an internal NLB. We'll publish the NLB private IPs for your inbound rules.
- Ports: 443 (mTLS) and 22 (for operator jumpbox during scheduled maintenance windows) are fine. We'll restrict 22 to our operator IPs and your bastion range.
- Attestation: we can provide HSM attestation reports; our vendor (SafeHSM) supports canonical attestation.
- Audit export: we can forward syslog to your ingestion endpoint over TLS; please provide an ingestion hostname.

Attachments: medsys-network-allowlist.csv (stub)

Next steps from our side: share the exact IPs for the NLB by EOD tomorrow (Aug 18). Our operator contact is Anita Rao <anita.rao@medsys.io>.

Regards,
Anita Rao
Infrastructure Lead, MedSys
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: MedSys Ops <ops@medsys.io>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Aug 2027 17:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: KMS custodian proxy wiring & rotation checklist

Great — thanks Anita and team. Summary / agreed actions:

1) MedSys to publish NLB private IPs and confirmed management-subnet deployment by EOD Aug 18 (Anita).
2) MedSys to provide HSM attestation artifacts (vendor-signed) and operator list by Aug 20.
3) Redwood to provide an audit ingestion hostname and short-runner for syslog over TLS (we will open a service ticket INF-3241).
4) Canary rotation test scheduled: Sep 2 (canary rewrap only). Production rotation week: Sep 20–24 (tentative).

Attachments: kms-proxy-wiring-diagram.pdf (updated stub)

Action owners: 
- Anita Rao (MedSys): NLB IPs + attestation artifacts (due Aug 20).
- Naomi Feldman (Redwood): finalize rotation checklist and test plan (due Aug 22).
- Marissa Cole / Jordan Blake (Redwood infra): prepare canary environment and control-plane playbook (due Aug 28).
- David Kim (Redwood): coordinate cutover window and acceptance criteria; raise INF-3241.

If there are any blockers with port constraints or attestation formatting, call it out today. Otherwise we'll treat the network wiring as accepted once Anita publishes the IPs.

Thanks everyone — I'll run the kickoff tomorrow at 11:00 PT if that works for MedSys.

Best,
David Kim
Director, Private Deployments
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Adaptive Pack — tier swap options & stepdown guardrails
messages:
From: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>
To: Eli Turner <eli.turner@courtyard.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Aug 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Adaptive Pack — tier swap options & stepdown guardrails

Hi Eli —

Thanks again for the time yesterday. Per our conversation I sketched three practical options for Courtyard’s move from Compact to Adaptive Pack with a fallback path if load dips below expected thresholds. Attached is a short PDF that summarizes the tradeoffs; calling out the parts most relevant to you below so it’s quick to scan.

Quick TL;DR:
- Option A (Gradual swap): 3-month ramp to full Adaptive Pack, prorated credits if throughput drops >30% month-over-month. Lowest up-front commitment.
- Option B (Pilot commit): 6-month pilot with guaranteed per-token floor and a mid-term pricing review; includes 2 pilot seats for free; best for validating tail latency at scale.
- Option C (Reserved throughput): annual reserved capacity (lowest unit cost) + stepdown credit ladder on downgrades.

Two clarifying questions for procurement/legal before we lock language:
1) Does Courtyard need an explicit escape hatch for seat-level reductions (we proposed a 45-day notice window)?
2) Which percentile (95th vs. 99th) should we size the burst protection for — you mentioned 95th in the call but procurement asked about worst-case.

source.subject: Contract renewal: Move invoicing to Azure Marketplace (SkyBridge / Redwood)
messages:
From: Timothy Hale <tim.hale@skybridge.gov>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@skybridge.gov>
Date: 2027-08-11T09:02:00-04:00
Subject: Contract Renewal — billing via Azure Marketplace (SkyBridge / Redwood)

Hi Ava,

We have an upcoming renewal for our contract (SKY-1819) with Redwood that needs to align with SkyBridge procurement rules. For FY28 we are required to route software purchases through Azure Marketplace (for audit and internal PO flow).

Can Redwood bill via Azure Marketplace for the renewal effective 2027-09-15? If so, what do you need from our side (PO, procurement contact, signer) and what is the timeline to appear as an Azure Marketplace subscription to our finance team?

We'd also like to understand whether the renewal can carry any existing committed discounts and whether there will be an invoice example we can share with our accounts payable.

Thanks — please loop in whoever at Redwood owns the marketplace onboarding.

Timothy Hale
Head of Procurement, SkyBridge Systems
Tim.Hale@skybridge.gov
Office: +1-202-555-0126

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Timothy Hale <tim.hale@skybridge.gov>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-08-11T10:18:00-04:00
Subject: Re: Contract Renewal — billing via Azure Marketplace (SkyBridge / Redwood)

Hi Timothy —

Thanks for the heads up and the clear timing. Short answer: yes, we can convert the renewal to Azure Marketplace invoicing, and we can preserve the commercial terms in most cases, but there are a couple of items we need to coordinate:

- Exact contract/PO reference (SKY-1819 is helpful).
- Formal PO line (if you have a PO number for the renewal).
- Primary procurement contact (we'll send the DocuSign and the Marketplace quote to them).
- Desired go-live date (you mentioned 2027-09-15).

Operationally we'll need to:
1) Map your current committed price to an Azure SKU (our Marketplace team will publish a private offer or map the existing SKU depending on your enterprise agreement).
2) Create the Marketplace listing/offer and assign entitlements to your tenant.
3) Issue a sample Marketplace invoice and a mutual action plan for the cutover.

I attached a copy of the current contract reference you mentioned for our team to confirm (attachment: contract_SKY-1819.pdf).

I'll bring Jordan (channel lead) and Grace (finance/program ops) into the thread to scope the enablement checklist and timelines. Expect a short internal outreach in the next couple hours to loop Microsoft.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
Cell: +1-415-555-3287
Redwood Inference

Attachment: contract_SKY-1819.pdf

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Azure Marketplace Partner Team <mp_team@microsoft.com>, Azure Co-sell Desk <cosell@microsoft.com>, Finance Ops <finance_ops@redwood.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-08-11T14:02:00-04:00
Subject: Fwd: SKY-1819 — Marketplace conversion request (SkyBridge)

Team,

Forwarding SkyBridge's renewal request. They are a public-sector customer and must route renewals through Azure Marketplace starting this fiscal year. Key asks from procurement: preserve committed discount and provide an example invoice/AP file to satisfy their PO process.

What we need from Microsoft:
- Confirmation of whether this will be a private offer or standard plan.
- Marketplace SKU and publisher IDs so we can provision entitlements.
- Expected lead time for the offer to be visible to SkyBridge's tenant (they are targeting 2027-09-15).
- Any co-sell credits/eligibility impact for revenue recognition.

Grace/Vivek — can you confirm internal ops: are there any blocking accounting items for a marketplace renewal on a public-sector contract? Also include a sample invoice from Marketplace if available.

Deal: DBL-2041 / SKY-1819

Thanks,
Jordan Blake
Channel Partnerships | Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com

From: Azure Marketplace Partner Team <mp_team@microsoft.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Azure Co-sell Desk <cosell@microsoft.com>, Timothy Hale <tim.hale@skybridge.gov>
Date: 2027-08-12T08:30:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Fwd: SKY-1819 — Marketplace conversion request (SkyBridge)

Hi Jordan / Ava,

Thanks for reaching out and for the clear timeline. We can support a private offer that preserves committed pricing. High level next steps:

1) Publisher onboarding confirmation (we show Redwood's publisher id: RWD-778).
2) Create private offer referencing the existing SKU and provide a DocuSign quote linked to SkyBridge's tenant.
3) Once signed, Microsoft will provision the entitlement and SkyBridge will see the subscription in their tenant within 48–72 hours.

What we need from SkyBridge/Redwood:
- Copy of the purchase order and exact PO number.
- Confirmation of the billing entity (Microsoft invoice to SkyBridge or reseller invoicing).
- Any special PO fields required for AP.

Attached is an onboarding checklist and a sample invoice template. I'll also open a co-sell case to confirm any incentive eligibility.

Azure Marketplace Partner Team
mp_team@microsoft.com
Attachment: azure_mp_onboarding_checklist.xlsx

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Timothy Hale <tim.hale@skybridge.gov>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Azure Marketplace Partner Team <mp_team@microsoft.com>
Date: 2027-08-12T09:45:00-04:00
Subject: Re: Contract Renewal — billing via Azure Marketplace (SkyBridge / Redwood)

Timothy —

Thanks, and quick update below with a proposed cutover plan:

Mutual action plan (proposed)
- 2027-08-12: Redwood / Microsoft send private offer and DocuSign to SkyBridge. (In progress)
- 2027-08-13: SkyBridge returns signed DocuSign + PO number.
- 2027-08-15: Azure provisions entitlement and subscription appears in SkyBridge tenant.
- 2027-09-15: Renewal effective date (billing starts through Azure Marketplace as agreed).

What we need from you: please confirm the PO number and whether SkyBridge requires Microsoft to include any specific PO fields on the invoice. Grace will prepare a sample Marketplace invoice and an AP-friendly PDF for your finance team.

Attached: MAP_SkyBridge_Aug2027.docx (mutual action plan / contact matrix). I will also add an example invoice once Grace confirms details.

If that timeline works, I can set a 20-minute kickoff call tomorrow afternoon to walk procurement and your AP team through the DocuSign and the sample invoice.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference

Attachment: MAP_SkyBridge_Aug2027.docx

From: Timothy Hale <tim.hale@skybridge.gov>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@skybridge.gov>, Azure Marketplace Partner Team <mp_team@microsoft.com>
Date: 2027-08-13T11:12:00-04:00
Subject: Re: Contract Renewal — billing via Azure Marketplace (SkyBridge / Redwood)

Ava / team —

Thanks — the proposed timeline looks good. We will sign DocuSign tomorrow (8/14) and provide PO-5563 as our renewal PO. A couple quick asks from AP:

- Can you provide a sample invoice file (PDF) and a CSV with line-level detail they can ingest into our ERP?
- Please include our PO number next to the invoice reference and in the invoice metadata.

If Azure can provision the entitlement by 8/15 that will give us a week to validate before the 9/15 effective date. Please send the DocuSign link and sample invoice to procurement@skybridge.gov.

Timothy Hale
Head of Procurement, SkyBridge Systems
Attachment: PO-5563.pdf

Subject: Contract Renewal — billing via Azure Marketplace (SkyBridge / Redwood)

Hi Ava,

We have an upcoming renewal for our contract (SKY-1819) with Redwood that needs to align with SkyBridge procurement rules. For FY28 we are required to route software purchases through Azure Marketplace (for audit and internal PO flow).

Can Redwood bill via Azure Marketplace for the renewal effective 2027-09-15? If so, what do you need from our side (PO, procurement contact, signer) and what is the timeline to appear as an Azure Marketplace subscription to our finance team?

We'd also like to understand whether the renewal can carry any existing committed discounts and whether there will be an invoice example we can share with our accounts payable.

Thanks — please loop in whoever at Redwood owns the marketplace onboarding.

Timothy Hale
Head of Procurement, SkyBridge Systems
Tim.Hale@skybridge.gov
Office: +1-202-555-0126

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Timothy Hale <tim.hale@skybridge.gov>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-08-11T10:18:00-04:00
Subject: Re: Contract Renewal — billing via Azure Marketplace (SkyBridge / Redwood)

Hi Timothy —

Thanks for the heads up and the clear timing. Short answer: yes, we can convert the renewal to Azure Marketplace invoicing, and we can preserve the commercial terms in most cases, but there are a couple of items we need to coordinate:

- Exact contract/PO reference (SKY-1819 is helpful).
- Formal PO line (if you have a PO number for the renewal).
- Primary procurement contact (we'll send the DocuSign and the Marketplace quote to them).
- Desired go-live date (you mentioned 2027-09-15).

Operationally we'll need to:
1) Map your current committed price to an Azure SKU (our Marketplace team will publish a private offer or map the existing SKU depending on your enterprise agreement).
2) Create the Marketplace listing/offer and assign entitlements to your tenant.
3) Issue a sample Marketplace invoice and a mutual action plan for the cutover.

I attached a copy of the current contract reference you mentioned for our team to confirm (attachment: contract_SKY-1819.pdf).

I'll bring Jordan (channel lead) and Grace (finance/program ops) into the thread to scope the enablement checklist and timelines. Expect a short internal outreach in the next couple hours to loop Microsoft.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
Cell: +1-415-555-3287
Redwood Inference

Attachment: contract_SKY-1819.pdf

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Azure Marketplace Partner Team <mp_team@microsoft.com>, Azure Co-sell Desk <cosell@microsoft.com>, Finance Ops <finance_ops@redwood.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-08-11T14:02:00-04:00
Subject: Fwd: SKY-1819 — Marketplace conversion request (SkyBridge)

Team,

Forwarding SkyBridge's renewal request. They are a public-sector customer and must route renewals through Azure Marketplace starting this fiscal year. Key asks from procurement: preserve committed discount and provide an example invoice/AP file to satisfy their PO process.

What we need from Microsoft:
- Confirmation of whether this will be a private offer or standard plan.
- Marketplace SKU and publisher IDs so we can provision entitlements.
- Expected lead time for the offer to be visible to SkyBridge's tenant (they are targeting 2027-09-15).
- Any co-sell credits/eligibility impact for revenue recognition.

Grace/Vivek — can you confirm internal ops: are there any blocking accounting items for a marketplace renewal on a public-sector contract? Also include a sample invoice from Marketplace if available.

Deal: DBL-2041 / SKY-1819

Thanks,
Jordan Blake
Channel Partnerships | Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com

From: Azure Marketplace Partner Team <mp_team@microsoft.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Azure Co-sell Desk <cosell@microsoft.com>, Timothy Hale <tim.hale@skybridge.gov>
Date: 2027-08-12T08:30:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Fwd: SKY-1819 — Marketplace conversion request (SkyBridge)

Hi Jordan / Ava,

Thanks for reaching out and for the clear timeline. We can support a private offer that preserves committed pricing. High level next steps:

1) Publisher onboarding confirmation (we show Redwood's publisher id: RWD-778).
2) Create private offer referencing the existing SKU and provide a DocuSign quote linked to SkyBridge's tenant.
3) Once signed, Microsoft will provision the entitlement and SkyBridge will see the subscription in their tenant within 48–72 hours.

What we need from SkyBridge/Redwood:
- Copy of the purchase order and exact PO number.
- Confirmation of the billing entity (Microsoft invoice to SkyBridge or reseller invoicing).
- Any special PO fields required for AP.

Attached is an onboarding checklist and a sample invoice template. I'll also open a co-sell case to confirm any incentive eligibility.

Azure Marketplace Partner Team
mp_team@microsoft.com
Attachment: azure_mp_onboarding_checklist.xlsx

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Timothy Hale <tim.hale@skybridge.gov>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Azure Marketplace Partner Team <mp_team@microsoft.com>
Date: 2027-08-12T09:45:00-04:00
Subject: Re: Contract Renewal — billing via Azure Marketplace (SkyBridge / Redwood)

Timothy —

Thanks, and quick update below with a proposed cutover plan:

Mutual action plan (proposed)
- 2027-08-12: Redwood / Microsoft send private offer and DocuSign to SkyBridge. (In progress)
- 2027-08-13: SkyBridge returns signed DocuSign + PO number.
- 2027-08-15: Azure provisions entitlement and subscription appears in SkyBridge tenant.
- 2027-09-15: Renewal effective date (billing starts through Azure Marketplace as agreed).

What we need from you: please confirm the PO number and whether SkyBridge requires Microsoft to include any specific PO fields on the invoice. Grace will prepare a sample Marketplace invoice and an AP-friendly PDF for your finance team.

Attached: MAP_SkyBridge_Aug2027.docx (mutual action plan / contact matrix). I will also add an example invoice once Grace confirms details.

If that timeline works, I can set a 20-minute kickoff call tomorrow afternoon to walk procurement and your AP team through the DocuSign and the sample invoice.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference

Attachment: MAP_SkyBridge_Aug2027.docx

From: Timothy Hale <tim.hale@skybridge.gov>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@skybridge.gov>, Azure Marketplace Partner Team <mp_team@microsoft.com>
Date: 2027-08-13T11:12:00-04:00
Subject: Re: Contract Renewal — billing via Azure Marketplace (SkyBridge / Redwood)

Ava / team —

Thanks — the proposed timeline looks good. We will sign DocuSign tomorrow (8/14) and provide PO-5563 as our renewal PO. A couple quick asks from AP:

- Can you provide a sample invoice file (PDF) and a CSV with line-level detail they can ingest into our ERP?
- Please include our PO number next to the invoice reference and in the invoice metadata.

If Azure can provision the entitlement by 8/15 that will give us a week to validate before the 9/15 effective date. Please send the DocuSign link and sample invoice to procurement@skybridge.gov.

Timothy Hale
Head of Procurement, SkyBridge Systems
Attachment: PO-5563.pdf

source.subject: Re: Greenline Pay demo — conflict with other commitment
messages:
From: Ellis Moore <ellis.m@greenlinepay.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 19 Jul 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Intro + demo — Tues 26th at 1pm PT?

Hi Vanessa —

Thanks for the intro last week. Priya (cc'd) and I are available next Tuesday, July 26 at 1:00pm PT for a 45-min product walkthrough and a brief discussion of our platform constraints. Can you confirm the session and send an agenda? We have a couple of execs joining for the last 15 minutes.

High-level goals:
- See latency/cost profile for embedding-heavy workflows
- Understand privacy/data retention options
- Quick run of a typical inference flow

Appreciate it —
Ellis
Ellis Moore
Director of Platform, Greenline Pay
ellis.m@greenlinepay.com

Attachments: none

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
To: Ellis Moore <ellis.m@greenlinepay.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 19 Jul 2027 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intro + demo — Tues 26th at 1pm PT?

Hi Ellis —

Perfect — we've penciled you in for Tue 26th, 1:00pm PT. I have Dylan (SE) joining to run the technical demo and Jordan (AM) will hop on for wrap-up to discuss next steps. I've attached a short agenda and a mutual action plan draft to this thread so your team can add items.

Quick note: Dylan flagged a potential calendar overlap on his end (customer onsite that day) — Dylan, can you confirm whether you can still lead or if we should swap with another SE? If needed we can move the session to 11:00am PT or shift to Wed 27th same window.

Agenda (attached): 30m demo + 15m exec Q&A / next steps\nNext steps: confirm agenda items and final attendee list

Thanks!
Vanessa Ortiz
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0162

Attachments: Greenline-demo-agenda-v1.pdf, mutual-action-plan.xlsx
Calendar: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=abc123

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ellis Moore <ellis.m@greenlinepay.com>, Priya Raman <priya.r@greenlinepay.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Jul 2027 10:27:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intro + demo — Tues 26th at 1pm PT?

Vanessa —

Sorry, I should have flagged this earlier. I'm scheduled to be onsite with another customer in SF on the 26th and my travel plans make the 1pm PT slot unreliable (I'll be in transit between 12:30–2:00). I can still present if we move to 11:00am PT on the 26th, or I can run it at 1pm PT on Wed 27th. If neither works for Greenline's execs, I can hand off to our SE, Mina Haddad, who is fully briefed and available at 1pm on the 26th.

Options: 
- Move demo to Tue 26, 11:00am PT (I present)
- Move demo to Wed 27, 1:00pm PT (I present)
- Keep Tue 26, 1:00pm PT and SE handoff to Mina Haddad (no content change)

Vanessa/Jordan — recommend quick check with Ellis on which is preferable. If we hand off to Mina I'll forward her notes and the agenda.

On travel: I fly out evening of 26th for onsite, so anything after 2pm is risky.

Thanks,
Dylan Brooks
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
dylan.brooks@redwood.ai | 415-555-0199

Quoted: "On Mon, Jul 19, 2027 at 10:03 AM Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai> wrote:"
> Perfect — we've penciled you in for Tue 26th, 1:00pm PT. I have Dylan (SE) joining to run the technical demo and Jordan (AM) will hop on for wrap-up to discuss next steps.

From: Ellis Moore <ellis.m@greenlinepay.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Priya Raman <priya.r@greenlinepay.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Jul 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intro + demo — Tues 26th at 1pm PT?

Thanks for the heads up, team. Exec availability is tight — the VP can only join at 1pm on the 26th. We'd prefer to keep the 1pm PT slot if possible.

Given that, we're happy to have Mina present in Dylan's stead if she's comfortable running the technical portion; we want the same content (latency/cost, retention, example flow) and 15 minutes at the end for exec Q&A. Vanessa — if Mina can present, please send a brief intro and Mina's prep notes to our team so we can prioritize the Q&A items.

I appreciate the quick alternatives — let's lock in Tue 26th, 1:00pm PT with Mina as the presenter unless I hear otherwise by end of day.

Ellis

-----
Mina Haddad will be copied on the calendar invite and receive the agenda + MAP.

Attachments: none

I can still present if we move to 11:00am PT on the 26th, or I can run it at 1pm PT on Wed 27th. If neither works for Greenline's execs, I can hand off to our SE, Mina Haddad, who is fully briefed and available at 1pm on the 26th.

Options: 
- Move demo to Tue 26, 11:00am PT (I present)
- Move demo to Wed 27, 1:00pm PT (I present)
- Keep Tue 26, 1:00pm PT and SE handoff to Mina Haddad (no content change)

Vanessa/Jordan — recommend quick check with Ellis on which is preferable. If we hand off to Mina I'll forward her notes and the agenda.

On travel: I fly out evening of 26th for onsite, so anything after 2pm is risky.

Thanks,
Dylan Brooks
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
dylan.brooks@redwood.ai | 415-555-0199

Quoted: \"On Mon, Jul 19, 2027 at 10:03 AM Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai> wrote:\"
> Perfect — we've penciled you in for Tue 26th, 1:00pm PT. I have Dylan (SE) joining to run the technical demo and Jordan (AM) will hop on for wrap-up to discuss next steps.


From: Ellis Moore <ellis.m@greenlinepay.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Priya Raman <priya.r@greenlinepay.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Jul 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intro + demo — Tues 26th at 1pm PT?

Thanks for the heads up, team. Exec availability is tight — the VP can only join at 1pm on the 26th. We'd prefer to keep the 1pm PT slot if possible.

Given that, we're happy to have Mina present in Dylan's stead if she's comfortable running the technical portion; we want the same content (latency/cost, retention, example flow) and 15 minutes at the end for exec Q&A. Vanessa — if Mina can present, please send a brief intro and Mina's prep notes to our team so we can prioritize the Q&A items.

I appreciate the quick alternatives — let's lock in Tue 26th, 1:00pm PT with Mina as the presenter unless I hear otherwise by end of day.

Ellis

-----
Mina Haddad will be copied on the calendar invite and receive the agenda + MAP.

Subject: Onboarding docs & PO instructions for Cephem Analytics

- Aisha

To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Claire Hughes <claire.hughes@cephem.io>
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2027 11:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding docs & PO instructions for Cephem Analytics

Thanks Aisha — happy to own the DPA + evidence. Summary / checklist below (cc Claire so procurement sees the plan):

1) DPA: We have the standard Redwood DPA v3. I'll prepare a DocuSign envelope and share the link to Claire. The DPA requires an authorized sig from Cephem; Redwood executes via our legal ops contact.
   - Attachment: redwood-DPA-v3.pdf (pdf, internal redlined copy)
   - DocuSign: https://docusign.com/tx?envelopeId=5F3A-CEPH-2027

2) SOC 2: here's a secure read-only link to our latest SOC 2 report (2027). Access token embedded; please do not re-host.
   - SOC2 report: https://drive.redwood.com/reports/soc2/2027/redwood-soc2-report-2027.pdf

3) Subprocessor inventory: we maintain a CSV export of our subprocessor list; I'll attach a snapshot to the DocuSign envelope and provide a direct download if you need the CSV.
   - Subprocessor snapshot: (will include in DocuSign)

4) PO & invoicing:
   - Who creates PO: Redwood accepts POs generated by the customer. Jordan (cc'd) will provide the billing email and the exact vendor legal name we expect on the PO so it matches our finance records.
   - Required fields: vendor legal name (Redwood Inference, Inc.), Redwood tax id: 12-3456789, billing contact: invoices@redwood.com.
   - Invoice cadence: Net 30 by default; if Cephem needs alternate terms, we'll escalate to finance.

Jordan — action for you: once Cephem confirms, please create PO record in Coupa (or our finance intake) and confirm PO number to Claire. If you want me to pre-authorize the onboarding (so we can provision the pilot before PO clears), say so and I can approve as Sales Ops for a one-off.

Claire — if that plan works, I'll send the DocuSign DPA now and include the SOC2 link and subprocessor snapshot. If Cephem prefers to receive artifacts by SFTP/secure share instead, let me know.

Ben Carter
Head of Customer Success (Sales Ops)
ben.carter@redwood.com

---
From: Claire Hughes <claire.hughes@cephem.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Jul 2027 15:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding docs & PO instructions for Cephem Analytics

We will:
- Sign the DPA via the DocuSign link today (procurement confirmed an authorized signer is available).
- Review SOC2 via the secure link.
- Expect subprocessor snapshot in the envelope.

Regarding the PO: Cephem prefers to issue the PO this week. Jordan, please send the exact vendor name and billing contact; we'll create the PO and route it through our AP system. If Redwood can provision pilot access ahead of PO (sales-approved hold), that would accelerate timelines — we'll accept a conditional provisioning note.

I've attached the signed vendor profile and the internal PO routing contact for Cephem.

Attachment: signed_vendor_profile_cephem.pdf (pdf)
PO contact: Laura Kim <laura.kim@cephem.io>
Billing email (Cephem): ap@cephem.io

We'll complete the DocuSign by EOD and queue the PO. Thanks for the fast turnaround.

—Claire
Claire Hughes
Finance & Procurement Lead
Cephem Analytics
procurement@cephem.io

>

source.subject: Micro-bundle swap & surge-credit — renewal coordination
source.messages_1:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena.torres@novumhealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Micro-bundle swap & surge-credit — renewal coordination

Hi Elena —

Thanks again for the discussion this morning. Following up with a concise proposal for the renewal + mid-contract expansion you asked about. High level: Novum wants to add a small burstable seat pool for summer traffic (micro-bundle of 50K token-units) and prefers to co-term the new capacity with your annual renewal on 2028-02-01. Two asks from your side: 1) confirmation the micro-bundle can be applied as a true-up credit toward the 2028 commit; 2) a short-term surge credit if usage spikes >2x in a given week.

Attached: 1) a one-page proposal (20270630-novum-microbundle-proposal.pdf) with pricing for the micro-bundle and surge-credit thresholds; 2) a marked SOW showing where the new units would be instrumented (novum-SOW-redlines.docx).

Quick summary of options (numbers are example rates, final pricing subject to signature):
- Option A (Flexible micro-bundle): 50K token-units, billed monthly, applied as credit toward next-term consumption; 8% discount on unit price vs pay-as-you-go.
- Option B (Reserved micro-bundle + surge cushion): 50K base + up to 25K weekly surge buffer billed at a blended rate; 12% discount if committed for 6 months.

We can co-term immediately (apply credits pro-rata to sync with 2028-02-01) or keep the micro-bundle as a standalone short-term add-on that rolls into the renewal. Jordan and Laura are looped to confirm invoice & billing handling and the operational rollout.

Next steps I suggest:
- Elena: confirm preferred option (A or B) and whether you need a PO before 2027-07-10.
- Jordan: if PO required, can you confirm invoicing cadence and whether Novum can use a single amended invoice for the micro-bundle + renewal credit?
- Laura: any support requirements we should schedule with Customer Success for onboarding the burst pool?

If useful I can put together a one-page mutual action plan with milestones (PO, activation, monitoring runbook).

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Exec | Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0124

Attachments: 20270630-novum-microbundle-proposal.pdf (PDF, 1pg), novum-SOW-redlines.docx (Word, 2pg)
source.messages_2:
From: Elena Torres <elena.torres@novumhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Markus Weber <m.weber@cloudfin.io>
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2027 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Micro-bundle swap & surge-credit — renewal coordination

On Wed, Jun 30, 2027 at 9:12 AM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Elena —
> 
> Thanks again for the discussion this morning...

Ben — thanks for the clear write-up and the attachments. A couple quick clarifying questions before we pick A vs B:

1) Co-term behavior: If we co-term now, do the 50K units reduce the target for the 2028 minimum commit (i.e., are they treated as pre-paid toward the next term) or do they sit as an adjustment credit applied after the renewal? We want the economics to reflect true capacity rather than a temporary credit line.

2) Surge credit math: for Option B, the 25K weekly surge is helpful — how is the surge credit applied if we exceed the buffer (is there an overage rate, or do you auto-bill at standard pay-as-you-go)? Is there a weekly cap on billable overages?

3) Timing & approvals: Markus and I will need an itemized cost line for procurement. If you can provide a two-line invoice example (micro-bundle fee plus any refundable/one-time activation), procurement can issue a PO quickly.

We prefer co-term if it doesn't artificially reduce our committed baseline; our goal is to have the micro-bundle contribute meaningfully to the 2028 commit. Otherwise we'll take it as a standalone add-on.

Thanks — Elena
Elena Torres
Head of Data Platform | Novum Health
elena.torres@novumhealth.com | +1 212-555-0199
source.messages_3:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena.torres@novumhealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 01 Jul 2027 08:47:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Micro-bundle swap & surge-credit — renewal coordination

Elena — good questions. Answering inline below and proposing concrete language we can include in the amendment/PO.

1) Co-term behavior (recommended approach): We propose the micro-bundle be treated as an advance consumption credit that counts toward the 2028 committed baseline (i.e., it reduces the remaining committed delta). In plain terms: if Novum commits to X token-units for 2028 and activates this 50K micro-bundle before co-term, that 50K will count toward the X when the contract renews. We will show this as a credit line on the renewal worksheet so your procurement can see the effect.

2) Surge credit math: For Option B we handle surge as a buffer that is billed up to the 25K weekly threshold at the blended buffer rate. Any usage above that weekly buffer will be billed at standard pay-as-you-go rates (no surprise uplift). We can cap total overage billable at 150% of the weekly buffer to avoid runaway costs; beyond that we will pause or flag usage and coordinate.

3) Invoice example & procurement lines: Jordan — can you draft a two-line invoice example?

Suggested amendment wording (for legal/procurement):
- "Micro-bundle Advance Credit": 50,000 token-units applied as credit toward 2028 committed units if renewal executed by 2028-02-01. Unused micro-bundle units at co-term will convert to credit dollars at the agreed unit rate.
- "Surge Buffer": up to 25,000 token-units/week billed at blended buffer rate; usage above buffer billed at standard PAYG rate; total weekly overage billable capped at 150% of buffer unless mutually agreed.

If that language looks good I can move to a simple amendment. For procurement I attached a one-page cost table in the proposal; I'll paste a minimal invoice example here as well:

Invoice sample (illustrative):
Line 1: Micro-bundle Advance Credit — 50,000 units — $8,500 (one-time prepaid credit)
Line 2: Surge Buffer (weekly, optional) — 25,000 units/week — $1,250/month (blended)

Elena — if you confirm Option B and indicate PO timing (and whether Novum wants the micro-bundle pre-paid or monthly-billed), we can fast-track an amendment for signature this week.

Laura — once PO path is confirmed, please advise on activation lead times and whether a brief onboarding call (30 min) is required.

Thanks,
Ben
source.messages_4:
From: Elena Torres <elena.torres@novumhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Markus Weber <m.weber@cloudfin.io>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 02 Jul 2027 15:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Micro-bundle swap & surge-credit — renewal coordination

Thanks for the thorough answers, Ben. We talked internally and Novum will proceed with Option B (Reserved micro-bundle + surge cushion) with the following constraints:

- Micro-bundle treated as advance credit toward 2028 commit (we want line-item visibility in the renewal worksheet).
- Surge buffer accepted per your terms; please include the 150% weekly overage cap in the doc.
- Procurement requires the two-line invoice example (exact line amounts will map to the PO). We'll issue PO by 2027-07-09 if you provide the invoice sample and the amendment draft.

Jordan — Markus and I will need the amended invoice format (two lines) and a short billing note confirming whether the pre-pay option reduces the invoice timeline (we prefer to pre-pay if it shortens activation).

Laura — please propose two 30-min onboarding slots next week for engineering handoff. We'll want metric hooks (token usage dashboard, alerts at 80/100/120% of buffer).

Ben — please send the amendment + invoice sample and I'll route it to procurement. Happy to sign and get the micro-bundle turned on quickly.

Best,
Elena

--
Elena Torres
Head of Data Platform | Novum Health
elena.torres@novumhealth.com
source.note: This thread contains internal participants limited to the directories in sources/gmail/ and external participants at Novum Health and CloudFin. Attachments are referenced as stubs per dataset rules.

title: Micro-bundle swap & surge-credit — renewal coordination
